title: Understanding Domain Extensions with Special Registration Requirements
content:
Choosing a domain name is exciting, but some domain extensions come with
special registration requirements
that can feel confusing if you’re not expecting them.
These requirements aren’t there to block you. They exist to
protect trust, security, and the intended purpose
of certain domain spaces.
Understanding this upfront helps you register the
right
domain faster, avoid delays, and make confident decisions.
This guide will help you understand
why some domain extensions have restrictions
, learn the
three main types
of restricted domain extensions, know
what to expect before you register
, avoid common delays or failed registrations, and feel confident choosing a domain that fits your needs.
Table of Contents
What Are Domain Extensions with Special Requirements?
1. Sponsored and Highly Restricted TLDs (sTLDs / gTLDs)
2. Country Code TLDs (ccTLDs) with Local Presence Requirements
3. Restricted or Purpose-Based Generic TLDs (gTLDs)
Other Registry-Specific Considerations
Technical and Policy-Based Rules
What This Means for You as a 1-grid Customer
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Are Domain Extensions with Special Requirements?
Some
Top-Level Domains (TLDs)
are governed by registries that apply extra rules around
who can register them
and
how they’re used
.
These rules help ensure:
Authentic representation (e.g. government, education, NGOs)
Regional or national integrity
Professional credibility
Improved security and trust online
At a high level, these TLDs fall into
three categories
.
1. Sponsored and Highly Restricted TLDs (sTLDs / gTLDs)
Sponsored TLDs are managed by official organisations and are
only available to eligible entities
. Registration requires proof before approval.
Common Characteristics
Strict eligibility checks
Manual verification
Not available to the general public
Designed to protect institutional trust
Examples
TLD
Who Can Register
.gov
United States government entities
.archi
Qualified, licensed architects and organisations recognised by the International Union of Architects (
UIA
)
.edu
Accredited post-secondary educational institutions
.mil
United States military
.aero
Members of the air transport industry
.coop
Verified cooperative associations
.jobs
Organisations focused on HR or employment
.museum
Accredited museums
.int
International treaty-based organisations
IMPORTANT:
If you’re not part of the eligible group, these domains won’t be approved even if payment is made.
2. Country Code TLDs (ccTLDs) with Local Presence Requirements
Country-code TLDs represent specific countries or regions. Some require a
local connection
to ensure domains are used appropriately.
Common Requirements
Citizenship or residency
Registered business presence
Local administrative contact
Examples
TLD
Requirement
.ca
Canadian citizen, resident, or organisation
.au
Australian citizen, permanent resident, registered company, or entity to hold a license
.de
Local administrative contact in Germany
.fr
Individual or business based in the EU
.ie
Irish resident, citizen, or company trading in Ireland
.eu
EU residency or registered organisation
.it
EU-based individuals or businesses
.us
US citizen, resident, or eligible organisation
.br
Brazilian ID or business registration
.ch
Swiss local presence
.sg
Registry-specific local presence rules
.es
Registry-specific local presence rules
IMPORTANT:
These requirements help maintain national trust and prevent misuse.
3. Restricted or Purpose-Based Generic TLDs (gTLDs)
Some generic extensions are intended for
specific purposes
or industries. While more flexible than sTLDs, they may still require validation or technical compliance.
Common Examples
TLD
Special Requirement
.pro
Verified professional credentials
.ngo / .ong
Verified non-profit status
.biz
Intended for business use
.name
Intended for personal use
.app / .dev
HTTPS & active SSL required
IMPORTANT:
These rules exist to protect users and improve security across the web.
Other Registry-Specific Considerations
Technical and Policy-Based Rules
Minimum character lengths
(some TLDs require 3+ characters)
Naming restrictions
(reserved words or patterns)
Security requirements
(mandatory SSL for some TLDs)
Verification timelines
(manual approvals can take longer)
Special Note on .ai
Although widely used by tech companies,
.ai
is technically a country-code TLD (Anguilla) and remains subject to registry policies.
What This Means for You as a 1-grid Customer
At 1-grid, our goal is to make domain registration
clear, supported, and transparent
.
We will always:
Clearly indicate if a domain has
special requirements
Let you know
before registration
what’s needed
Guide you through verification if required
Help you choose
alternatives
if a domain isn’t eligible
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Explaining eligibility requirements
Identifying suitable alternative TLDs
Guidance on registry rules
Outside Our Scope
What We Are Unable to Assist With
Overriding registry eligibility rules
Bypassing verification requirements
Guaranteed approval for restricted domains
FAQs
Q. Why was my domain registration delayed?
Some TLDs require manual registry verification, which can take longer than standard registrations.
Q. Can I register a restricted TLD if I don’t meet the requirements?
No, registry rules are enforced globally and cannot be bypassed.
Q. Do all country domains have restrictions?
No, some ccTLDs are open globally, while others require local presence.
Q. Will 1-grid tell me if a domain has special rules?
Yes, we’ll always flag this upfront and guide you before proceeding.
Q. Can these rules change?
Yes, domain registry policies can evolve, which is why we keep our guidance up to date.
Additional Resources
Domains FAQs
A Guide to Registering Specialised Domain Extensions (TLDs)
Domain Extensions (TLDs) Available at 1-grid
Registering Your Domain with 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding domain extensions with special registration requirements doesn’t have to feel confusing with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Domain Extensions (TLDs) Available at 1-grid
content:
Choosing the right domain extension is an important step in shaping your online presence, credibility, and reach, which is why it is important to us to make the right domain extensions available to you.
At 1-grid, we offer a wide range of
trusted, modern, and industry-specific domain extensions
from familiar options like .com and .co.za to creative, professional, and global alternatives.
This guide will show you
all domain extensions currently available at 1-grid
, explain the
purpose and best use cases
for each category, highlight
special registration or residency requirements
, where applicable, and help you confidently choose a domain that fits your brand, audience, and goals.
Table of Contents
What Is a Domain Extension (TLD)?
Domain Extensions Available at 1-grid
Popular and Everyday Domains
South African and Local Presence Domains
International Country Domains (ccTLDs)
Business and Professional Domains
Industry, Niche and Brand-Focused Domains
Creative, Lifestyle and Modern Domains
Special Registration Notes (Quick Reference)
How to Choose the Right Domain Extension
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Domain Extension (TLD)?
A
domain extension
, also known as a
Top-Level Domain (TLD)
, is the part of a domain name that comes after the dot (for example, .com, .co.za, or .tech).
Different TLDs serve different purposes, where some indicate
location
, and others
industry
,
organisation type
, or
brand personality
.
Domain Extensions Available at 1-grid
If a domain has
special requirements
, we’ll guide you before
registration
, so there are no surprises.
Popular and Everyday Domains
Best for broad use, familiarity, and instant trust:
.com

.org
.biz
.info
.name
IMPORTANT:
These are open for registration with no special requirements.
South African and Local Presence Domains
Ideal for businesses targeting South African customers:
.co.za
.org.za
.net.za
.web.za
.joburg
.capetown
.durban
TIP:
They build local trust, improve regional relevance, and signal a strong South African presence.
International Country Domains (ccTLDs)
Great for regional reach or international branding:
.uk
.co.uk
.org.uk
.eu
.us
.de
.es
.ie
.nl
.gr

.cc

.tv

Some country domains require:
Local residency or citizenship
A local administrative contact
Registry verification
If required, 1-grid will guide you before
registration
if you’re considering one of these that has special registration requirements.
Business and Professional Domains
Designed for credibility and clarity
:
.ltd

Perfect for:
Companies, consultants, and professional services.
Industry, Niche and Brand-Focused Domains
Help your domain instantly explain what you do
:

.cloud
.shop

Creative, Lifestyle and Modern Domains
For bold brands, creators, and modern projects

Some extensions may require
additional verification

– EU residency or registered organisation required

– Local admin contact may be required

– Registry-specific validation policies
We’ll always guide you through any
requirements
before proceeding, but you can explore our guide here to see what requirements are needed for each one that we offer.
How to Choose the Right Domain Extension
Ask yourself:
Who is
my audience

Do I want

or

Should my domain reflect my

Then, when you’re ready, registering your domain with 1-grid is made as easy as 1-2-3 with our empowering guides for you to explore, and bring your brand to life online.

title: A Guide to Registering Specialised Domain Extensions (TLDs)
content:
Some domain extensions have simple registration rules (like .com), while others have
additional requirements
or
eligibility checks
as they are classified as specialised domain extensions (TLDs). Without clarity, customers may see an extension available but then be surprised by a failed registration or additional verification steps.
We understand the importance of being able to make informative decisions about the future of your digital brand. That’s why we set expectations upfront to reduce confusion and accidental failed registrations so that we can help you choose the best domains for your brand.
This guide will help you understand
how to register specialised top-level domains (TLDs)
like
.de, .uk
(and many more), including creative or industry-focused extensions such as .
earth, .blog, .ltd
, and
.vip
, as well as other country-specific and restricted options. You’ll learn the differences between TLD types, any eligibility or verification requirements, and how to proceed confidently when registering them with 1-grid.
Table of Contents
What is a Specialised TLD?
Types of Specialised TLDs
1. Brandable & Creative Extensions
2. Country-Code TLDs (ccTLDs)
3. Restricted and Sponsored TLDs (sTLDs)
How Registration Works (All TLDs)
Important Policies to Know (Before You Register)
Naming Rules
Privacy Protection
Renewals and Ownership
Acceptable Use
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What is a Specialised TLD?
A
Top-Level Domain (TLD)
is the extension at the end of a domain name (such as .com or .co.za).
Specialised TLDs
are domains that come with
specific registration rules
. These typically include:
Country-Code TLDs (ccTLDs),
which are linked to a specific country
Restricted or Sponsored TLDs (sTLDs)
, which are limited to verified industries or institutions
Choosing the right TLD builds credibility, but choosing the wrong one can delay your launch or block registration entirely.
Types of Specialised TLDs
1. Brandable & Creative Extensions
These include newer or less traditional domain endings that are popular for branding, niche identity, or industry visibility. Examples of customers who can search and register at 1-grid include:
.earth
is perfect for environmental, lifestyle, or sustainability brands
.blog
is ideal for content creators, personal blogs, and editorial sites
.ltd
is great for private companies and enterprises
.vip
is used to highlight premium or exclusive brands
And many more like
.store
,
.online
,
.site
,
.tech
, etc.
These are still generally open for registration without strict eligibility requirements, meaning
anyone can register them if available
.
Benefit:
Brandable TLDs help your site stand out in search and be more memorable to customers.
2. Country-Code TLDs (ccTLDs)
These domains are tied to a specific country and often require a
local presence or “nexus.”
Common ccTLD Examples & Requirements
TLD
Who Can Register
Key Requirement
.co.za
Individuals & businesses
Local contact details may be required
.uk
Eligible individuals or entities
UK address or connection
.ca
Canadians only
Citizenship, residency, or corporation
.eu
EU/EEA residents or organisations
Proof of EU presence
.us
US citizens or organisations
WHOIS privacy not allowed
.de
Anyone
Admin contact with a German address
TIP:
These TLDs often strengthen regional trust and improve local search visibility when your audience is geographic.
IMPORTANT: Availability does not equal eligibility.
A domain may appear available but still fail registration if requirements aren’t met.
3. Restricted and Sponsored TLDs (sTLDs)
These domains are limited to
verified professions, industries, or institutions
.
Common sTLD Examples
TLD
Who Can Register
Verification Required
.gov
Government bodies
Official authorisation
.edu
Accredited institutions
Accreditation validation
.mil
Military
Restricted
.bank
Financial institutions
Compliance & identity checks
.abogado
Lawyers
Professional credentials
.med
Medical professionals
Licensing verification
A Trust Signal
These TLDs increase trust and credibility because they signify legitimacy in a specialised sector, but they require verification before approval.
How Registration Works (All TLDs)
Most brandable extensions like .earth, .blog, .ltd, and .vip follow the normal
registration
process without extra verification, alongside most gTLDs (Generic TLDs).
Check availability
in the
1-grid Domain Search
.
Use our
AI Domain Name Generator
for unique suggestions
Provide your contact details
during
registration
.
Pay the registration fee
for your chosen term (typically 1 year(s)).
Complete any eligibility verification
if required (for ccTLDs or restricted TLDs).
Once approved, your domain becomes active and ready to use.
Important Policies to Know (Before You Register)
Naming Rules
1
to
64
characters
Letters
,
numbers
, and
hyphens only
Cannot start or end with a hyphen
Privacy Protection
Not all TLDs allow
WHOIS privacy
(e.g. .us)
Renewals and Ownership
Domains are leased (1 to 10 years)
Missed
renewals
may lead to
redemption fees
Acceptable Use
Domains used for spam or illegal activity may be revoked
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Basic assistance with
checking eligibility requirements
Guide you through
registration steps
Explain
why a registration may fail
Advise on
alternative TLD options
Outside Our Scope
What We Are Unable to Assist With
Overriding registry eligibility rules
Bypassing verification requirements
Registering restricted domains without valid documentation, where these
TLDs are available
for registering with us
FAQs
Q: Why did my domain registration fail even though it was available?
Availability does not guarantee eligibility as some TLDs require residency, credentials, or validation.
Q: Can I register a ccTLD if I don’t live in that country?
It depends on the TLD, as some allow foreign registrants with local contacts; others do not.
Q: How long does verification take?
Most complete within 24 to 72 hours, but restricted TLDs may take longer.
Q: Can I switch TLDs if I don’t qualify?
Yes, we can help suggest suitable alternatives.
Additional Resources
Registering Your Domain with 1-grid
Understanding Domain Extensions with Special Registration Requirements
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Smart Digital Branding with 1-grid’s AI Domain Name Generator
Smart Strategies for Affordable Premium Domain Alternatives
Understanding Premium Domains
Choosing a Great Domain Name
Why Renewal Invoices Are Sent Early
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Registering a specialised domain extension (TLD) doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: What is a Registry, Registrar and Registrant?
content:
If you’ve ever wondered “Why can’t this be changed?” or “Who actually owns my domain?”, you’re not alone. Understanding these roles removes confusion and helps you resolve issues faster and with confidence.
This guide will clearly explain the three key roles involved in domain registration: the registry, registrar, and registrant, so you know exactly who manages your domain, who you work with, and what you control.
Table of Contents
At a Glance
What is a Domain Registry?
What is a Domain Registrar?
What is a Domain Registrant?
How These Roles Work Together
Who Oversees the Domain System? (ICANN)
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
At a Glance
Know who to contact, what can be changed, and where responsibility sits.
Every registered domain involves
three distinct roles
:
Role
What They Do
Registry
Manages the domain extension
Registrar
Sells and manages the domain
Registrant
Owns the right to use the domain
What is a Domain Registry?
The registry sets the rules; they don’t sell domains to the public, such as
ZARC
.
A
registry
is the organisation responsible for
managing a specific top-level domain (TLD)
, such as .com, .org, or .co.za.
What a Registry Does
Maintains the
master database
of all domains under that extension
Sets
policies and rules
for registrations, renewals, and transfers
Publishes DNS zone files for the TLD
Works
only with accredited registrars
IMPORTANT:
Customers
cannot
register domains directly with a registry.
What is a Domain Registrar?
Your registrar is your main point of contact for domain management.
A
registrar
is an
ICANN-accredited provider
authorised to sell domain names to the public and manage them on your behalf.
What a Registrar Does
Registers domains with the registry
Handles renewals, transfers, and ownership updates
Provides access to DNS and contact settings
Offers customer support and guidance
At 1-grid
, we act as your
registrar
, managing communication with registries, so you don’t have to.
What is a Domain Registrant?
The registrant is the legal holder of the domain’s usage rights.
A
registrant
is the individual or business that
registers and controls the domain
for the duration of the registration period (usually 1 to 10 years).
Registrant Responsibilities
Ensure contact details are accurate
Renew the domain before expiry
Authorise transfers or ownership changes
IMPORTANT:
Registering a domain gives you
usage rights
, not permanent ownership; it must be renewed to stay active.
How These Roles Work Together
When you register a domain:
You become the
registrant
1-grid acts as the
registrar
The
registry
records the domain under its TLD
Changes you request are:
Submitted to your
registrar
Validated against
registry rules
Updated in the
registry database
Who Oversees the Domain System? (ICANN)
ICANN
ensures the internet stays organised and fair.
The
Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN)
oversees the global domain name system.
ICANN’s Role
Accredits registrars
Coordinates registries
Ensures domain names and IP addresses remain unique
Enforces global DNS policies
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Domain
registration
and
renewals
DNS configuration
guidance
Contact detail updates
Transfers
and registrar changes
Outside Our Scope
What We Are Unable to Assist With
Registry-level policy decisions
ICANN regulations
Trademark or legal disputes
FAQs
Q. Who owns my domain name?
You do, as the registrant, for the duration of the registration.
Q. Can I change registrars?
Yes, domains can be transferred between registrars if eligibility rules are met.
Q. Why are some changes restricted?
Registries enforce mandatory policies to protect domain integrity.
Q. Is a domain the same as hosting?
No, a domain is your address; hosting is where your website lives.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding what a Registry, Registrar and Registrant is doesn’t have to feel confusing with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: A Guide to Company Registration with 1-grid (Step-by-Step)
content:
Unlock the potential of your business idea with our
Company Registration
service, including domain, web hosting, professional email, and website builder.
Whether you’re registering your first company or formalising an existing business, we’re here to make the process simple, compliant, and stress-free.
This guide will walk you through how to register your company with 1-grid, what information you’ll need, what happens behind the scenes with
CIPC
and SARS, and what to expect after registration so you can start your business with clarity and confidence.
Table of Contents
Why Register Your Company with 1-grid?
How Company Registration Works
1: Complete the Online Application
2: Secure Payment
3: Director Verification
4: We Register Your Company
5: Receive Your Documents and Access to Your 6-Month Free Services
What Information Do We Need from You?
What You’ll Receive After Registration
Official Company Documents
Automatic SARS Registration
Quick Turnaround Time
Understanding the CIPC and SARS Process
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Why Register Your Company with 1-grid?
You focus on building your business while we handle the paperwork, taking care of all CIPC processing, and filing to ensure your company registration is compliant.
Registering through 1-grid
means:
No direct CIPC admin or guesswork
A guided, step-by-step process
Automatic income tax registration with SARS
You get
free tools and products
to help you get online and operational faster:
AI Domain Name Generator
Tool (
Free
)
Use our
AI Domain Name Generator
to find the perfect name for your business before registering.
Domain Name Registration (
Free for 1 Year
)
You get to
choose the perfect web address
that represents your new business online with one year of free registration.
Web Hosting with our
AI-powered Website Builder
(
Free for 6 Months
)
Create the perfect website to represent your business online with our simple but powerful website builder.
Email Hosting with our
Professional Email
(
Free for 6 Months
)
Enhance business communications and establish trust with customers using our professional email account.
We manage the full CIPC submission on your behalf and keep you informed at every stage.
How Company Registration Works
1: Complete the Online Application
You need to fill in a short online form with your business and
director
details.
IMPORTANT:
Provide
at least 3 company name options,
as this allows CIPC to consider alternatives, should your desired one not be available. This means it is important to do your research and try to match the 3 variations as close as possible to your business.
TIP:
Having your
certified ID document
ready upfront helps avoid delays later.
2: Secure Payment
Pay once-off using your preferred secure payment method.
3: Director Verification
Then, each
director
receives an email requesting:
A
certified
copy of their South African ID or passport
IMPORTANT
: CIPC legally requires identity verification before registration can proceed.
4: We Register Your Company
Once all details are submitted:
We reserve your company name with CIPC
File your incorporation documents
Then, complete the SARS income tax registration
5: Receive Your Documents and Access to Your 6-Month Free Services
Once finalised, you’ll receive your official company documents via email and gain access to your included Professional Email and Website Builder products that are
free for the first 6 months
. You’ll have the option to continue hosting and/or email on a paid, invoiced basis, meaning no automatic charges without notice.
IMPORTANT:
In the 7
th
month, your
Professional Email
and
Website Builder
products will resume normal
invoicing
and will become
payable
. Similarly, your free domain becomes renewable annually, and you will be
invoiced
for this to make your payment in the next
renewal cycle
.
What Information Do We Need from You?
Full name, email address, and mobile number
Certified
copies of ID(s)
Up to
three company name options
Preferred domain name
Financial year-end (February is recommended, but not mandatory, for alignment with SARS)
Authorised share capital (minimum 100; 10,000 recommended as it provides flexibility for future share allocations without needing amendments later)
Director details (full names, email addresses)
TIP:
However, if your company name is rejected, simply provide three new alternatives, and we’ll handle the resubmission.
What You’ll Receive After Registration
Official Company Documents
Registration Certificate (COR14.3)
Notice of Incorporation
Memorandum of Incorporation (COR15.1A)
Automatic SARS Registration
Income tax number generated by SARS
Once issued, we share this with you
IMPORTANT:
VAT registration
is
not
included, so CIPC services are
VAT-exempt
, and VAT registration must be done separately through SARS if required.
Quick Turnaround Time
Once all required documents are submitted, registrations are typically completed within
7 to 14 calendar days
.
Understanding the CIPC and SARS Process
CIPC Registration
We submit your company details to CIPC on your behalf.
Automatic SARS Registration
CIPC shares your company details with SARS.
Income Tax Number Issued
SARS generates your company’s income tax number.
eFiling Registration
You’ll need to register on
SARS eFiling
separately to submit tax returns.
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Company registration submissions
CIPC filings and document delivery
Name reservations and resubmissions
Guidance on next steps after registration
Outside Our Scope
What We Are Unable to Assist With
Providing CIPC login credentials (managed securely by 1-grid)
Submitting VAT registrations on your behalf
Amending company details without a formal request
FAQs
Q. Why was my company name rejected?
If CIPC rejects names that are too similar to existing companies or restricted terms, then simply submit three new options, and we’ll proceed.
Q. Do I get CIPC login details?
No, registrations are processed through 1-grid’s secure CIPC account. Any amendments must be requested via our Support Team.
Q. When will I receive my tax number?
We share it with you once SARS issues this, which is usually alongside your registration documents.
Q. Is VAT included?
No, CIPC services are VAT-exempt, and VAT registration must be done directly with SARS if applicable.
Additional Resources
More New Company Registration FAQ’s
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Completing your company registration through us is made simple with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: What is a Domain Name?
content:
If you’re starting a website, launching a business, or creating a professional email address, your domain name is your
online identity
. Understanding it upfront helps you make confident decisions and avoid costly mistakes later.
This guide will explain what a domain name is, how it works, and why it matters, using simple language, clear examples, and real-world context.
Table of Contents
What Is a Domain Name?
Why Domain Names Matter
Anatomy of a Domain Name
Domain Levels Explained
Top-Level Domains (TLDs)
Second-Level Domains (SLDs)
Subdomains (Optional)
How Do Domain Names Work?
Who Manages Domain Names?
ICANN, WHOIS and Ownership
How to Buy and Register a Domain Name
Using a Domain for Email and Websites
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Domain Name?
A domain name makes your website easy to find, remember, and trust.
A domain name is the
human-readable address
people type into a browser to visit a website like
1grid.co.za
.
Behind the scenes, computers use numeric
IP addresses
to find websites. A domain name simply translates those numbers into something
memorable and meaningful
for humans.
Why Domain Names Matter
A domain name:
Gives your website a unique identity
Builds credibility and professionalism
Makes your brand easier to find and remember
Enables branded email addresses (e.g.
[email protected]
)
IMPORTANT:
This is often the
first impression
of your business online, so
choosing the right name
matters.
Anatomy of a Domain Name
Let’s look at:
www.example.co.za
.za
→ Country-code top-level domain (South Africa)
.co
→ Second-level domain (commercial identifier)
example
→ Your unique name or brand
www
→ A common subdomain (optional)
TIP:
You don’t need “www” for a website to work, so it’s optional.
Domain Levels Explained
Top-Level Domains (TLDs)
These are the endings of domain names, such as:
.com
– global and widely recognised
.co.za
– South African businesses
.org
,
.net
,
.blog
,
.tech
– purpose or industry-based
SEO TIP:
Search engines use TLDs as relevance signals, so choose one that matches your audience.
Second-Level Domains (SLDs)
This is the
name you choose
, like
1grid
in
1grid.co.za
.
Best Practice:
Short and easy to spell
Easy to pronounce
Brand-aligned
Subdomains (Optional)
Subdomains
help organise content:
blog.example.com
mail.example.com
shop.example.com
They’re useful, but
not required
for beginners.
How Do Domain Names Work?
You don’t need to understand
DNS
to use it confidently.
When someone types your domain into a browser:
The browser asks the Domain Name System (DNS) for help
DNS translates the name into an IP address
The browser connects to the server
Your website loads
Think of DNS as the
Internet’s phonebook
.
Who Manages Domain Names?
Registry, Registrar, Registrant
Registry:
Manages a TLD (e.g. .com, .co.za)
Registrar:
Sells domain names (like 1-grid)
Registrant:
You (the domain owner)
Domains are registered annually and can be renewed indefinitely.
ICANN
,
WHOIS
and Ownership
When you register a domain:
You become the legal registrant for the registration period
Ownership details are recorded via
WHOIS
Domains can be
transferred
or
renewed
, but not shared
IMPORTANT:
Registering a domain
does
not
register a company
; it only secures your online name.
How to Buy and Register a Domain Name
Quick Start Process:
Choose a name
aligned to your brand
Check availability
Select a suitable TLD
Register the domain
Allow
DNS propagation
(up to 24 to 48 hours)
For full steps, explore our guide on
registering your domain with 1-grid
.
Using a Domain for Email and Websites
A domain allows you to:
Create professional email addresses
Connect hosting for a website
Build credibility from day one
IMPORTANT:
A domain alone does not include
email
or a
website
. This means that these would need to be purchased separately, or consider one of our
hosting
packages.
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Domain registration and renewal
Company registrations
DNS configuration guidance
Connecting domains to hosting and email
Outside Our Scope
What We Are Unable to Assist With
Control third-party domain providers
FAQs
Q. Is a domain name the same as hosting?
No, a domain is your address; hosting is where your website lives.
Q. Can I own a domain without a website?
Yes, many people reserve domains before building.
Q. Does my domain affect SEO?
Yes, as clarity, relevance, and TLD choice all matter.
Q. Can I change my domain later?
No, this would require you to register a new domain. Keep in mind that changing domains can affect branding and
SEO
if you have already established your online presence.
Additional Resources
AI Domain Name Generator (1-grid)
Tips for Choosing a Great Domain Name
What Are Premium Domain Names?
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding what a domain name is doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Setting Up Google Workspace Using Your 1-grid Domain
content:
Activating Google Workspace on your 1-grid domain is easy, but the most important step is
verifying your domain
and setting up your
DNS records
so Google knows you own it.
Whether you’re setting up your first business email or moving your email over to Google Workspace, this guide walks you through every step clearly and simply.
This guide will show you how to sign up for Google Workspace, verify your 1-grid domain, add DNS records correctly, set up your email routing, test your new Google Workspace mail, and manage additional settings for reliability and security.
Let’s get you set up smoothly and confidently.
Table of Contents
Signing Up for Google Workspace
Verifying Your Domain with Google Workspace
Accessing Your DNS Zone for Your Domain
Adding the TXT Record (Domain Verification)
Completing Domain Verification
Setting Up Your Email (MX Records)
Configuring Additional Settings (Optional but Recommended)
SPF Record
DKIM Authentication
DMARC Policy (Advanced)
Testing Your Setup
FAQs
Additional Resources
Signing Up for Google Workspace
Visit
https://workspace.google.com/
Click
Get Started
Follow the guided prompts to add your business details
Enter your
domain name registered at 1-grid
Create your Google Workspace admin username and password
After signing up, Google will prompt you to
verify your domain
.
Verifying Your Domain with Google Workspace
Google needs to confirm that you are the owner of your domain before email can work.
You will be asked to verify ownership using a
TXT record
added to your
DNS
zone.
What You’ll Need:
Access to your
1-grid Customer Zone
Access to your DNS management page (
cPanel
,
Plesk
, or
Website Panel
)
Accessing Your DNS Zone for Your Domain
Your DNS zone is where you manage records such as TXT, MX, and CNAME.
1-grid provides DNS management through:
cPanel
Plesk (Windows Hosting)
Website Panel (.NET Hosting)
Adding the TXT Record (Domain Verification)
Google will provide a TXT record similar to:
google-site-verification=abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890
Add it to your DNS:
Log in to the
1-grid Customer Zone
Open your hosting panel (
cPanel
,
Plesk
, or
WSP
)
Navigate to
DNS Zone Editor
Click
Add Record
Select
TXT
Enter:
Name/Host:
leave blank or use @ (varies per panel)
Value:
paste the full Google TXT verification string
TTL:
default is fine
Save the record
Important:
DNS changes require
propagation time
. Learn more:
Understanding DNS Propagation and Why It Takes So Long
Completing Domain Verification
Once the TXT record is saved:
Return to the
Google Workspace setup wizard
Click
Verify
Google will validate your TXT record. This can take a few minutes or longer if DNS is still propagating.
When successful, you will see a confirmation message.
Setting Up Your Email (MX Records)
After verifying your domain, you must configure
Google Workspace MX records
so that emails are delivered to Gmail instead of your old email provider.
Use these exact MX entries:
Priority
Server
1
ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM
5
ALT1.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM
5
ALT2.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM
10
ALT3.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM
10
ALT4.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM
Steps:
Go to your
DNS Zone Editor
Remove old
MX records
(such as 1-grid or cPanel mail entries)
Add each
Google MX record
Save
your changes
Google recommends allowing up to
48 hours
for MX
propagation
.
Configuring Additional Settings (Optional but Recommended)
SPF Record
Add or update
SPF
to include Google servers:
v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all
DKIM Authentication
Enable
DKIM
in your Google Admin Console for improved email trust.
DMARC Policy (Advanced)
Add a
DMARC
record for extra protection:
v=DMARC1; p=none;
rua=mailto:
[email protected]
This helps track spoofing attempts and improves deliverability.
Testing Your Setup
Once MX propagation completes:
Send an outgoing email from your new Workspace mailbox
Send a test email to your Workspace mailbox from another provider
Check your spam folder
Inspect
headers
(optional) to confirm Google routing
If you encounter issues, explore our guides:
Basic Troubleshooting Steps for Email
Resolving Email Error Messages When Sending to Gmail Accounts
Preventing Bounce Back or Spam Issues When Sending to Gmail
FAQs
Q. How long does domain verification take?
It may take a few minutes to a few hours, depending on DNS propagation.
Q. Do I need to keep the TXT record after verification?
Yes, Google recommends keeping it for future account ownership checks.
Q. Can I use both 1-grid email and Google Workspace at the same time?
No, only one MX routing option can be active.
Q. Do I need DNS access to complete the setup?
Yes, verification and email routing require DNS configuration.
Q. My email isn’t working after changing MX records — what should I do?
Check for:
Old MX records are still present
Typo errors
Missing SPF
DNS propagation delays
Q. Does 1-grid support Google Workspace setup?
We provide guidance, but full setup is outside our standard support scope. However, you can follow this guide or refer to Google’s documentation.
Q. Will my old emails be transferred to Google Workspace automatically?
No, email migration must be done separately using Google’s migration tools.
Additional Resources
Understanding DNS, Its Record Types and How It Works
DNS Records and How to Check These
Understanding DNS Propagation and Why It Takes So Long
How to Manage DNS Zone File in cPanel
Managing DNS Zones in Windows Plesk
How to Manage DNS Zone in Website Panel (.Net)
Basic Troubleshooting Steps for Email
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Setting up Google Workspace using your 1-grid domain doesn’t have to feel like a map with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Registering Your Domain with 1-grid
content:
At 1-grid, we understand how important your
domain name
is as it is your
digital address
on the internet – it is what customers type to find you, remember you, and trust you. Your domain becomes your
smart brand name
online, the foundation of your website and email, and a long-term digital asset you control, and we’re here to help you secure it online by registering your domain.
Registering your domain with 1-grid
gives you
ownership, credibility, and control
over your online identity while it’s securely managed in one place, with local support when you need it. Whether you’re registering your very first domain or securing the next step in your online journey, this guide is designed to give you clarity, peace of mind, and a strong foundation to build on.
This guide will walk you through how to register a domain with 1-grid, explain why your domain matters as a long-term digital asset, and help you make confident decisions that support your brand, visibility, and growth.
Table of Contents
Why Registering Your Domain Matters
Domain Extensions Available at 1-grid
Smart Digital Branding with 1-grid’s AI Domain Name Generator
Choosing a Great Domain Name for Your Online Brand
Understanding Premium Domains and Finding the Right Alternative
Smart Strategies for Affordable Premium Domain Alternatives
How to Register a Domain with 1-grid
What Happens After Registration?
Understanding Domain Renewal Invoices at 1-grid
Important Things to Know and Remember
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Why Registering Your Domain Matters
1. Credibility and Trust
A custom domain (like yourbusiness.co.za) shows customers you’re legitimate, professional, and established, especially compared to free subdomains.
Customers are more likely to trust and contact a business with its own domain and branded email address.
2. Brand Identity and Recognition
Your domain is often the
first impression
someone has of your brand. A clear, relevant name helps customers remember you and find you again.
Using the same domain across your website, email, and social media creates consistency and stronger brand recall.
3. Brand Protection and Security
Registering your domain early prevents:
Competitors or third parties from taking it
Cybersquatting
or misuse
Confusion caused by similar domain names
TIP:
Registering right away
protects your name before someone else does.
4. SEO and Online Visibility
Search engines consider domain relevance and trust. A well-chosen domain helps:
Improve search visibility
Drive direct traffic
Strengthen local
SEO
with regional extensions
5. Long-Term Control and Growth
Your domain stays with you, even if your website, hosting, or services change, as long as you wish to keep it and renew it every year.
Domain Extensions Available at 1-grid
1-grid supports a
wide range of domain extensions
, including but not limited to:
Geo-specific domains (e.g. .co.za, .africa, .capetown)
Global domains (e.g. .com, .co.uk, .de)
Industry/niche and business or brand-focused options like .blog, .earth, .ltd, and .vip
Choosing the right extension helps align your domain with your audience and purpose.
Smart Digital Branding with 1-grid’s AI Domain Name Generator
Choosing the right domain name doesn’t have to be overwhelming. Our
AI Domain Name Generator
helps you explore creative, relevant, and available domain ideas that align with your brand, industry, and goals.
If you’re looking for a strong domain name that improves brand recall, credibility, and search visibility, explore our full
Smart Digital Branding with 1-grid’s AI Domain Name Generator
guide to find the right suggestions that can save hours of guesswork and the perfect name for your brand.
Choosing a Great Domain Name for Your Online Brand
Your domain name is often the
first interaction
someone has with your brand. A clear, memorable, and relevant domain helps customers find you, trust you, and return to you.
Explore our
Choosing a Great Domain Name for Your Online Brand Matters
guide to find the right domain that supports marketing, SEO, email credibility, and long-term brand growth.
Understanding Premium Domains and Finding the Right Alternative
Some domains are classified as
premium
due to their length, popularity, or market demand. While these domains can be valuable, they’re not always necessary for success.
Our guide to
Understanding Premium Domains and Finding the Right Alternative
provides clarity on what makes a domain “premium” so explore it as it helps you make informed choices and avoid unnecessary costs while still building a strong brand presence.
Smart Strategies for Affordable Premium Domain Alternatives
Not all great domains need to come with a premium price tag. If your ideal domain is unavailable or listed as premium, there are strategic alternatives that still protect your brand and budget.
You can secure a professional domain without compromising trust or identity, so explore our
Smart Strategies for Affordable Premium Domain Alternatives
guide to help you with the right extension, wording, or structure for your brand.
How to Register a Domain with 1-grid
When you’re ready and have found the perfect domain name for your brand,
registering with 1-grid
is as easy as 1-2-3. Whether you’re just getting started, expanding beyond your current domain extension, or growing beyond your current brand.
New Customers
Visit the
1-grid Domain Registration
page
Search for your desired domain name
Select your preferred extension
Complete the checkout process
Your domain is registered once payment is confirmed
Existing 1-grid Customers
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
Navigate to
Domains
Search for and select your domain
Complete the registration process
Manage your domain from your dashboard
Via the 1-grid App
Download and log in to the
1-grid App
Navigate to
Secure a new domain
or
Order New Service
Search for and select your domain
Complete the registration process
Manage your domain from your dashboard
What Happens After Registration?
Once your domain is registered:
It is added to your
1-grid Customer Zone
You can connect it to
hosting
,
email
, or other services
Nameserver
and
DNS
settings become available for management, depending on the additional services purchased
Your domain remains active as long as it is
renewed
IMPORTANT:
Some domain changes may take time to
propagate
across the internet.
Understanding Domain Renewal Invoices at 1-grid
Domain renewals are billed in advance to protect your ownership and prevent accidental expiry. This proactive approach ensures your domain and everything connected to it stays secure.
An expired domain can disrupt your website, email, and brand trust. Advance renewal billing, as explained in our
Understanding Domain Renewal Invoices at 1-grid
guide, is designed to give you time, clarity, and control.
Important Things to Know and Remember
Domain registrations are billed annually
Domains must be renewed to remain active
Domain names are subject to availability
Ownership remains with you while the domain is active
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help You With
Guided domain registration
Helping you search and confirm domain availability
Explaining domain extensions and what they mean
Walking you through the steps to register in your Customer Zone
Basic account setup and consolidation
Ensuring your new domain is added to the correct 1-grid Customer Zone
Helping if you accidentally registered under a different profile
Domain activation and first steps
Confirming your domain is active after registration
Explaining how to link your domain to a website, email, or hosting (general pointers)
Billing and renewals
Clarifying why renewal invoices are sent 60 days ahead of expiry
Helping explain charges associated with domain registration or renewal
Security and DNS basics
Explaining what DNS is and where to find it
Directing you to resources on DNS changes or propagation basics
General domain info
Helping you understand:
Premium vs standard domains
Choosing domain alternatives
What various domain extensions represent
We’ll guide you through the next steps securely and as smoothly as possible.
Outside Our Scope
What We Are Unable to Assist With
Domain transfer decisions or third-party transfers,
unless explicitly supported
Choosing a specific domain name for you
(we provide guidance but not selection)
Guaranteeing domain availability
Bypassing domain policies
such as ICANN rules or registry restrictions
Restoring domains expired outside 1-grid
domain redemption periods without following the redemption/re-registering process
Providing legal, trademark, or business naming advice
Managing domains not registered or hosted through 1-grid
FAQs
Q. How long does a domain registration last?
Domain registrations typically last for one year, after which you can renew them. You maintain ownership as long as you renew on time.
Q. Can someone else take my domain if I don’t register it quickly?
Yes, domain names are unique. This means that if someone else registers the domain you want before you do, it may no longer be available. That’s why securing it early is recommended.
Q. What do the different domain extensions mean?
Different extensions like .co.za, .com, .blog, or .vip speak to:
Local audiences (e.g., .co.za)
General global presence (e.g., .com)
Creative or industry focus (e.g., .blog, .ltd, .vip)
Each extension helps position your brand differently, which is why we encourage multiple extensions at the time of registering, and we’re here to help you understand those differences.
Q. Can I register multiple domains in the same account?
Yes, once logged into your Customer Zone, you can register multiple domains and manage them all in one place.
Q. Can I connect my domain to a website or email after registration?
Yes, your domain can later be connected to a website, email service, or hosting.
Q. Can 1-grid help if I registered the domain but can’t find it?
Absolutely, we can help you locate any domain registered under your account, confirm ownership, and make sure you have access to manage it.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Smart Digital Branding with 1-grid’s AI Domain Name Generator
Smart Strategies for Affordable Premium Domain Alternatives
Understanding Premium Domains
Choosing a Great Domain Name
Why Renewal Invoices Are Sent Early
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Registering a domain with 1-grid doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Connecting a Wix Domain to an External Site
content:
If your domain is registered with Wix but your
website is hosted
somewhere else, like 1-grid, you can still connect the two. Wix does not allow
external nameservers
, so connecting a Wix domain to an external site is done entirely through
DNS pointing
.
This guide walks you through what this means, which
DNS records
you’ll need, and how to update your domain in Wix, so it loads your external website correctly.
Table of Contents
What This Means
Before You Begin
Updating DNS Records (A & CNAME) in Wix
FAQs
Additional Resources
What This Means
When your domain is hosted at Wix, and your website is hosted elsewhere:
You
cannot
change your nameservers to an external provider.
Instead, you connect your domain by
pointing the DNS
to your hosting provider.
Your external hosting provider must give you the correct
A and CNAME records
.
Once added in Wix, the domain will load your external website even though the
DNS
remains in Wix.
This method ensures seamless website loading while keeping your domain registered with Wix.
Before You Begin
IMPORTANT: You must request
DNS records
from your hosting provider, such as 1-grid, if you are hosting your website with us.
Ask them for:
The
A record
(IPv4 address of your hosting server)
The
CNAME record
(often “www” → your root domain)
Tell your provider, such as 1-grid:
“I need DNS A and CNAME records to connect my Wix domain without changing nameservers.”
If your host
requires nameservers
, then you must
transfer your domain away
from Wix
, because Wix does
not
allow
external nameserver changes
.
Updating DNS Records (A & CNAME) in Wix
Log in
to your Wix account.
Go to
Settings → Domains
.
Select the domain you want to connect.
Click
DNS Records
.
Under
A (Host)
records:
Edit the existing A record or delete and add a new one.
Host:
@
Points to:
your hosting provider’s IP address
Under
CNAME
:
Update the “www” CNAME.
Host:
www
Points to:
your domain name
(e.g. example.co.za)
or the value your host provided
Save your changes.
Allow up to
24 hours
for full
DNS propagation
.
Once complete, it should appear correctly and connecting a Wix domain to an external site was successful.
FAQs
Q: Why can’t I change nameservers for a Wix domain?
Wix does not allow custom nameservers for domains registered with them. All DNS must stay inside Wix.
Q: Can I connect my Wix domain to 1-grid hosting?
Yes, you must just use your 1-grid DNS A and CNAME records.
Q. Can I host my website elsewhere but keep email at 1-grid?
Yes, you will also need to
point your MX record to 1-grid
.
Additional Resources
Understanding DNS at 1-grid
Get Your 1-grid Domain Set Up Easily with Wix
How to Point Your Domain to Wix (Plesk)
How to Point Your Domain to Wix (cPanel)
Need Additional Support?
Connecting a Wix domain to an external site, such as your 1-grid-hosted website, doesn’t have to seem complicated with this easy-to-use guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (

title: Domains: How to Update Your Nameservers at 1-grid
content:
Learn what nameservers are, when you need to update them, and find the full list of 1-grid nameservers for different hosting types.
What Are Nameservers?
Nameservers are a key part of the
Domain Name System (DNS)
. They connect your domain (e.g., yourbusiness.co.za) to the server where your website and email are hosted. Nameservers make sure your domain points to the right place online.
If you’ve just registered a domain or moved your hosting, you may need to update the nameservers to make sure your website and email work correctly.
When Do You Need to Update Nameservers?
You may need to update your nameservers if:
You’ve registered a new domain and want to point it to your hosting.
You’ve transferred your hosting to 1-grid.
You’ve set up a VPS, Web Services, or cPanel/Plesk hosting package.
You have email or website hosting on an external platform and would like to point your domain to that server.
1-grid Nameservers List
Hosting Type
Nameservers
Windows Plesk
petra.ns.1-grid.com
petra.ns.1-grid.co.uk
petra.ns.1-grid.co.za
petra.ns.1-grid.net
Linux Plesk
thor.ns.1-grid.com
thor.ns.1-grid.co.uk
thor.ns.1-grid.co.za
thor.ns.1-grid.net
Linux cPanel (1)
linus.ns.1-grid.com
linus.ns.1-grid.co.uk
linus.ns.1-grid.co.za
linus.ns.1-grid.net
Linux cPanel (2)
ns1.hostserv.co.za
ns2.hostserv.co.za
Website Design
lnxwzdns01.hostserv.co.za
lnxwzdns02.hostserv.co.za
Business VPS
myserver.ns.1-grid.com
myserver.ns.1-grid.co.uk
myserver.ns.1-grid.co.za
myserver.ns.1-grid.net
How to Update Nameservers in Your Customer Zone
1. Make sure your website files, databases, and email accounts are set up on your 1-grid service.
2. Log in to your
Customer Zone.
3. Go to
Domains
.
4.
Select
the domain you want to update.
5. Choose
Manage Nameservers
.
6. Enter the correct nameservers from the list above.
7. Click
Change Nameservers
.
Important: DNS changes can take
4–48 hours
to fully update worldwide. You can check the progress using the free tool
DNS Checker
(
https://dnschecker.org/
).
How to Update Your Nameservers to Another Hosting Provider
1. Make sure your domain is configured to the server that you want to update your domain’s Nameserver to.  (You can confirm this by asking your host or checking the Nameservers in your DNS zone.)
2.
Log in
to your
Customer Zone.
3. Go to
Domains
→
My Domains
.
4.
Select
the domain you want to update.
5. Choose
Manage Nameservers
.
6. Enter the correct nameservers from your hosting provider.
7. Click

.

title: How to Enable or Disable Domain Auto-Renewal
content:
At 1-grid, your online brand and uninterrupted services are our top priority. That’s why we give you the freedom to choose whether to enable or disable your domain auto-renewal status.
Auto-renewal
helps you keep your domains active without worry, and automates your
invoicing for your domain
. By enabling this feature, you prevent accidental expirations, ensuring uninterrupted website and email services.
This guide will explain how to enable or disable this setting and the simple process to request changes.

Overview of Domain Auto-Renewal
Domain auto-renewal automatically renews your domain before it expires, so you never have to worry about your website or email going offline. This feature is designed to give you peace of mind, reduce administrative tasks, and help prevent accidental service disruptions.
Benefits of Enabling Auto-Renewal
Avoid Domain Expiry:
Ensures your domains remain active at all times.
Prevent Service Interruptions:
Websites, emails, and other domain-dependent services stay online.
Simplify Management:
Reduce manual renewals and focus on running your business.
Proactive Control:
Keep your domains secure and avoid potential downtime.
How to Request Enabling or Disabling Auto-Renewal
Changing your domain’s auto-renewal status is simple. Our Cancellation Team handles these requests quickly and securely.
Steps to Request Changes
Contact the Cancellation Team
Email:
[email protected]
Phone:
021 200 1200
Provide Domain Names
List all domains that need to be updated.
Provide a Reason (if disabling)
If you want to disable auto-renewal, include a clear reason. This ensures that our team can accurately record your request and provide the best support.
Confirmation
The Cancellation Team will verify your request and update your domain settings. Most changes are processed immediately.
IMPORTANT:
Should you request to
disable the auto-renewal function
on your domain, this means that
no further billing will occur
for this; however, your domain will remain in an “
Active
” status in your Customer Zone, and will be set to expire on the

of the domain.

Contact our Billing Team if you:
Have questions about invoices or billing cycles.

Need clarification on how auto-renewal affects your payments.

Can I enable auto-renew for multiple domains at once?
Yes, simply provide all domain names in your email or call request.
What happens if I disable auto-renew and forget to renew manually?
Your domain could expire, causing downtime for your website or email services.
How long does it take for changes to apply?
Changes are usually applied immediately, after your request is confirmed as actioned.
Why do I need to give a reason to disable auto-renewal?
Providing a reason helps our team keep accurate records and ensures we continue improving customer support.

title: Smart Strategies for Affordable Premium Domain Alternatives
content:
When building your online presence, your
domain name
is often the first impression people have of your brand. Sometimes, you’ll come across
premium domain names
which are short, memorable, and powerful domains that can instantly boost your brand’s credibility, but it does mean you will need to invest more by finding affordable premium domain alternatives.
At 1-grid, we don’t want to let a premium domain stop your online journey, so with a little creativity and the right tools, we’re here to help you still claim an affordable name that feels perfect for you and your online brand!
Let’s explore some strategies to find great, affordable alternatives using 1-grid’s tools, and what to consider before buying one, because we understand that this is not always affordable for you.
Table of Contents
Why Affordable Domains Are Still Powerful
When to Consider Investing in a Premium Domain
Smart Strategies for Finding an Affordable Domain
1. Use the AI Domain Generator
2. Add Keywords or Modifiers
3. Try Creative Word Combinations
4. Experiment with Different Extensions
5. Leverage Industry Keywords
6. Keep It Brandable
Comparison Pricing of Standard vs Premium Domains at Registration to Renewal with 1-grid
Benefits of Finding the Right Alternative to a Premium Domain Name
1. Builds Trust and Credibility
2. Improves SEO and Search Visibility
3. Easier to Remember and Share
Protecting and Managing Your Domain After Purchase
Protecting Your Domain
Managing Your Domain
FAQs
Additional Resources
Why Affordable Domains Are Still Powerful
A powerful domain name doesn’t have to come with a premium price tag. What truly defines online success is not
how much
you spend, but
how strategically
you build your brand around it.
Many thriving small businesses began with creative, affordable domain names using names that were easy to remember, relevant to their brand, and supported by great customer experiences. Over time, it was their
consistency
,
authentic storytelling
, and
commitment to service
that built brand trust, not the domain price.
Think of your domain as your
digital foundation,
where it’s what you build
on top
of it that makes the real impact. With strong SEO practices, social visibility, and a memorable customer journey, an affordable domain can grow into a powerful brand identity.
At 1-grid, we make it easy to explore creative, cost-effective options, affordable premium domain alternatives through our
Domain Search
and
AI Domain Generator
to find meaningful, keyword-rich, and creative alternatives that tell your story and fit your budget, because your
domain
is more than just a
URL
.
Your domain name doesn’t need to be expensive to be effective.
It needs to be
purposeful
,
brand-aligned
, and
easy for your customers to find and trust.
When to Consider Investing in a Premium Domain
While affordable premium domain alternatives offer exceptional value, there are moments when investing in a
premium domain
can be a smart, strategic move, especially for businesses ready to scale or dominate their niche.
A premium domain may be worth considering if:
It includes
high-value keywords
that can instantly boost your visibility and
SEO
ranking
.
It perfectly
matches your brand name
or a trademarked identity, helping customers find you faster.
It already has
strong existing traffic or backlinks
, giving you an immediate credibility lift.
You’re building a
long-term digital presence
and want to secure the most recognisable version of your brand online.
Before making a purchase, it’s important to
weigh the return on investment
, not just the price. Sometimes, an affordable variation can achieve the same results with the right digital strategy.
Smart Strategies for Finding an Affordable Domain
While a premium domain can strengthen your brand, it’s not the only path to success. What truly matters is finding a domain that fits your business identity, budget, and growth goals.
If a premium domain doesn’t fit your budget, don’t worry! There are plenty of
creative, effective, affordable premium domain alternatives
that work just as well.
At 1-grid, we believe every business deserves a strong, memorable
domain
, without the premium price tag.
Here’s how you can
find one
:
1. Use the AI Domain Generator
Use our
AI Domain Generator
to instantly generate creative, relevant, and available options based on your keywords.
Just type in your business name or idea, and our smart tool will suggest hundreds of names that fit your brand and budget.
2. Add Keywords or Modifiers
Use relevant words like “shop,” “online,” “hq,” or “official” to make your domain unique yet recognisable, and add small tweaks like “my,” or “get,” to your ideal domain.
Example: If
bloombeauty.com
is taken, try
bloombeautyonline.co.za
.
3. Try Creative Word Combinations
Blend two words or add a local touch.
Example:
capebloom.co.za
or
bloomstudio.co.za
.
4. Experiment with Different Extensions
Explore new and relevant
TLDs
such as
.co.za
,
.store
, or
.shop
., and highlight how this can make your business stand out while staying budget-friendly.
If
.com
or
.co.za
is unavailable,
explore newer or different domain extensions
like:
.online
for digital brands
.store
for eCommerce
.tech
for startups
.africa
for regional identity
5. Leverage Industry Keywords
Include descriptive, industry-specific words to instantly communicate what you do.
Example:
bloomhair.co.za
or
bloomspa.co.za
.
6. Keep It Brandable
Focus on creating a name that’s easy to say, spell, and remember. Choose one that reflects your brand’s personality and story.
Comparison Pricing of Standard vs Premium Domains at Registration to Renewal with 1-grid
When considering
registering a new domain
, it is important to consider initial registration pricing and how renewal pricing will impact you in the future. At 1-grid, we make it easy for your budget proactively, understanding that future planning is an integral part of the success of your brand.
See All Pricing
Benefits of Finding the Right Alternative to a Premium Domain Name
Investing in the right domain name can give your brand a head start in building trust and visibility online.
1. Builds Trust and Credibility
A relevant domain instantly communicates professionalism and reliability, helping potential customers feel confident in your brand.
2. Improves SEO and Search Visibility
Domains that often include strong,
high-value keywords
improve your chances of
ranking
higher on search engines.
3. Easier to Remember and Share
Memorable domains drive more direct traffic and word-of-mouth referrals, making it easier for customers to find you again.
Protecting and Managing Your Domain After Purchase
Protecting Your Domain
Register
variations and misspellings to avoid confusion.
Enable auto-renew
so you don’t lose control.
Managing Your Domain
To learn everything from how to secure your domain to managing it with us, you can find some useful information in our
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Guide.
FAQs
Why does my domain name matter so much?
Your domain name is often the first impression customers have of your brand. It affects how easy it is for people to find, remember, and trust your website. A great domain name builds credibility and supports your online growth from day one.
What makes a domain name “great”?
A great domain is short, easy to spell, memorable, and relevant to your brand or business. It should reflect who you are while being simple enough for people to type and share without errors.
Can I register more than one domain name?
Definitely. Many businesses secure multiple extensions (like: .co.za, .com, and .net) or similar variations of their name to protect their brand and redirect visitors to their main website.
What if my preferred domain name is already taken?
Try slight variations or add relevant words to your domain — for example, if brandname.co.za is taken, try getbrandname.co.za or brandnameonline.co.za.
You can also use the 1-grid
AI Domain Generator
to discover creative, available alternatives.
How can I check who owns a domain name I want?
You can perform a
WHOIS lookup
to find out who owns the domain and see if it’s available for purchase. Sometimes, owners are open to selling domains that aren’t actively in use.
What should I do once I’ve found the perfect domain name?
Register
immediately through 1-grid before someone else does. Once it’s registered, you can connect it to your
hosting
,
email
, or
website builder
and start building your online presence right away.

:

title: Understanding Premium Domains and Finding the Right Alternative
content:
When building your online presence, your
domain name
is often the first impression people have of your brand. Sometimes, you’ll come across premium domains which are short, memorable, and powerful domains that can instantly boost your brand’s credibility.
But what makes a
domain
“premium”? And do you really need one? Let’s explore what they are, why they cost more, and what to consider before buying one, including how to find great, affordable alternatives using 1-grid’s tools.
Table of Contents
Why Your Domain Name Matters
What Are Premium Domain Names?
The Two Types of Premium Domains
1. Aftermarket Premium Domains
2. Registry Premium Domains
Examples of Premium Top-Level Domains (TLDs)
Comparison Pricing of Standard vs Premium Domains at Registration to Renewal with 1-grid
Benefits of Premium Domain Names
1. Builds Trust and Credibility
2. Improves SEO and Search Visibility
3. Easier to Remember and Share
4. Long-Term Digital Asset Value
What to Consider Before Buying a Premium Domain
Smart Strategies for Finding Affordable Alternatives
1. Use the AI Domain Generator
2. Try Domain Variations
3. Explore Alternative Extensions
4. Keep It Brandable
Protecting and Managing Your Domain After You Buy
Protecting Your Domain
Managing Your Domain
FAQs
Additional Resources
Why Your Domain Name Matters
A
domain
is more than just a
URL
. It affects how people perceive your brand, how easy it is to share your site, and even how well you
rank
. A good name builds credibility; a bad one causes mis-clicks or confusion.
This means
it is important
in the following contexts:
Branding, credibility, memorability
SEO
implications (keywords, extension, readability)
Longevity because you want a name that scales
A premium domain can strengthen your brand, but it’s not the only path to success. What truly matters is finding a domain that fits your business identity, budget, and growth goals.
At 1-grid, we make it easy, whether you’re
buying a premium name
or exploring creative, cost-effective options through our
Domain Search
and
AI Domain Name Generator
.
Your domain is your digital home, so choose one that reflects your vision and helps your business grow confidently online.
What Are Premium Domain Names?
A premium domain name is a high-value web address that’s easy to remember, short, keyword-rich, and often already associated with a specific industry, service, or niche.
Because these domains are in high demand, they usually come at a higher initial cost, and sometimes, a higher renewal cost (when on promotion at a lower first-time registering cost), but they can offer real advantages for branding, marketing, and SEO.
Premium domains often use popular top-level domains (TLDs) like .com, .net, or .org, but can also include newer extensions like .store, .tech, or .online, and have shorter domain names. These also include newer, high-value extensions like .ai and .io, as well as brandable ones like .co, .vip, and .pro.
The Two Types of Premium Domains
There are
two main categories
of premium domain names:
1. Aftermarket Premium Domains
These are domains already registered by someone else and listed for resale.
Typically, more expensive upfront.
Renew at standard domain rates after purchase.
2. Registry Premium Domains
These are domains that aren’t registered but are designated as “premium” by the registry.
Often include newer extensions like
.tech
,
.store
, or
.online
.
Both registration and renewal prices are higher than standard domains.
Examples of Premium Top-Level Domains (TLDs)
Examples of premium domain names include short, memorable, industry-specific, or keyword-rich domains like Cars.com or Business.com, as well as three-letter acronyms like nyc.com. These domains are highly valuable and command high prices on the aftermarket because of their branding power and scarcity.
Examples of premium domain names:
Cars.com
: Short, industry-specific, and a powerful keyword.
Business.com
: Sold for $345 million and is a prime example of a high-value keyword domain.
Insurance.com
: Another multi-million-dollar sale that demonstrates the value of a specific, industry-defining keyword.
Lasvegas.com
: An example of a geographic premium domain.
Nyc.com
: Short, well-known, and often used as a premium domain example.
Newer and Specialised TLDs
.ai
Popular for AI-related businesses and technology companies.
.io
A TLD with a premium price tag is often used by tech startups and software companies.
Other Considerations
Country code TLDs (ccTLDs)
Some country-code domains, particularly those that are short or have high demand, can also be considered premium depending on how they are used.
For example,
.africa
is a pan-African TLD that signals a regional connection but only becomes considered a premium domain based on its usage, such as
travel.africa
because it contains both a “
high-intent keyword
” and a “geographic identifier”.
Registry-reserved Domains
Registries can set aside specific premium domains that are particularly valuable, such as short words or acronyms.
Comparison Pricing of Standard vs Premium Domains at Registration to Renewal with 1-grid
When considering
registering a new domain
, it is important to consider initial registration pricing and how renewal pricing will impact you in the future. With us at 1-grid, we make it easy for your budget proactively, understanding that future planning is an integral part of the success of your brand.
See All Pricing
Benefits of Premium Domain Names
Investing in a premium domain can give your brand a head start in building trust and visibility online.
1. Builds Trust and Credibility
A short, relevant domain instantly communicates professionalism and reliability, helping potential customers feel confident in your brand.
2. Improves SEO and Search Visibility
Premium domains often include strong, high-value keywords, improving your chances of
ranking
higher on search engines.
3. Easier to Remember and Share
Memorable domains drive more direct traffic and word-of-mouth referrals, making it easier for customers to find you again.
4. Long-Term Digital Asset Value
Over time, a premium domain can increase in value, making it a strong business investment as your brand grows.
Examples of Premium Domains in Action:
Cars.com
Hotels.com
Voice.com
CarInsurance.com
These names are simple, direct, and unforgettable, and that’s exactly what makes them premium.
What to Consider Before Buying a Premium Domain
Before purchasing, it’s important to balance
brand value
with
budget and long-term goals
. Here are key factors to keep in mind:
Understand the Value:
Premium domains are short, keyword-rich, and easy to remember but are not always essential for success.
Set a Realistic Budget:
Plan based on your marketing goals and potential return on investment.
Research Market Pricing:
Review recent domain sales and trends to understand what’s fair and competitive.
Verify Ownership and Legitimacy:
Use a
WHOIS lookup
to confirm who owns the domain and check if it’s truly available for registration.
Negotiate if Possible:
Some sellers are open to negotiation, so don’t hesitate to make a reasonable offer.
Use a Secure Payment Method:
Choose reputable marketplaces or use escrow services to protect your transaction.
Check for Trademarks:
Avoid names that could infringe on existing brands to prevent future legal issues.
Review Renewal Fees:
Registry premium domains may have higher renewal costs, so factor these into your long-term budget.
Plan How You’ll Use It:
Whether for your main website, marketing campaigns, or brand protection, know your domain’s purpose before purchasing.
Smart Strategies for Finding Affordable Alternatives
If a premium domain doesn’t fit your budget, don’t worry! There are plenty of
creative, effective alternatives
that work just as well.
At 1-grid, we believe every business deserves a strong, memorable
domain
, without the premium price tag.
Here’s how you can
find one
:
1. Use the AI Domain Generator
Use our
AI Domain Generator
to instantly generate creative, relevant, and available options based on your keywords.
Just type in your business name or idea, and our smart tool will suggest hundreds of names that fit your brand and budget.
2. Try Domain Variations
Add small tweaks like “my,” “get,” or “hq” to your ideal domain.
Example: If
example.co.za
is taken, try
myexample.co.za
or
examplehq.co.za
.
3. Explore Alternative Extensions
If
.com
or
.co.za
is unavailable, explore newer domain extensions like:
.online
for digital brands
.store
for eCommerce
.tech
for startups
.africa
for regional identity
4. Keep It Brandable
Focus on creating a name that’s easy to say, spell, and remember, choose one that reflects your brand’s personality and story.
Protecting and Managing Your Domain After You Buy
Protecting Your Domain
Register variations and misspellings to avoid confusion.
Enable auto-renew so you don’t lose control.
Managing Your Domain
To learn everything from how to secure your domain to managing it with us, you can find some useful information in our
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Guide.
FAQs
Can I register more than one domain name?
Definitely, and we recommend it! Many businesses secure multiple extensions (like .co.za, .com, and .net) or similar variations of their name to protect their brand and redirect visitors to their main website.
Can I change my domain name later?
No, a registered domain name cannot be changed. You can, however, register a new domain name. Due to its annual renewal process, this means you pay upfront for the domain. It’s not ideal once your brand is established because changing a domain can affect SEO rankings, email accounts, and brand recognition. It’s best to invest time upfront in choosing the right name for long-term success.
What should I do once I’ve found the perfect domain name?
Register
immediately through 1-grid before someone else does. Once it’s registered, you can connect it to your
hosting
,
email
, or
website builder
and start building your online presence right away.

title: Choosing a Great Domain Name for Your Online Brand Matters

Your
domain
name is not just a web address; it’s your brand’s first impression so choosing a great domain name matters. Pick wisely, and it helps people remember, trust, and find you. Pick poorly, and you might cause confusion or regret.
In this guide, we walk through what makes a domain name strong, how to use 1-grid’s
AI generator
, and how to avoid common pitfalls to empower you with
registering
the right domain name for your brand. Once it’s registered, you can connect it to your
hosting
,
email
, or

and start building your online presence right away.

2. Keep It Simple and Clear
3. Shorter Is Stronger
4. Avoid Hyphens and Confusion
5. Explore Other Domain Extensions
6. Keep It Singular
7. Use WHOIS for Domain Insights
8. Make It Keyword-Rich (But Natural)
9. Think About Direct Traffic (Type-In Traffic)

11. Make It Brandable and Memorable

13. Think Long-Term
Using Our AI Domain Generator

Protecting and Managing Your Domain After You Buy

Managing Your Domain
FAQs
