title: Inter-Account Transfers: Moving a Domain or Hosting Service from One Profile to Another
content:
There are times when moving a domain or hosting service from one 1-grid profile to another is necessary as an inter-account transfer, whether due to a change in ownership for a specific service or domain, account restructuring, or a handover to a new person or business.
We understand that transfers can feel uncertain because they involve multiple parties and backend changes, which is why we’ve designed this process to be clear and straightforward.
This guide will explain how the transfer works, the difference between inter-account transfers and a full change of ownership, what is required from each party, and what you can expect at every step, so your service move is secure, seamless, and stress-free.
Table of Contents
Inter-Account Transfer vs. Change of Ownership
How an Inter-Account Transfer Works
How Outstanding Invoices Are Handled During a Transfer
Your Options (Step-by-Step)
1: The Current Owner Settles the Balance (Simplest Route)
2: The New Owner Takes Responsibility (Flexible Route)
Important to Know
Step-by-Step Transfer Instructions
1. Submit Your Transfer Request
2. Copy the New Profile Owner
3. Receiving Party Confirms Acceptance
4. We Action the Transfer
How to View Your Moved Services
Domains
Hosting and Other Services
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Inter-Account Transfer vs. Change of Ownership
An
inter-account transfer
moves specific domains or hosting services from one 1-grid profile to another, while both accounts remain active. A
Change of Ownership (COO)
, on the other hand, transfers full control of an entire 1-grid account, including all linked services, to a new owner. This means that if you only need to move individual services, it would be an inter-account transfer.
How an Inter-Account Transfer Works
When you move a domain or hosting service between 1-grid profiles, you are changing which 1-grid account owns those services. This can be useful when:
You sell or transfer ownership of a website or business
You restructure accounts
You consolidate services under one profile
How Outstanding Invoices Are Handled During a Transfer
We know that billing arrangements can feel sensitive when services are moving between profiles. Our goal is to make this part of the process transparent, fair, and stress-free for both parties, so you can move forward with confidence and clarity.
If there are any outstanding invoices on the current owner’s account, you have two clear, flexible options so you can choose what works best for your situation.
Your Options (Step-by-Step)
1: The Current Owner
Settles
the Balance (Simplest Route)
The current owner confirms that they will take responsibility for the outstanding
invoice
.
The invoice is paid in full on the current owner’s profile.
Once payment is reflected, we proceed with the service transfer.
Confirmation of the completed transfer is sent to both parties.
This option supports a clean handover with no billing carried over to the new owner.
2: The New Owner Takes
Responsibility
(Flexible Route)
The current owner confirms in writing that the new owner will take responsibility for the outstanding balance.
The new owner also confirms this in writing.
One of the following then happens:
The new owner settles the invoice before the transfer, or
We cancel the invoice on the current owner’s account, complete the transfer, and reissue the invoice on the new owner’s profile.
Once settled (or re-issued), we confirm completion of the transfer to both parties.
Selecting this option when moving forward allows for a smooth transition even when billing responsibility is changing hands.
Important to Know
Invoices
cannot be transferred between accounts
, which is why a new invoice must be created if responsibility moves to the new owner.
Written confirmation from both parties helps us protect everyone involved and avoid misunderstandings.
Step-by-Step Transfer Instructions
Below are the steps required to complete a service inter-account transfer.
All steps must be followed before we can take action on the request.
1. Submit Your Transfer Request
Send an email from the registered email address of the current owner to
[email protected]
with the following information:
The services you want to transfer
The
originating profile
customer code
The
receiving profile
customer code
The
preferred
invoicing option, and
CC the new owner
in for written consent on all the above from their end
IMPORTANT:
This gives our team everything they need to start the process without back-and-forth emails.
2. Copy the New Profile Owner
Copy the person or company taking over the services in your request email.
Why this matters:
This confirms everyone involved is aware and ready to participate. It also helps to avoid delays and misunderstandings and ensures transparency and agreement between both parties.
3. Receiving Party Confirms Acceptance
The new account holder must reply to that same email and confirm that they accept the service transfer.
IMPORTANT:
This protects you and the new owner and ensures that only services with shared consent are moved.
4. We Action the Transfer
Once both sides have confirmed, our Billing team will process the transfer on the back end.
You’ll receive
confirmation
once the transfer is complete.
How to View Your Moved Services
After the transfer is completed:
Domains
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
Go to
Domains
Use the search bar if you don’t see the moved domain immediately
TIP:
This is a quick way to confirm ownership has transferred correctly.
Hosting and Other Services
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
Go to
Services
Locate the moved hosting package
TIP:
Everything should now be neat under your new profile.
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Assist With
Confirming your Customer Zone details
Explaining the steps to both parties
Checking the status of a transfer request
Ensuring services appear in the correct profile
Outside Our Scope
What We Cannot Assist With, or Action
Transfer a service without approval from
both
the current and receiving owners
Move services without proper identifying information
Perform changes without written consent
FAQs
Q: How long does the transfer take?
Typically, it depends on how quickly both parties respond, since we need authorisation before we can proceed.
Q: Can I transfer multiple services at once?
Yes, just list all services clearly in the same email request.
Q: What if the other party doesn’t respond?
We can follow up, but transfers can only proceed with their confirmation.
Q: Do I lose data or settings when moving services?
No, everything stays intact because only the owning profile changes.
Additional Resources
How to Keep Your 1-grid Account Details Up to Date
How to Keep Your 1-grid Domain Contact Details Up to Date
Recovering Your Lost 1-grid Customer Zone Password
How to Update Your 1-grid Customer Zone Password While Logged In
Verifying Your 1-grid Account for Support
Viewing Your Logged Support Tickets in Your 1-grid Customer Zone
Managing Your DNS Records for Your Domain
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Doing an inter-account transfer doesn’t have to feel stressful with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Ad-Blockers Might Prevent 3D Secure Payments
content:
At 1-grid, your online safety and uninterrupted services are our top priority. When making secure payments online, whether by
credit card
or
Instant EFT
through
PayFast
, you’ll be prompted to verify your transaction using
3D Secure authentication
(like Visa Secure or Mastercard SecureCode).
However,
ad-blockers
or certain
browser extensions
can sometimes interfere with this step, preventing your payment from being completed successfully.
This guide explains what
3D Secure
is, how
ad-blockers might affect payments
, and the simple steps you can take to ensure a smooth, secure, and successful transaction every time.
Table of Contents
What Is 3D Secure (Visa Secure / Mastercard SecureCode)?
Mastercard SecureCode/NetCode Explained
Verified by Visa Explained
How Ad-Blockers Might Prevent 3D Secure Payments
How to Prevent This When Making Payment Online
When Your Security and Transparency Are Top Priority
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is 3D Secure (Visa Secure / Mastercard SecureCode)?
With your online safety as our top priority, all card and Instant EFT payments that are processed through
PayFast
use
3D Secure technology
, an added layer of authentication developed by
Visa
and
Mastercard
.
3D Secure, also known as
Visa Secure
(formerly “Verified by Visa”) or
Mastercard SecureCode/NetCode
helps protect you from unauthorised card transactions by confirming your identity before processing your payment.
When you make a payment, you’ll see a secure pop-up or redirected page from your bank, prompting you to verify the transaction using a
One-Time PIN (OTP)
or your
banking app
. This step ensures only you can approve the purchase, reducing the risk of fraud.
Mastercard SecureCode/NetCode Explained
Mastercard SecureCode
(sometimes called
NetCode
) is a private code that acts like an extra password for your card.
When you pay online via PayFast, your bank sends a One-Time PIN or prompts you to authorise the payment in your mobile app.
Once confirmed, your transaction is securely processed, and your
invoice
payment reflects instantly on your 1-grid account.
Verified by Visa Explained
Verified by Visa (now Visa Secure)
works in a similar way where it verifies that you are the legitimate cardholder using your bank’s secure verification method.
This might involve entering a One-Time PIN or approving the payment via your banking app.
This extra step gives you peace of mind knowing that your card details remain private, even when paying online.
How Ad-Blockers Might Prevent 3D Secure Payments
While
ad-blockers
and
browser extensions
help protect your online privacy, they can sometimes
interfere with secure payment pages
like those used for 3D Secure authentication.
These tools may:
Block the secure pop-up from your bank.
Prevent redirects to the 3D Secure verification page.
Disrupt the loading of
PayFast
’s
secure payment window.
When this happens, your transaction may not complete successfully, or the page might appear to “freeze” or “time out.”
TIP: If you use
AdBlock
Plus
, please turn this off.
How to Prevent This When Making Payment Online
To ensure smooth, secure payment processing, follow these steps.
Disable your ad-blocker temporarily
before starting your online payment.
Use a trusted browser
(such as Chrome, Firefox, or Edge) and keep it up to date.
Clear your browser cache and cookies
before retrying your payment.
Ensure pop-ups are allowed
for PayFast and your bank’s verification page.
Check your internet connection
and avoid public Wi-Fi when making payments.
TIP:
You can
whitelist
PayFast by visiting its official domain (
https://www.payfast.co.za
) to allow it to load properly, even with your ad-blocker enabled.
When Your Security and Transparency Are Top Priority
We know financial information is sensitive, and your trust matters. That’s why all secure transactions with 1-grid are
processed securely
through
PCI DSS-compliant payment gateways
, ensuring your data remains safe and encrypted, making 3D Secure Payments a priority!
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if you:
Made a payment that didn’t complete due to a 3D Secure block,
Been charged, but your payment hasn’t reflected, or
Tried disabling your ad-blocker, but still encounter verification issues
FAQs
Can I safely whitelist PayFast for payments?
Yes, PayFast is a
PCI DSS Level 1 compliant
payment gateway; the highest global standard for payment security. You can safely whitelist
https://www.payfast.co.za
.
My payment failed after 3D Secure verification. What should I do?
Retry your payment after clearing the cache and disabling your ad-blocker. If it still fails, contact
[email protected]
for quick resolution.
Which payment methods use 3D Secure?
All card payments (Visa, Mastercard) and some
Instant EFT
transactions processed through
PayFast
require 3D Secure authentication for your protection.
Additional Resources
Paying Invoices with Credit Card
Easy-to-Use Payment Methods
Paying Invoices with Instant EFT
Why Pay with Credit Card?
How to Update Your Credit Card Details
Invoicing at 1-grid Keeps Your Online and On Time
Avoiding Suspensions is Priority for Business
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Figuring out why your secure payment won’t work doesn’t have to feel like a challenge with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
content:
In today’s digital world, passwords alone are no longer enough.
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
adds an extra layer of protection by ensuring only you can access your account, even if someone compromises your password.
This guide will help you understand what Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is, why it plays a critical role in protecting your account, and how to enable and use it confidently within your 1-grid Customer Zone.
Table of Contents
What Is Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)?
How Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Works
Why Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Is Critical for Next-Level Security
How 1-grid Uses Two-Factor Authentication
First-Time 2FA Automatic Prompt on Login
Quick Setup Steps to Add Your 1-grid Account by Authenticator App for 2FA
Logging In After 2FA Is Enabled
Using Authenticator App
Using a Backup Code
Logging in After 2FA Setup Using the Mobile-Only Method: Use a Backup Code
Using a Backup Code (Mobile-Friendly Login)
Passwords + 2FA = Strongest Protection
Troubleshooting Authentication App Issues (2FA)
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)?
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is a security method that requires
two different types of verification
before granting access.
It combines:
Something you know
(your password)
Something you have
(a code from an authenticator app or trusted device)
This extra step makes it significantly harder for
unauthorised users
to access your account, even if they know your password.
How Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Works
Typically, the steps are the same for most 2FA login requirements.
You log in with your email address and password
Then, you complete a second verification step
You enter a time-sensitive code from an authenticator app
The system grants access once you complete and confirm both steps
IMPORTANT:
Your account remains protected as long as you don’t share your 2FA device.
Why Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Is Critical for Next-Level Security
Key Benefits for Customers
Prevents
unauthorised access
Protects sensitive billing and personal information
Reduces the risk of
phishing attacks
Adds peace of mind without adding complexity
TIP:
2FA actively involves
you
in your account security by turning protection into a partnership, not a passive system.
How 1-grid Uses Two-Factor Authentication
At 1-grid, we use
an authenticator-based 2FA
for
Customer Zone
logins, which iswidely trusted and used by platforms like Google, Microsoft, and Apple.
Supported authenticator apps include:
Google Authenticator
Microsoft Authenticator
Apple Authenticator App
Authy
Duo
First-Time 2FA Automatic Prompt on Login
Quick Steps to Follow
Log in to
Customer Zone
Click
Get Started >>
Open
the Authenticator App
on your phone
Scan the
QR code
Enter the
authenticator code
Click
Submit
Securely store your
Backup Code
IMPORTANT:
You use a
single backup code once
if you lose your authenticator device or can’t access your Authenticator app. Store your backup codes securely, because the system generates a new code each time you use one.
Quick Setup Steps to Add Your 1-grid Account by Authenticator App for 2FA
Adding your 1-grid account to your Authentication App
is easy. We’ve provided quick steps below, but explore our
full guide
for full steps.
Google Authenticator App
Quick
Steps to Follow
Open
Google Authenticator
on your phone.
Tap the
+
button →
Scan a QR code
.
Scan the QR code shown on your screen for Customer Zone login.
Enter the 6-digit code displayed in the app to confirm.
Double-check all steps have been completed, and make sure to securely store your
Backup Code
.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Google Authenticator, you can also explore this
short video
.
Microsoft Authenticator App
Quick
Steps to Follow
Open
Microsoft Authenticator
.
Tap
+
(top right).
Select
Personal account
.
Choose
Scan a QR code
.
Scan the QR code displayed in your 1-grid setup screen using your phone.
Enter the generated 6-digit code to complete setup.
Double-check all steps have been completed, and make sure to securely store your
Backup Code
.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Microsoft Authenticator, you can explore this
short video
.
Apple Authenticator (via iOS Passwords)
Quick
Steps to Follow
On your iPhone, go to
Settings → Passwords
.
Select the relevant account (or add a new one).
Tap
Set Up Verification Code
→
Scan QR Code
.
Scan the QR code and enter the generated code to confirm.
Double-check all steps have been completed, and make sure to securely store your
Backup Code
.
NOTE:
To add your account via the Apple Authenticator app specifically and not through Passwords, you can check out their useful guide
here
.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Apple Passwords, you can explore this
guide
further.
Authy Authenticator App
Quick
Steps to Follow
Open
Authy
.
Tap
+ Add Account
.
Choose
Scan QR Code
.
Scan the QR code shown on your screen using your phone.
Enter the 6-digit code to verify setup.
Double-check all steps have been completed, and make sure to securely store your
Backup Code
.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Authy, you can explore this
short video
.
Logging In After 2FA Is Enabled
Using Authenticator App
Quick
Steps to Follow
Log in to
Customer Zone
Enter your
registered email address
and
password
Click
Login
Open your
Authenticator App
and
generate a one-time code
Enter
the generated code
Click
Login
Using a Backup Code
Quick
Steps to Follow
Log in to
Customer Zone
Enter your
registered email address
and
password
Click
Login
Select
Login using Backup Code
Enter
the backup code
Click
Login
Save the
new backup code
displayed and click
Continue
Logging in After 2FA Setup Using the Mobile-Only Method: Use a Backup Code
It is recommended to use this method if:
You’re logging into the 1-grid Customer Zone using a
mobile browser
Your
Authenticator app is on the same device
Switching between the browser and the Authenticator app causes the login page to refresh
You’re unable to enter the 6-digit code in time before the page reloads
This is a known mobile behaviour on some devices and browsers, and not an error with your account or setup.
When app switching isn’t possible, the
“Login using Backup Code”
option is the recommended method as it is the most stable and secure way to complete your login.
Using a Backup Code (Mobile-Friendly Login)
Quick Steps to Follow
Open your mobile browser and go to the
1-grid Customer Zone login page
Enter your email address and password as normal
When prompted for your 2FA code, select
“Login using Backup Code”
Enter one of the backup codes that were generated when you enabled 2FA
You’ll be logged in successfully without needing to switch apps
IMPORTANT:
Backup codes are
single-use
, secure, and designed specifically for situations like this.
Passwords + 2FA = Strongest Protection
2FA works best when combined with a
strong password
.
We recommend exploring our guides below to help you choose and update your passwords regularly:
How to Make Sure Your Password Is Very Strong
How Do I Choose a Good Password
Update Your 1-grid Password While Logged In
Troubleshooting Authentication App Issues (2FA)
If you’re having trouble generating or using authentication codes during setup or login, don’t worry, as
most authentication issues are quick and easy to resolve
with our
troubleshooting guide
.
The steps below quickly address the most common causes across popular authenticator apps, including
Google Authenticator
,
Microsoft Authenticator
, and similar apps.
Most Common Fix
Check Your Phone’s Date and Time Settings
Authentication apps rely on
time-based codes
. If your phone’s time is even slightly out of sync, valid codes may be rejected.
What to do:
Set your phone’s
Date & Time
to
Automatic
Enable
Automatic Time Zone
App-specific tips:
Google Authenticator:
Go to
Settings → Time Correction for Codes → Sync now
Microsoft Authenticator:
The phone’s date and time must be set to
Automatic
TIP:
This single step resolves the majority of “invalid code” errors.
Additional Common Quick Fixes
Make Sure You’re Using the Correct Code
Important
:
Codes expire every
30 seconds
Ensure you’re entering the
current 6-digit code
Confirm the code is for your
1-grid account
, not another saved account in the app
Restart and Update Your Authenticator App
Temporary app issues can cause codes to fail.
Try the following:
Close the authenticator app completely
Restart your phone
Update the app to the latest version via your app store
Check App Permissions and Notifications
Some authenticator apps rely on system permissions to function properly.
Check that:
Notifications are enabled for the app
Battery optimisation or power-saving mode is disabled (especially on Android)
Background app activity is allowed
Disable VPNs or Network Restrictions
VPN
s or restricted networks can sometimes interfere with authentication.
Temporarily disable any
VPN
Try logging in using a standard mobile or home internet connection
Remove Your Account to Re-Add It
Re-registering the account often fixes the issue.
What to do:
Remove the 1-grid account from your authenticator app
Then, contact our Support Team to let them know that you need to re-add the account
Once our Team has assisted with resetting your 2FA, you can set it up again by scanning the new QR code during the prompt when you log in to your Customer Zone.
TIP:
This creates a fresh, properly synced authentication link.
Use Backup Codes
If you’ve switched devices and did not restore your authenticator app from a backup.
If you’re unable to log in, contact our Support Team for assistance with identity verification and secure 2FA reset on your new device’s installed Authenticator app.
Use
backup codes
(if you saved them during setup) for mobile-friendly
setups
or
access
.
Important to Know
Authentication apps work
offline
, but require correct device time setups.
2FA adds an important security layer, but
we’re here to help
if setup doesn’t go smoothly.
We can assist with
resetting 2FA
, but for security reasons, we can’t bypass authentication checks.
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Re-enabling, resetting or understanding 2FA
Login issues related to 2FA
Backup code guidance
We’ll guide you through the next steps securely and as smoothly as possible.
Outside Our Scope
What We’re Unable to Assist With
Bypassing security protections, such as the codes, during authentication checks
FAQs
Q. Is Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) mandatory?
Yes, for next-level security on your 1-grid account, 2FA is required.
Q. What if I lose my phone or get a new one?
Use your backup code to log in if you don’t have a new phone yet and then contact our Support Team to assist you with resetting your 2FA so that you can reconfigure 2FA on your new phone.
Q. Can multiple users use the same 1-grid account with 2FA enabled?
Yes, multiple users can access the same 1-grid account,
but
each user should use their own login
and authentication app
.
For security and accountability:
Each user should have
their own unique credentials
Each login should be protected with
its own 2FA setup
Sharing a single login or authentication device is
not recommended
IMPORTANT:
It keeps access secure, prevents lockouts, and ensures you can manage or remove access without affecting other users.
However, if a single login is absolutely required, backup codes can be used, ensuring that this is shared securely and safely between users each time it is reset at login.
Q. What happens if an employee leaves and they had 2FA access?
If an employee leaves your organisation, their
access should be removed
as soon as possible
. Removing access promptly prevents unauthorised logins and keeps your account fully under your control.
What to do:
Disable or
remove
the user’s login from your 1-grid account
Reset any shared credentials immediately, like
passwords
(if applicable)
Confirm that their authentication app is no longer linked to your account
If you’re unsure which users have access or need help securing the account, our Support Team can assist.
Q. Is Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) the same as Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)?
No, 2FA uses two factors; MFA uses two or more.
Additional Resources
Adding an Account for 2FA Using an Authenticator App
Authenticator App Troubleshooting Guide
Retrieve a Lost 1-grid Customer Zone Password
Everything Password-Related at 1-grid
How To Keep Your 1-grid Account Details Up to Date
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for next-level security on your 1-grid Customer Zone doesn’t have to cause you worry with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: How to Update Your Credit Card Details for Secure Payments
content:
At 1-grid, we understand how important it is to keep your hosting, email, and domain services running smoothly, without interruptions or delays, so we’ve made it quick and easy to update your credit card details.
Updating your credit card details ensures that your payments are processed automatically and on time, keeping your services active and avoiding unnecessary suspensions or disruptions. With
PayFast
’s encrypted technology
and
PCI DSS compliance
, your payment information stays fully protected while transactions are processed instantly.
This quick guide will show you
how to update your credit card details securely in your Customer Zone
, ensuring that your account stays up to date and your online services remain uninterrupted while getting the
benefits
of
paying via credit card
securely paying your
invoices
via your Customer Zone.
Table of Contents
How to Update Your Credit Card Details Securely
Update Your Payment Method via Your 1-grid Customer Zone
Set Up Your Credit Card Details (Subscribing Using PayFast)
Select Your Credit Card Details for Recurring Payment
Security and Transparency: Keeping Your Payments Safe
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
How to Update Your Credit Card Details Securely
We understand the urgency of making updates quickly so that you can get back to business, so we’ve provided quick steps on how to get your credit card details set up. Each section provides you with a full guide, so just click on the one you need, and we’ll empower you with step-by-step guidance, in detail.
Update Your Payment Method via Your 1-grid Customer Zone
Follow these quick, simple steps to update your payment method to PayFast Secure Credit Card for all future invoices:
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Click on
Update
in
Your Info
section.
Select
PayFast Secure Credit Card
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
Click on
Save Changes
.
Set Up Your Credit Card Details (Subscribing Using PayFast)
To set up your credit card details for the first time to use Secure Credit Card via PayFast:
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Click on
Billing → My Invoices
.
Locate the unpaid invoice and click on it to open it.
Make sure that
PayFast Secure Credit Card
is selected under
Payment Method
.
Click
Subscribe Using Payfast
and
fill in your details
.
You’ll be redirected to the secure
PayFast portal
.
Enter your
credit card details
and follow the
3D Secure prompts
.
Complete your
first payment
using your credit card details.
Once completed, you’ll receive instant payment confirmation via email.
Your account will be updated immediately, and your services will remain active.
Select Your Credit Card Details for Recurring Payment
Once you have subscribed to use your credit card via PayFast, you can pay all future invoices as recurring payments:
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Click on
Billing → My Invoices
.
Locate the unpaid invoice and click on it to open it.
Select
Recurring Payment
.
You’ll be redirected to the secure
PayFast portal
.
Enter your
credit card details
and follow the
3D Secure prompts
.
Once completed, you’ll receive instant payment confirmation via email.
TIP:
Always double-check that your account has sufficient funds to avoid failed transactions or service suspensions.
Security and Transparency: Keeping Your Payments Safe
At 1-grid, your trust and online security always come first. That’s why all
credit card payments processed through PayFast
are protected by
PCI DSS Level 1 compliance
— the highest global standard for payment data protection. This ensures that every transaction is handled securely, giving you complete peace of mind when updating or saving your payment details.
We partner only with
trusted, verified payment providers
, and every step of the process is encrypted to prevent unauthorised access or fraud.
To keep your account even more secure:
Activate your card for online transactions
before making a payment.
Never share your One-Time PIN (OTP)
, CVV, or online banking login details with anyone.
Double-check the URL
to always ensure you’re on the official
PayFast domain
(
https://payment.payfast.io/eng/process/payment/
) before entering your card information.
Monitor your bank statements
regularly to confirm that all transactions are accurate and authorised.
Reach out to 1-grid Billing Support immediately
if you suspect any irregular activity or have concerns about a transaction from 1-grid.
By following these simple steps, you can ensure your payments are secure, transparent, and fully protected, just the way they should be.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if:
You’ve contacted your bank to troubleshoot any issues on their end.
Your card payment fails, but funds are deducted.
You’re unsure if the transaction was successful.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Is it safe to update or pay with my credit card through PayFast?
Absolutely, as all PayFast transactions are secured with
SSL encryption
and
3D Secure verification
, ensuring your personal and payment details are always protected. PayFast is also
PCI DSS Level 1 compliant
, which is the highest international standard for payment security.
Can I store my credit card details for faster future payments?
Yes, when “Subscribing Using PayFast” you can
safely save your card details
on PayFast as “Recurring Payment” for quick, one-click payments next time. Your information is encrypted and stored securely, giving you both convenience and peace of mind.
How quickly will my credit card payment reflect on my 1-grid account?
Payments made through PayFast are
processed instantly
, which means your services remain active without interruption or delays. This makes credit card payments one of the fastest and most reliable ways to settle invoices.
What should I do if my credit card payment doesn’t go through?
If your payment fails, first ensure that your
card is activated for online transactions
and that
sufficient funds
are available. If the issue continues, contact your bank to confirm your payment limits or security settings. You can also reach out to
[email protected]
for quick assistance.
Can I switch to Debit Order or another payment method later?
Yes, you have full flexibility. Debit Order remains our
recommended payment method
for long-term convenience, but you can easily update your preferred payment option anytime through your
Customer Zone
.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Debit Orders at 1-grid
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
How Do I Update My Payment Method?
Checking Your Billing Cycle via Your Customer Zone
Viewing Your Invoices via Your Customer Zone
Less Stress, More Focus Means Easy Payments with 1-grid
Pay On Time and Stay Online with Invoicing at 1-grid
Making No Suspensions a Priority for Your Business
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Updating your credit card details to pay securely via PayFast for your services doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Switch to Secure Credit Card When Making a Payment
content:
At 1-grid, we know how important it is to keep your hosting, email, and domain services running smoothly without interruptions, delays, or unnecessary admin. That’s why we’ve made it easy to pay with
secure credit card payments
via
PayFast
, South Africa’s trusted and verified payment gateway, even as a one-off payment.
Switching to PayFast allows you to settle invoices instantly, even if they were issued under a different payment method. With
PCI DSS Level 1 compliance
and
end-to-end encryption
, your payment details are always protected while transactions reflect immediately, ensuring your services stay active without downtime.
This quick guide will show you how to
switch to a credit card payment in your Customer Zone
for paying invoices securely, while still getting the
benefits
of
paying via credit card
securely to settle your
invoices
, even if you don’t want to use this payment method every time.
Table of Contents
How to Switch to Secure Credit Card Payment Securely
Selecting PayFast Secure Credit Card (Once-Off Payment)
Selecting Your Existing Credit Card Details for Recurring Payment
Security and Transparency with Credit Card Payments
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
How to Switch to Secure Credit Card Payment Securely
Selecting PayFast Secure Credit Card (Once-Off Payment)
If you’re using your credit card for the first time to make a payment, you may need to set up your credit card details to subscribe using PayFast, so that you can make your payment.
We’ve made it easy with these quick steps:
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Click on
Billing → My Invoices
.
Locate the unpaid invoice and click on it to open it.
Make sure that
PayFast Secure Credit Card
is selected under
Payment Method
.
Click
Subscribe Using Payfast
and
fill in your details
.
You’ll be redirected to the secure
PayFast portal
.
Enter your
credit card details
and follow the
3D Secure prompts
.
Complete your
first payment
using your credit card details.
Once completed, you’ll receive instant payment confirmation via email.
Your account will be updated immediately, and your services will remain active.
Selecting Your Existing Credit Card Details for Recurring Payment
Once you have subscribed to use your credit card via PayFast, you can pay current or any future invoices as recurring payments, each time you switch to secure credit card by selecting “PayFast Secure Credit Card”, making payments quick and secure, every time!
Simply follow these steps for recurring payments for invoices on different payment methods:
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Click on
Billing → My Invoices
.
Locate the unpaid invoice and click on it to open it.
Select
Recurring Payment
.
You’ll be redirected to the secure
PayFast portal
.
Enter your
credit card details
and follow the
3D Secure prompts
.
Once completed, you’ll receive instant payment confirmation via email.
TIP:
Always double-check that your account has sufficient funds to avoid failed transactions or service suspensions.
Security and Transparency with Credit Card Payments
At 1-grid, your trust and data security are always our top priority. Every credit card transaction made through
PayFast
is fully protected by
PCI DSS Level 1 compliance
, ensuring your payment information stays encrypted and secure at all times.
We only work with verified, reputable payment partners, and all payment data is safely transmitted through encrypted channels to prevent unauthorised access or fraud.
To keep your payments extra secure:
Ensure your card is activated for online purchases before making a payment.
Never share your
OTP, CVV
, or
bank login details
.
Always confirm you’re paying through the official PayFast URL (
https://payment.payfast.io/eng/process/payment/
).
Regularly review your bank statements for any unusual or unauthorised transactions.
If you ever notice anything suspicious, reach out to
1-grid Billing Support
immediately.
By taking these simple precautions, you can enjoy fast, secure, and worry-free online payments every time!
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if:
You’re struggling to switch to make a payment.
Your card payment fails, but funds are deducted.
You’re unsure if the transaction was successful.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Is it safe to pay or update my credit card details through PayFast?
Yes, absolutely. All transactions made through
PayFast
are encrypted and secured with
SSL
and
3D Secure verification
, keeping your details private and protected. In addition, PayFast is
PCI DSS Level 1 compliant
, the highest international standard for payment security, so you can update or make payments with confidence.
Can I use my credit card for a once-off payment?
Yes, you can make a
once-off payment
for any outstanding invoice using your credit card through PayFast. Simply select
PayFast Secure Credit Card
. The payment reflects instantly, ensuring your hosting, email, or domain services stay active without delay.
Can I switch to a credit card for all future invoices?
Definitely! You can easily
update your payment method
in your
Customer Zone
to make credit card your preferred option for all upcoming invoices. Once set, future payments can be processed automatically through PayFast for faster, hassle-free renewals.
What should I do if my credit card payment fails?
If a payment doesn’t go through, first confirm that your card is enabled for online purchases and has sufficient funds available. Next, ensure your bank hasn’t placed any temporary holds or security restrictions. If the issue persists, contact your bank or reach out to
[email protected]
for quick support.
Can I switch to Debit Order instead?
Yes, and in fact,
Debit Order remains the most recommended and convenient option
for long-term payments. It ensures your invoices are paid automatically on time, helping you avoid missed payments or service interruptions. You can switch to Debit Order or any other payment method anytime directly in your
Customer Zone
.
Additional Resources
Debit Orders at 1-grid Means Hassle-Free Payments, Everytime!
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid Mean No More Worries!
Updating Your Payment Method Is Easy at 1-grid!
Need to Know How Often You Are Paying?
Check Out Your Billing Cycle in Your Customer Zone
Find Your Invoices Securely in Your Customer Zone
Got a Payment Query?
Invoicing at 1-grid Keeps You Paying On Time and Online!
No Suspensions Means Always Online with Us at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Making the switch to secure credit card to pay securely via PayFast for your invoice doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: The Benefits of Paying with Secure Credit Card via PayFast
content:
At 1-grid, we know how important it is to keep your online services running smoothly, without interruptions or missed payments. That’s why we believe in the benefits of paying with credit card for your
invoices
.
Paying by
credit card
is one of the easiest and most reliable alternative ways to pay, alongside
debit order
, to stay in control of your renewals. It’s
secure, convenient, and instant
, giving you peace of mind that your hosting, domains, and emails remain active at all times.
This guide explains why paying with a secure credit card via
PayFast
is a great choice for seamless billing, faster service renewal, and uninterrupted business continuity.
Table of Contents
Why Secure Credit Card via PayFast May Be Right for You
Updating Your Payment Method and Paying Your Invoices with Secure Credit Card
Security and Transparency
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Why Secure Credit Card via PayFast May Be Right for You
Paying with a credit card offers several customer-centric advantages designed to make managing your online services effortless and stress-free.
Top Benefits:
Instant Payment Confirmation:
Payments are reflected immediately, so your services remain active without delay or manual follow-up.
Safe and Secure:
All transactions are processed through PayFast, ensuring your payment details are protected through bank-level encryption.
Time-Saving Convenience:
No need for manual EFTs, proof of payment, or waiting for funds to reflect.
Automatic Renewals:
Credit cards support automated billing, so you’ll never miss a renewal again.
Flexible and Accessible:
Accepted 24/7, anywhere, using any device with an internet connection.
Switching to credit card payments not only simplifies your billing; it builds
reliability and financial confidence
. With PayFast’s secure, automated system integration with 1-grid, you’re always in control, ensuring your online presence stays uninterrupted so you can focus on what truly matters: growing your brand!
Updating Your Payment Method and Paying Your Invoices with Secure Credit Card
If you’d like to
switch your billing to credit card payments
, you can easily
update your payment method and credit card details
via your
Customer Zone,
and follow our
easy-to-use guide
on
how to make payments
for
invoices
directly.
We know that business means business, so we have made paying your invoices just as easy as signing that latest deal!
Security and Transparency
We know financial information is sensitive, and your trust matters. That’s why all debit order transactions with 1-grid are
processed securely
through
PCI DSS-compliant payment gateways
, ensuring your data remains safe and encrypted.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if you:
Experience an error while processing your credit card payment.
Notice that a payment hasn’t been reflected after completing checkout.
Want to verify that your card details have been updated successfully.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Is it safe to pay by credit card at 1-grid?
Yes, all payments are processed through PayFast using secure encryption and comply with international PCI DSS standards.
Can I save my credit card for future payments?
Yes, when you select credit card payments, PayFast securely stores your details for future transactions.
Why hasn’t my credit card payment reflected yet?
Most payments are instant, but if your bank takes longer to process, it can take up to a few minutes. If it doesn’t update, contact
[email protected]
.
Can I use a business credit card?
Yes, both personal and business Visa/Mastercard credit cards are accepted.
Can I switch from EFT or
Debit Order
to Credit Card?
Absolutely, yes! You can update your payment method in your Customer Zone anytime.
Additional Resources
Payments at 1-grid Mean Less Stress, More Focus
Invoicing at 1-grid Keeps Your Online and On Time
Avoiding Suspensions is Priority for Business
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding the benefits of paying with credit card for your services doesn’t have to sound complicated with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/

title: View Your 1-grid Invoices via Customer Zone
content:
At 1-grid, we make it simple for you to access your billing information whenever you need it and to view your invoices. Your
invoice
contains all the essential details about your services, from product renewals to payment dates, ensuring transparency and helping you stay on top of your account.
Through your
Customer Zone
, you can view, download, and pay your invoices in just a few clicks.
This guide will walk you through what an
invoice
is and why it matters, how to view your invoices via Customer Zone, and how to
make payments
quickly and securely.
Table of Contents
What Is an Invoice and Why Does This Matter?
How Do I View My Invoice via Customer Zone?
How Do I Pay My Invoice?
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is an Invoice and Why Does This Matter?
An
invoice
is a detailed record of services you’ve purchased or renewed with 1-grid. It shows what you’re being billed for, how much you owe, and when your payment is due.
Invoices are important because they:
Keep your billing organised and transparent.
Help prevent service interruptions by reminding you of upcoming payments.
Serve as official proof of purchase for accounting or business purposes.
How Do I View My Invoice via Customer Zone?
You can view and download any of your invoices directly from your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Follow these simple steps:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3. You’ll see a list of all your invoices, including:
Invoice Number
Invoice Date
Due Date
Total
Status (Paid, Unpaid, Cancelled)
4. Click on an
Invoice line
to open it.
5. Once opened, you can:
View the
invoice details
online
.
Download or print
a copy for your records.
Pay directly
from the invoice page if payment is due.
TIP:
Regularly review invoices to stay up to date with renewals and payments.
How Do I Pay My Invoice?
You can
pay
your invoices instantly via
several secure payment options available
through 1-grid’s billing system. We’ve made keeping your business online easy and stress-free so that you can focus on building your brand!
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if you:
Can’t find a specific invoice in your Customer Zone.
Need help with a failed or delayed payment.
Notice incorrect details on your invoice.
Require an
official statement
or invoice copy for accounting.
FAQs
Can I view invoices for past services?
Yes, all your past and current invoices remain available under
Billing → My Invoices
in your Customer Zone.
Can I download my invoices for my records?
Absolutely, you can simply open your invoice and click
Download PDF
to save it locally.
What if my invoice status shows “Unpaid”?
It means the payment has not yet been reflected or has not yet been received. Please complete payment immediately to avoid service suspension.
My payment was made, but the invoice still shows “Unpaid.” What should I do?
Payments may take time to reflect, depending on your payment method. If it remains unpaid after 24 to 48 hours, contact
[email protected]
for assistance.
Can I receive my invoices via email?
Yes, invoices are automatically emailed to your registered address and are always available in your Customer Zone.
Additional Resources
Making Payments Stress-Free at 1-grid
Invoicing at 1-grid: All You Need to Know
A Guide to Avoiding Suspensions at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Viewing your 1-grid invoices via Customer Zone doesn’t have to feel like a maze with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/

title: Billing Cycles at 1-grid: A Guide
content:
We believe in keeping your billing experience simple, transparent, and stress-free. That’s why we offer different billing cycles at 1-grid and make it easy for you to find the right one that works for you.
Your
billing cycle
determines when you’re invoiced and how often you’re charged for your hosting, domains, and other services.
Understanding your billing cycle helps you plan your payments, avoid unexpected charges, and ensure that your services remain active without interruption.
This guide will explain what a billing cycle is, what options are available, and how to view yours directly through your
Customer Zone
.
Table of Contents
What Is a Billing Cycle?
What Billing Cycles Can I Choose?
How Do I Check My Billing Cycle?
How Can I Change My Billing Cycle?
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Billing Cycle?
A
billing cycle
is the recurring period between two invoice dates for your 1-grid products or services.
It defines how often you are billed and when payments are due.
For example:
Monthly cycle:
You’re billed every month for your services.
Annual cycle:
You’re billed once per year, usually at a discounted rate compared to monthly payments.
Your billing cycle starts on your account’s activation or renewal date and repeats according to your selected plan.
What Billing Cycles Can I Choose?
At 1-grid, we offer flexible billing cycles to suit your budget and business needs:
Billing Cycle
Description
Ideal For
Monthly
Billed once a month. Easier to manage cash flow with smaller, more frequent payments.
Startups, freelancers, or short-term projects.
Quarterly
Billed every 3 months.
Small businesses seeking short-term commitment.
Semi-Annually (6 months)
Billed twice per year.
Customers who prefer moderate billing frequency.
Annually
Billed once per year. Usually, it includes savings compared to shorter cycles.
Established businesses that prefer convenience and fewer transactions. And typically for domains.
Biennially (Every 2 years)
Billed every two years. Offers the most cost-effective option with the least frequent billing.
Long-term customers who are looking for stability and savings.
Triennially (Every 3 years)
Billed every three years. Offers the most cost-effective option with the least frequent billing.
Long-term customers who are looking for extra stability and savings.
TIP:
Review your billing cycle to align with their budget and renewal strategy.
How Do I Check My Billing Cycle?
You can view your current billing cycle anytime in your
Customer Zone
.
Steps to Check Your Billing Cycle:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2. Navigate to
Your Active Products/Services
, and then select
View Details
next to the product or service.
4. Under
Information
, you’ll see:
Registration Date
Recurring Amount
Billing Cycle
(Monthly, Quarterly, Annually, etc.)
Next Due Date
Payment Method
TIP:
If you’re unsure which cycle you selected,
viewing your latest invoice
under
Billing →
Invoices
also confirms your billing schedule.
How Can I Change My Billing Cycle?
If you’d like to change how often you’re billed, our
Billing Team
can assist you in switching between available cycles, depending on the service you would like to change.
Important:
The change may involve a
pro-rata adjustment
to align billing dates correctly.
Once the change is approved, your future invoices will reflect the new cycle.
Domain renewals
cannot be placed on a monthly cycle due to registry policies; only hosting and related services can.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if you:
Want to switch from monthly to annual billing (or vice versa).
See a mismatch between your invoices and the billing cycle.
Have questions about how billing cycles at 1-grid affect your renewals.
Need clarity about pro-rata adjustments or payments in progress.
FAQs
Can I change my billing cycle anytime?
Yes, you can request a change at any time. However, adjustments are processed manually and may include a pro-rata billing update.
Will changing my billing cycle affect my current services?
No, your services remain active while your billing cycle change is processed.
Can I view my billing cycle on my invoices?
Yes, each invoice includes your
billing frequency
and
next due date
in the summary section.
Is there a benefit to choosing an annual billing cycle?
Absolutely, annual billing often includes
discounts
, fewer transactions, and less administrative effort.
What happens if I miss a payment in my cycle?
You’ll receive automated payment reminders before the due date. Services may be suspended if unpaid, but reactivation is quick once payment reflects.
Additional Resources
1-grid Payments: All You Need to Never Miss a Payment
All You Need to Know About Invoicing at 1-grid
Suspensions: Guides to Keep You Online
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Viewing or updating your billing cycle doesn’t have to feel like a challenge with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Updating Your Payment Method via Your 1-grid Customer Zone
content:
At 1-grid, we want managing your account to feel simple, seamless, and stress-free and paying your
invoices
on time. That’s why we give you the flexibility to
choose
and update your preferred payment method directly through your Customer Zone
, all in just a few clicks.
Whether you’re switching from
Direct EFT
to
Credit Card
for easier billing or updating your account details for Debit Order for automatic renewals.
This guide walks you through everything you need to know, where we’ll explain what payment methods are available, and how to update your preferred payment method.
Table of Contents
What Is a Payment Method?
What Payment Methods Can I Choose From?
How Do I Update My Payment Method via Customer Zone?
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Payment Method?
A
payment method
is the way you choose to pay for your hosting, domains, and other 1-grid services.
Your selected payment method determines how your invoices are billed, processed, and renewed.
Payment methods help ensure that your services remain
active without interruption
by offering both
automated and manual options
for convenience and flexibility.
TIP:
Our easy-to-use payment methods make staying in control of your billing one less thing to worry about!
What Payment Methods Can I Choose From?
1-grid offers
easy, secure, and flexible payment methods
to suit every customer’s preference.
Here’s how each option makes things simpler for you so you can take care of your business!
1.
Debit Order
(Highly Recommended)
Making billing reliable and hands-free! We do the admin; you do your business!
2.
Secure Credit Card via PayFast
Making payments fast, one-time or recurring, while you focus on your business matters!
3.
Instant EFT via PayFast
Making traditional bank payments simpler with instant confirmation, so you can get back to business quickly.
4.
SnapScan
Making on-the-go payments convenient so your business meetings run on time.
5.
Direct EFT
(Manual Transfer)
Making manual control of payments better, while managing the books traditionally.
How Do I Update My Payment Method via Customer Zone?
Follow these steps to update or change your payment method securely through your
1-grid Customer Zone
:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3. Select
your preferred option
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4. Click on
Save Changes
.
TIP:
If you’re switching to
Debit Order
, update your details
at least 7 days before
your next billing date to ensure smooth processing.
If you update your payment method after an invoice has already been issued, the invoice remains valid and will be processed using the new payment method at the next cycle, except for Debit Order (if this is made later than at least 7 days before the due date).
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if you:
Experience issues saving or updating your payment method.
Want to confirm if your update has taken effect.
Have recently switched to Debit Order and want to verify your first debit date.
FAQs
If I change my payment method after an invoice is issued, will I get a new invoice?
No, the existing invoice remains valid and can still be paid with your new method before the due date.
How soon will my new payment method take effect?
Immediately, unless it’s a Debit Order, which must be updated at least 7 days before the debit date.
What happens if my saved payment method fails?
You’ll receive an automatic reminder to update your details or use an alternate method before the due date.
Is it safe to store my payment details on 1-grid?
Absolutely, al payment processing is handled through
PayFast’s secure, PCI DSS-compliant gateway
to ensure complete data protection.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need for 1-grid Payments
A Guide to Invoices at 1-grid, and When to Expect Yours
All You Need to Know About Suspensions at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Updating your payment method via your Customer Zone doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: How To Keep Your 1-grid Domain Contact Details Up to Date
content:
At 1-grid, we know your domain is more than a name; it’s your online identity. That’s why keeping your
domain contact information up to date
is essential.
Your contact details help verify domain ownership, ensure you receive important
renewal
and verification notices, and maintain compliance with
ICANN
’s global domain registration requirements
.
This guide explains everything you need to know about updating your domain’s contact details, how ICANN and ZARC verification works, and how to stay compliant and protected.
Table of Contents
Understanding WHOIS Records and Domain Contacts
Registry
Registrar
Registrant
Domain Contact Details on WHOIS
What is Domain Contact Information?
What Is ICANN Verification and Why Does It Matter?
Understanding RDDS (WHOIS) Verification Requirements
Understanding ZARC Verification Requirements
What Is a Change of Registrant and How It Works
Why Keeping Contact Information Updated Matters
How to Update Domain Owner Contact Details (via Customer Zone)
When to Contact 1-grid Domains Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Understanding WHOIS Records and Domain Contacts
Keeping your domain contact information up to date is essential for managing your website, emails, and legal ownership.
Here’s a simple overview of the
key roles
listed on a domain’s WHOIS record:
Registry
The registry manages the top-level domain (TLD) itself. They set rules, manage registrations, and generate the zone file. Some registries manage multiple TLDs (like ZARC for .co.za and .org.za), while others manage just one (like SIDN for .nl).
Registrar
The registrar acts as the bridge between you and the registry. They are accredited to register domains on your behalf. At 1-grid, we serve as the registrar for several TLDs, including .online, .co.za, and .africa.
Registrant
The registrant is the domain owner; the person or organisation who has the right to use the domain for their website and email, provided the domain fees are paid. If you host your domain with 1-grid, you are the registrant.
Domain Contact Details on WHOIS
WHOIS records list key contacts associated with your domain:
Registrant Contact:
The legal owner of the domain.
Administrative (Admin) Contact:
Handles administrative matters and authorisations.
Technical (Tech) Contact:
Responsible for technical operations, such as DNS settings.
Billing Contact:
Receives invoices and manages payment for the domain.
Keeping these contacts accurate ensures uninterrupted service, smooth communication, and quick resolution of any domain-related issues.
What is Domain Contact Information?
Your
domain contact information
includes your
name, organisation, email address, phone number, and physical address
linked to your domain registration.
It’s used by domain registries and registrars (like 1-grid) to:
Verify domain ownership.
Contact you regarding renewals, verifications, or updates.
Display limited information publicly via the
RDDS (Registration Data Directory Service)
,
ZARC
or
WHOIS lookup
(depending on privacy settings).
While some of the information is hidden for privacy, the information remains stored on the WHOIS servers for domain information accuracy.
Keeping this information accurate ensures you remain in full control of your domain and that you receive important notices on time.
What Is ICANN Verification and Why Does It Matter?
The
Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN)
require all accredited registrars, including 1-grid, to verify the accuracy of registrant email addresses. This can include verification from ZARC for .za extensions.
You may receive a
Verification Email
when:
You
register a new domain
.
You
transfer a domain to 1-grid
.
You update your domain’s email address or contact information.
You must verify your email address by clicking the link in that email.
If you don’t verify within
15 days
, your domain may be
suspended
until verification is complete.
IMPORTANT:
Verification protects your ownership, not restricts it. Think of it as a safeguard for your domain identity.
Understanding RDDS (WHOIS) Verification Requirements
The
RDDS (Registration Data Directory Service)
, formerly known as WHOIS, provides public records of domain ownership and contact information.
By law, all registrars must maintain accurate and verified information.
ICANN’s
WHOIS Data Reminder Policy (WDRP)
requires registrars like 1-grid to:
Send annual reminders to confirm your contact details.
Promptly update records when changes are made.
If the information is inaccurate or unverifiable, domains may be
suspended or removed from the registry
until corrected.
Understanding ZARC Verification Requirements
ZARC (ZA Central Consortium), is a South African organisation that manages internet domain names for the country, including .za second-level domains like .co.za and .org.za, as well as geographical top-level domains for cities like .capetown, .joburg, and .durban.
ZARC domain name verification includes:
Domain Transfer:
When transferring a domain, you’ll receive an email from ZARC requesting that you accept the transfer, which you confirm by clicking a link and a button on the voting page.
Registrar Accreditation:
ZARC also conducts its own verification process for organisations that apply to be ICANN-accredited registrars.
Domain Registration:
Verification is part of the standard process for registering a .za domain, managed through a registrar. For example, if you want to transfer a domain, you must complete the process by verifying the transfer request.
Technical Verification:
ZARC is responsible for the technical verification of domain names and uses the EPP protocols
(Extensible Provisioning Protocol)
for domain management, ensuring it aligns with international standards.
What Is a Change of Registrant and How It Works
A
Change of Registrant
occurs when ‘ownership’ details (such as the name, company, or email address) of a domain are updated.
ICANN requires verification for any significant changes to ensure the rightful owner authorises them.
When this happens:
Both the
current
and
new registrant
receive an email.
Both must approve the change via the verification links.
The change only takes effect once both parties confirm.
NOTE:
If not confirmed within
7 days
, the request expires, and no changes are made.
IMPORTANT:
This process helps protect your domain from unauthorised transfers or hijacking.
Why Keeping Contact Information Updated Matters
Keeping your contact information accurate ensures that:
You retain
full ownership and control
of your domain.
You receive
important renewal, verification, and policy update notifications
.
You comply with
ICANN / ZARC requirements
.
You avoid
suspension or domain loss
due to unverified details.
Keeping contact details current = smooth service, continuous ownership, and no surprises.
How to Update Domain Owner Contact Details (via Customer Zone)
Domain contacts (owner, admin, and tech) can be the same or different for alternative users. Make sure their email addresses are accurate and avoid using email addresses associated with your domain (e.g.,
[email protected]
). We can recommend that you use a reliable third-party email like Gmail.
When
registering a new domain
or
transferring one to 1-grid
, we default to using your 1-grid primary Account Profile details.
After registration, you can update this information from your
Domains
dashboard in your 1-grid Customer Zone.
Step-by-Step Guide:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2. Navigate to
Domains → My Domains
.
3. Select the domain you want to update.
4. Click
Edit Contact Information
.
5. Update the relevant details (name, organisation, email, phone, address).
6. Click
Save Changes
.
7. Watch for a
verification email
from 1-grid or ICANN/ZARC, then open it and click the link to verify.
Once verified, your contact records are updated globally.
*A 60-day transfer lock may be applied; however, you can confirm the status of this via
ZARC WHOIS
or
ICANN Lookup
.
IMPORTANT:
Double-check your email inbox (and spam folder) for verification links to avoid unnecessary suspensions.
NOTE:
Domain contact information updates take up to 5 working days to take effect for .co.za domains. During this time, you would not be able to make any other changes to the domain, such as
nameserver updates
.  For any other domain, such as .com, you will receive a verification email to accept the changes made. If the information is not verified, the domain will be suspended, which will impact both emails and websites.
When to Contact 1-grid Domains Support
Contact our Team if:
You haven’t received your verification email after 24 hours.
Your verification link has expired.
You cannot access your domain management area.
You believe your contact details are incorrect, but locked from editing.
TIP:
Submit a
Support Ticket
or email us at
[email protected]
.
FAQs
There’s a notification on my domain that I need to verify, but I haven’t received the email. What should I do?
Check your spam or junk mail folder. If it’s not there, contact 1-grid Support to request a new verification email.
I clicked on the verification link, but it’s expired. What does that mean?
Verification links expire after a short time for security reasons. You’ll need to request a new link by contacting 1-grid Support.
How often do I need to verify my details?
Once verified, your contact information only needs re-verification if it changes (such as your email or organisation name).
What happens if I don’t verify my domain contact information?
Your domain may be temporarily suspended until verification is complete, and your website or email may stop working.
Can I update my contact details anytime?
Yes, you can update them anytime in your Customer Zone, but major ownership changes require ICANN verification.
Additional Resources
Understanding Domain Renewal Invoices at 1-grid
Reasons Why Your Domain Transfer Could Fail
Domain Auto-Renewal Statuses: A Guide
Domains: Renewal Process for Local and International Domains
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Updating your domain contact information or understanding the verification process doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Services Still Suspended After Making Payment
content:
At 1-grid, we understand that keeping your services active and running smoothly is essential to your business and online presence. Sometimes, finding your services still
suspended
after
making payment
can be both confusing and frustrating, and we want to make sure you get clarity and have confidence that this can be resolved quickly.
This guide explains
why
suspensions
occur
,
what might delay
automatic reactivation
, and
what you can do to
get your services back online
quickly
.
Table of Contents
What Is Suspension Due to Non-Payment?
Possible Reasons Why Your Service Is Still Suspended After Making a Payment
The Payment Hasn’t Cleared Yet
The Payment Was Made AfterSuspension Took Effect
The Payment Was Not Linked to the Correct Invoice or Account
The Payment Was Made from an International Account
Reactivation Requires Manual Approval
Outstanding Balances or Partial Payments
How Reactivation Works at 1-grid
Paying on Time and Its Benefits
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is Suspension Due to Non-Payment?
A
service suspension
occurs when an account or service (such as hosting, domain, or email) remains unpaid after the due date.
When
invoices
go unpaid for a certain period, the system automatically suspends the service to prevent continued usage without payment.
Suspension temporarily restricts access but
does not delete your data, as long as payment is made for the full outstanding balance
within 60 to 90 days
of the latest outstanding invoice due date
. Once payment is made and verified, services can be
reinstated
.
Possible Reasons Why Your Service Is Still Suspended After Making a Payment
Even after payment, there can be several reasons why your service still shows as
suspended
.
Here’s what could be happening, and what you can do:
The Payment Hasn’t Cleared Yet
Payments made via
Direct EFT
can take
2 to 3 working days
to reflect, depending on your bank’s processing time.
During this time, your service remains suspended until payment is fully allocated.
TIP:
If you paid via
PayFast
,
SnapScan
, or
Instant EFT
, reactivation should happen almost immediately after confirmation.
The Payment Was Made After
Suspension Took Effect
If the suspension has already taken place before your payment cleared, your service will need to be
manually reactivated
by our Billing Team.
This helps us confirm that the payment has been allocated correctly and that your account is in good standing.
NOTE:
This process is quick once confirmation is received.
The Payment Was Not Linked to the Correct Invoice or Account
If the payment reference used was incorrect or missing, it may not have been automatically matched to your account.
This can delay reactivation because the system cannot identify which service the payment applies to.
TIP: Always use your
Customer ID
as the payment reference.
The Payment Was Made from an International Account
Cross-border or foreign currency payments may take longer to clear and verify.
If you made an
international EFT
, it may take up to
10 business days
for your service to reflect as paid and to be reactivated.
Reactivation Requires Manual Approval
Certain suspended services (like hosting accounts or domain renewals) require manual verification by our Technical or Domains teams.
This ensures that the system reactivates your services safely without data loss.
Outstanding Balances or Partial Payments
If there are
older unpaid invoices
, the system automatically allocates your payment to the
oldest balance first
, especially when making payment via
Direct EFT
.
Your most recent invoice will remain unpaid until all older amounts are settled, or the full balance (where a partial payment is made to an invoice) is made, keeping the suspension active.
IMPORTANT:
Review your full invoice list
in the
Customer Zone → Billing → My Invoices
section to ensure all outstanding amounts are cleared.
How Reactivation Works at 1-grid
Once payment has been made, you can confirm the status via your invoice:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3. Confirm that the invoice shows as
Paid
.
4. If it does, wait 15 to 30 minutes and then check your service again.
5. If the issue persists, contact the
Billing Team
with proof of payment.
Proof of Payment details:
Include your invoice number, amount, date, and payment reference used to help us locate your payment quickly.
Paying on Time and Its Benefits
Paying your invoices before the due date ensures:
Your services stay active and uninterrupted.
You avoid
suspension fees
or
reactivation delays
.
You maintain a strong
account standing
with faster renewals and billing processes.
You don’t risk data loss or
domain expiry
.
TIP:
Proactive payment supports business continuity and peace of mind.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
We understand that your business is a priority, which is why we offer
debit order
as the
most recommended method for recurring services like hosting, domain renewals, and business email accounts, so that you don’t have to worry about the small things!
It’s more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building trust, reliability, and financial confidence.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if:
Your payment has reflected on your bank statement, but your service remains suspended.
You’ve made a payment via EFT more than
3 working days ago
.
You accidentally used the wrong payment reference or transferred from a different account.
You’ve settled all invoices, but your hosting or domain is still suspended.
TIP:
Submit a
Support Ticket
or email us at
[email protected]
.
FAQs
I used the wrong reference when making a payment. How can I fix it?
Send your proof of payment to
[email protected]
, and our team will manually allocate it to the correct account.
Will my service be deleted during suspension?
No, suspended services are inactive but not deleted as long as reactivation is done within 60to 90 days of the outstanding invoice due date, so until then, your data remains safe.
Do I need to pay a reactivation fee?
Yes, a R50.00 reactivation fee will apply for reactivation before your service is restored.
Can I avoid this from happening again?
Yes, this can definitely be avoided. We recommend that you set up a
debit order
,
credit card
, or use
PayFast Instant EFT
for automatic and instant payments to prevent future suspensions.
Additional Resources
Invoicing at 1-grid and What Makes This Important for You
Everything You Need for 1-grid Payments
All You Need to Know About Suspensions at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Wondering why your services are still suspended after making payments doesn’t have to cause you further worry with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Requesting Your 1-grid Account Statement
content:
At 1-grid, we believe managing your
hosting
,
email
, or
domain
services should be simple and transparent. This is why it matters to us to make requesting your 1-grid
account statement
easy, giving you a clear view of your billing history, payments received, and any outstanding balances, all in one place.
By requesting your latest statement, you can
stay informed
,
track payments
, and
keep your account up to date
, all without having to worry about the status of your account.
This guide will provide you with information on
what an account statement is
,
why these matter
and
how you can request yours
.
Table of Contents
What Is an Account Statement?
Why Are Account Statements Important?
Track Your Payment History
Manage Business Finances
Ensure Transparency
Simplify Communication
How to Request Your 1-grid Account Statement
Security and Transparency
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is an Account Statement?
An
account statement
is a summary of all billing activities on your 1-grid account for a specific period. It includes:
Invoices issued and paid.
Outstanding balances.
Credits, debits, and refunds applied.
Payment references and dates.
In short, it’s your
financial snapshot
, helping you keep track of your hosting, email and domain expenses in one convenient document.
Why Are Account Statements Important?
Account statements are an essential part of transparent billing and
record-keeping
.
They help you:
Track Your Payment History
See which invoices are paid, pending, or overdue.
Manage Business Finances
Use statements for accounting or reconciliation.
Ensure Transparency
Always know what’s been billed and when.
Simplify Communication
Easily share statements with your Finance team or Bookkeeper.
How to Request Your 1-grid Account Statement
At 1-grid, it is our priority to ensure that the information reflected in your Account Statement is accurate. This means that our Team diligently downloads this for you, checks it, and then sends it to you via your primary registered email address whenever you request this.
Here’s how it works:
Statement Request received via email at
[email protected]
or via phone.
Include:
Period required
Our Team will manually reconcile this for you and send this to you via email.
This ensures accurate reporting of your payment history on your 1-grid account and the
security
of your information.
Security and Transparency
At 1-grid, your data security is our priority. This means that statements can only be requested by authorised account holders.
You can trust that your financial data is:
Protected from
unauthorised access
.
Accessible only to the right people.
Always up to date
with real-time transaction information.
IMPORTANT: If you need your statement sent to a different contact or email address, you can
invite a new user
to your account and add them as a sub-account or
add a technical/support contact
to allow them different levels of access to your account and support on your behalf. This keeps your account secure while giving the right people access to logins, support, account functions, technical functions and management.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team with a follow-up if:
Notice discrepancies between your statement and invoices.
Can’t access or download your statement from the email you received from them.
Need assistance understanding your billing activity.
FAQs
How often are statements updated?
Your account statement updates automatically with each payment, refund, or new invoice.
Can I request older statements?
Yes, you can request older, past statements. They may take a little longer to consolidate, so please allow our Team some time to process these for you.
What’s the difference between an invoice and a statement?
An
invoice
is a request for payment for a specific service. A
statement
is a summary of all transactions (invoices, payments, and credits) over time.
What happens if I find discrepancies?
Our Billing Team will reach out to our Finance Team to have them generate a more in-depth statement for additional review and consolidation to be done before sending this to you.
