This, however, means some additional time would be needed, and it is important to be specific when highlighting your concerns to our Team.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Payments at 1-grid
Suspensions at 1-grid, and How to Deal with These
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Requesting and understanding your 1-grid Account Statement doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Understanding Domain Renewal Invoices at 1-grid
content:
At 1-grid, we understand how vital your
domain
is to your business identity and online presence. To ensure your domain never expires or becomes vulnerable to loss, we send
domain renewal invoices 60 days in advance
. This means understanding invoicing for domain renewals is an integral part of making sure your brand stays online.
This proactive approach gives you
ample time to review, budget, and renew
so you can stay focused on your business while we keep your domain secure.
This guide will give you insight into invoicing for your domain, why this matters, and how you can keep your domain secure with proactive renewal.
Table of Contents
What Is a Domain Renewal Invoice?
Why Are Domain Renewal Invoices Important?
How Domain Renewal Invoicing Works at 1-grid
Benefits of Invoicing in Advance
Our Invoicing Process and Reminder Timelines
Standard Renewal Invoicing and Reminder Process
Why This Matters
Where Do Invoices Go?
How Can I View My Invoice?
How To Pay Your Domain Renewal Invoice
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Domain Renewal Invoice?
A
domain renewal invoice
is a billing notice that lets you know your domain name is nearing its renewal date when you have
enabled auto-renew
for your domain. So, we’ve set processes in place to ensure that your domain does not expire and try to save you the cost of paying an additional rescue and/or
redemption fee
. For this reason, our system sends out
domain renewal invoices 60 days in advance
.
It includes key details such as:
The
domain name
and
renewal
period
(typically one year).
The
invoice amount
and
due date
.
Clear payment options (
Debit Order
,
Direct EFT
,
Credit Card
,
SnapScan
,
PayFast
, etc.).
These invoices ensure that your ownership of the domain continues seamlessly.
Why Are Domain Renewal Invoices Important?
Your domain name is your online identity. If it expires, your
hosting
,
email
, or
domain
services can be disrupted, and your domain could be made available for resale.
Early invoicing prevents that risk by ensuring:
You’re notified well before
renewal deadlines
.
You have enough time to update your payment method or billing details.
Your domain remains active and protected without interruption.
How Domain Renewal Invoicing Works at 1-grid
At 1-grid, domain invoices are automatically generated
60 days before the domain’s expiry date for domains
that have
auto-renew en
abled
.
Here’s briefly
how it works
:
Invoice Issued:
60 days before renewal, your invoice is created and sent to your registered email address.
Reminders Sent:
Follow-up reminders are sent closer to the due date.
Auto-Renew (if applicable):
If you’ve enabled Debit Order or Auto Renew, the payment is processed automatically.
Manual Renewal:
If you prefer manual payment, you can renew at any time before its expiry via the Customer Zone, once your invoice has been issued.
This system ensures uninterrupted ownership, even if billing or card details change during the renewal window.
Benefits of Invoicing in Advance
Sending renewal invoices in advance offers several customer benefits:
Prevents domain loss:
Protects you from accidental expiry.
Gives you flexibility:
Allows time for financial planning or bulk renewal management.
Improves transparency:
You know exactly when and how renewals are billed.
Supports proactive service:
You stay in control, not caught off guard by last-minute renewals.
This proactive approach gives you full control over your payments, renewals, and avoids last-minute billing stress, giving you one less thing to worry about. It also saves your domain from going into
redemption
.
IMPORTANT: Failure to settle domain
renewal
fees in time may result in a
redemption fee
of R500 to R5000, dependent on the applicable Registry.
Our Invoicing Process and Reminder Timelines
At 1-grid, we’re committed to helping you manage your domain with confidence and ease. To keep your domain and services active and uninterrupted, we follow a
consistent, transparent reminder and invoicing process
supported by automated reminders, giving you plenty of time to review, plan, and pay before any action is required.
Our invoicing and reminder system ensures you’re always informed about upcoming payments and due dates, reducing the risk of missed invoices or unexpected expiry or service interruptions.
Standard Renewal Invoicing and Reminder Process
Here’s how our renewal cycle works:
90 days
prior to the expiration, a domain renewal notice is sent via email.
60 days
prior to the expiration date, an invoice is generated.
45 days
prior to the expiration date, the invoice will be due.
30 days
prior to the expiration date, a domain renewal notice will be sent via email.
7 days
prior to the expiration, a final domain renewal notice will be sent via email.
5 days
after the domain has expired, a notice will be sent that explains the domain has been deleted.
Why This Matters
By following a clear, predictable invoicing and reminder process, we help you:
Plan:
You’ll always know when to expect your next invoice.
Stay informed:
Multiple reminders ensure you never miss a due date.
Avoid interruptions:
Early notice gives you time to act before any suspension.
Manage services easily:
Log in anytime to view or pay domain renewal invoices directly via the
Customer Zone
.
Where Do Invoices Go?
All invoices are automatically:
Emailed
to the
primary contact email address
on your 1-grid account.
Available
anytime in your
Customer Zone
under
Billing →
My Invoices
.
If you can’t find an invoice in your inbox, check your
spam or trash folder
, or log in to the
Customer Zone
to
view these
for instant access.
TIP:
You can also
add a Sub-Account to your 1-grid account
as an alternate email address from the primary contact to receive billing information and invoices, such as when you need your Accounting or Finance Team to receive and attend to these on your behalf.
How Can I View My Invoice?
It matters to us that you spend less time worrying about when your next payment is due or struggling to find your invoice, so we’ve got you covered with
this guide
to make it easy for you to view or download your invoice directly from your
Customer Zone
.
How To Pay Your Domain Renewal Invoice
You can easily pay your renewal invoice in one of
several secure ways
:
Debit Order
(recommended):
Automatic, secure, and ensure continuous service.
PayFast (
Credit Card
/
Instant EFT
/
SnapScan
):
Fast and real-time.
Direct EFT
:
Transfer funds directly from your bank.
Account Credit
:
Use available credit for automatic renewal.
TIP:
Log in to your
Customer Zone → Billing → My Invoices
to view, download, and pay your renewal invoice instantly, as long as it is available. Alternatively,
keep credit
on your account to ensure that it automatically applies when your invoice for your domain renewal is due.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if you:
Didn’t receive your renewal invoice.
Recently updated your
domain contact
or payment information.
Need help confirming your renewal date or payment.
FAQs
Why do I get domain invoices earlier than my hosting invoices?
Domains have fixed registry renewal cycles. Sending invoices earlier ensures no risk of losing your domain due to late payment or registry delays.
What happens if I don’t pay my domain renewal invoice on time?
The domain may enter a grace or redemption period, during which recovery fees may apply. It’s best to renew before the expiry date.
Can I enable auto-renewal for my domain?
Absolutely!
Enabling Auto-Renew
ensures a seamless renewal without manual intervention, so reach out to our Billing Tea,m and they will be happy to assist with this, if it is not automatically on.
Additional Resources
Domain Renewals Guides at 1-grid
Avoiding Domain Redemptions with 1-grid
Making Payments at 1-grid
Everything You Need to Know About Invoicing at 1-grid
Everything You Need to Know About Reactivating Suspended or Cancelled Services
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding your domain invoices at 1-grid doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: What is a Service Reactivation Fee and When is This Charged?
content:
At 1-grid, we know that maintaining continuous access to your hosting, domains, and email is crucial for your business and online presence. However, when services are
suspended due to non-payment or prolonged inactivity
, restoring them may involve administrative and technical work; this is where a
Service Reactivation Fee
applies.
This guide will help you understand
what the fee covers
,
why it’s necessary
,
how to pay it
, and
how you can prevent service interruptions in the future
.
Table of Contents
What is a Service Reactivation Fee?
Why Do I Have to Pay a Reactivation Fee for Non-Payment?
Importance of Paying Services Timely
How Do I Pay a Reactivation Fee?
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What is a Service Reactivation Fee?
A
Service Reactivation Fee
is a small charge applied when your
hosting
,
email
, or
domain
service has been
suspended
and requires manual restoration.
This fee covers the
administrative, billing, and potentially any technical work
needed to:
Safely restore your services and files.
Reconnect your hosting and email accounts to the server.
Verify and re-enable your databases.
Ensure your data integrity and system security.
The system generates the
R50.00 standard admin service reactivation fee
upon receiving late payment for your outstanding invoice, as part of the recovery process.
IMPORTANT:
Sometimes, though, when a more technical restoration is required, like when your services have been
suspended or cancelled for more than 60 days
, and we can recover your data and services, the fee may be higher due to the manual restoration process.
Why Do I Have to Pay a Reactivation Fee for Non-Payment?
At 1-grid, we strive to provide the best possible service to you. We offer services with different billing cycles so that you can choose the one that best suits your business needs.
We also send invoices over 14 days (7 days before the due date and 7 days after the due date) to allow you to pay.
In addition to that, we send a 48-hour notice prior to
suspension
to notify you beforehand.
Once services have been suspended, we charge a fee for the administration costs of reactivating your services.
Importance of Paying Services Timely
Paying
invoices
on time ensures your services remain active and uninterrupted.
Timely payment:
Prevents suspension and reactivation fees.
Protects your website data and email continuity.
Keeps your
domain ownership
secure.
Reduces administrative delays.
TIP:
Switch to
Debit Order
for automatic payments; it’s the easiest way to avoid service interruptions and reactivation costs.
How Do I Pay a Reactivation Fee?
Paying your reactivation fee is quick and secure through your
1-grid Customer Zone:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3.
Locate
the
Service Reactivation Fee
invoice (typically the total will reflect as R50.00 for standard non-payment suspensions) and
click
on it to
open it
.
4. Select your
preferred payment method
and follow the prompts to
Pay Now
.
Debit Order
(recommended)
Secure Credit Card via PayFast
Instant EFT via PayFast
SnapScan
Direct EFT
NOTE:
Reactivation takes place within a few hours once the payment has reflected.
IMPORTANT
:
Direct EFT
payments
may take up to 2 to 3 working days
, depending on your bank.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
If you’ve
paid but your service is not yet reactivated
after 2 to 4 hours.
If you’re unsure whether your payment has been reflected.
If you believe your suspension or fee was applied in error.
If you’d like to discuss
reactivation fees
or
product upgrades
.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
The Invoice # in question.
Impacted product or service.
FAQs
What happens if I don’t pay the reactivation fee?
Your services will remain suspended until the outstanding amount (invoice + reactivation fee) is paid.
Can I avoid this fee in the future?
Yes, you can avoid this by enabling
Debit Order
or paying your invoices before the due date to prevent suspension.
Is the reactivation fee refundable?
No, the fee is non-refundable as it covers the administrative process already completed to restore your service.
Additional Resources
Updating Your Payment Method via Your Customer Zone
Benefits of Paying by Debit Order
Payment FAQs
Everything You Need to Know About Domains at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Receiving a Service Reactivation Fee invoice and understanding why you were charged one does not have to be confusing with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Reactivating Suspended or Cancelled Hosting Services at 1-grid
content:
We understand that an uninterrupted online presence is crucial to your business and brand. This means we know how important it is to have the option of
reactivating your
suspended
or cancelled services
at 1-grid.
This guide will help you understand
what hosting suspension and cancellation mean
,
how to reactivate your services quickly
, and
how to stay ahead
of billing cycles to
avoid downtime
in the future.
Table of Contents
What is a Suspension?
What is a Cancellation?
The Benefits of Paying Your Invoices On Time
What Hosting Services Can Be Reactivated?
How to Reactivate Services Suspended Due to Non-Payment
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Need Additional Support?
What is a Suspension?
A
suspension
occurs when your hosting account or related service (
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
) is temporarily disabled due to an
unpaid
invoice
,
overdue
renewal
, or a
security-related issue
(e.g.,
malware
,
phishing activity
, or
overuse of resources
).
The great news is that we can reverse the suspension once you complete the payment or required action.
What is a Cancellation?
A
cancellation
means your services have automatically gone into cancellation due to non-payment, while in suspension, or you have manually requested to cancel your services. This means that the service may enter into a terminated status if the
60 to 90-day suspension/cancellation period has passed
, and the product or service is about to, or has reached the end of the retention period. This means that the data may no longer be recoverable.
This is why it is important to request reactivation for either a service in “Suspended” status or “Cancellation” status
within the limited time window of 60 to 90 days
.
The Benefits of Paying Your Invoices On Time
Proactive payments prevent downtime and reinforce reliability:
Avoids service suspension
and the inconvenience of reactivation delays.
Keeps your website and email live
, maintaining your online credibility.
Prevents additional costs
, such as reactivation fees or reconnection delays.
Builds trust and consistency
in your business operations.
Ensures smooth renewals
for connected services (domains, email, SSL).
TIP:
Paying your 1-grid services by
debit order
is more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
By automating your payments, you save time, reduce administrative effort, and gain peace of mind knowing that your hosting, email, and domains stay up and running without interruptions.
What Hosting Services Can Be Reactivated?
You can reactivate most
hosting
products
(
Web Hosting
,
Email Hosting
,
WordPress Hosting
,
VPS
) if:
The outstanding balance is settled in full.
The service hasn’t been permanently deleted or replaced.
The account is still within its
reactivation window of 60 to 90 days
.
It is important to note the following when considering reviewing the possibility of reactivating your services with 1-grid:
Managed
and
Self-Managed
Servers (
VPS
or
Dedi
) require manual reactivation, which takes longer.
For previously cancelled services, an invoice would not exist. Our Billing Department manually generates the invoice.
IMPORTANT:
If you need to reactivate a service beyond 90 days, you will need a new setup or restoration because the data will no longer be available on our servers.
How to Reactivate Services Suspended Due to Non-Payment
When reactivating your services with 1-grid, it is important to remember a few important things regarding
suspensions
and
fees
involved in the service reactivation.
To make paying your outstanding invoices easy, we have
quick guides
for the payment method that suits you, making it easy and stress-free.
But here are some quick steps, and information:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2. Go to
Billing > My Invoices
.
3. Locate your
unpaid invoice
for the suspended service.
4. Choose your
preferred payment method
, and follow the prompts or steps for the method selected, then click
Pay Now
.
IMPORTANT:
Once payment clears, the system will automatically process the reactivation.
Reactivation for suspensions may take a few hours after payment confirmation.
For cancelled services, contact
Billing Support
via a
Customer Zone ticket
or directly via email at
[email protected]
to request manual reactivation.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
If you’ve paid but your service has not yet been reactivated after 2 hours.
If your hosting product was cancelled, and you need to confirm eligibility for reactivation.
If you believe your account was suspended by mistake or due to fraud prevention.
If you need assistance restoring files or backups from a cancelled service, where available.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
REMEMBER:
When you make a payment via
Direct EFT
, allow 2 to 3 business days for it to reflect, and payments are always allocated to the oldest outstanding invoice first, so make sure your payment covers all your services.
FAQs
How long does reactivation take after I’ve made a payment?
Reactivation is typically automatic within a few hours once your payment reflects. For EFT payments, allow 2 to 3 working days, depending on your bank’s clearance period.
Can I recover my data after a cancellation?
If your service was cancelled recently, our Team can check for recoverable backups. However, data recovery isn’t guaranteed once a hosting plan expires past its retention period.
Will I be charged a reactivation fee?
Yes,
reactivation fees may apply of R50.00
to restore services that were temporarily suspended due to non-payment, while a slightly different fee may apply for products or services that were fully suspended or cancelled. This fee covers the administrative process required to reconnect your hosting, etc.
Additional Resources
A Guide to Suspensions, Invoices and Payments at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Reactivating your services with 1-grid, which have been suspended or cancelled services doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Suspensions at 1-grid: A Guide
content:
At 1-grid, we understand that uninterrupted service is essential to your business and digital presence, but sometimes,
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services may be affected by temporary
suspensions
due to outstanding payments or security-related issues.
This guide will help you understand
what a suspension means
,
why suspensions occur
, what you can do to
restore your services quickly
, and how to
prevent future disruptions
, ensuring your website and email stay up and running with confidence.
Table of Contents
What is a Suspension?
Suspension Reasons
Invoicing and Payments During Suspension
Benefits of Paying on Time
What is a Service Reactivation Fee?
When Can I Request Reactivation?
What Hosting Services Can Be Reactivated?
How to Reactivate a Hosting Package due to Non-Payment
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What is a Suspension?
A
temporary
and
reversible
suspension
happens when your hosting account or related service (email, domain, or website) is temporarily disabled due to an
unpaid
invoice
,
overdue
renewal
, or a
security-related issue
(e.g.,
malware
,
phishing activity
, or
overuse of resources
). This can be reversed once
payment
or the required action is completed.
At 1-grid, we believe in the importance of uninterrupted services and supporting you in keeping your brand online, which is why we play our part in proactively sending invoices in advance, sending notifications, and reminders before any potential suspension may occur.
Suspension Reasons
Common reasons for service suspension include:
Unpaid or Overdue Invoices:
The most common reason for suspension. If payment isn’t received after multiple reminders, your services are automatically paused to prevent account debt and resource overuse.
Learn more:
Why Do I Have to Pay a Service Reactivation Fee?
Security Concerns:
Services may be temporarily suspended if suspicious activity, phishing, or malware is detected to protect your data and other customers on the network.
Exceeded Resource Limits:
In some cases, hosting accounts can be suspended for exceeding storage, bandwidth, or email quota limits.
REMEMBER:
Suspension is not permanent.
Invoicing and Payments During Suspension
At 1-grid, we understand that payment delays can happen, and our goal is to help you get your services back online as quickly and seamlessly as possible.
When a service is suspended due to non-payment, it’s important to know that
your billing cycle continues
until the account is formally
cancelled or terminated
.
Suspension is a
temporary status
that restricts access, but your service remains active in the background.
This means that:
Your hosting space, domain allocation, and IP resources remain reserved for you.
Your website data and content are securely stored and not deleted.
We continue to maintain and pay for the underlying infrastructure that supports your service.
Because your service isn’t cancelled,
the normal billing process continues
until payment is received or a cancellation request is processed. Once payment reflects, your account is automatically reactivated, and access to your services is restored immediately.
To avoid unnecessary charges or downtime, we recommend:
Keeping your payment details up to date in your
Customer Zone
.
Paying outstanding invoices promptly to avoid suspension.
Cancelling services you no longer need through the official 1-grid Cancellation Process to stop billing.
Your trust matters to us, and we’re here to make reactivating your services or managing your billing as quick and stress-free as possible.
Benefits of Paying on Time
Timely payments support continuity and peace of mind for you, while your business needs your focus:
Avoids Interruptions:
Paying
invoices
on time keeps your hosting, domain, and email active without any downtime.
Protects Your Data:
Prevents accidental loss of website files or email due to
prolonged suspension
.
Builds Reliability:
Consistent payments reflect professionalism and help maintain trust with your customers.
Simplifies Renewals:
Ensures connected services (like
SSL certificates
and
domains
) stay automatically renewed.
TIP:
By automating your payments and paying your 1-grid services by
debit order
makes business a little more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, time-saving, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing and building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
What is a Service Reactivation Fee?
A
reactivation fee
covers the administrative process of restoring data, reconnecting services, and verifying account integrity, and
the fee starts at R50.00
for a standard suspension due to non-payment and can vary depending on the administration costs involved in needing to do a recovery. Reactivation requests that require a manual restore often can result in a higher fee, for cases after
60 days of suspension or cancellation
.
When Can I Request Reactivation?
Non-Payment
Settle your outstanding invoice through
Debit Order
,
PayFast
,
EFT
, or
SnapScan
, etc
.
Security or Malware Issues
Remove or clean up any compromised files, then contact
1-grid Support
to confirm.
Resource Limits
Upgrade your hosting plan
or free up
storage space
.
What Hosting Services Can Be Reactivated?
At 1-grid, most
hosting
products
(
Web Hosting
,
Email Hosting
,
WordPress Hosting
,
VPS
) can be
reactivated within 60 to 90 days
, if:
The outstanding balance is settled in full.
The service hasn’t been permanently deleted or replaced.
IMPORTANT:
Services cancelled beyond the data retention period require a new setup or restoration.
How to Reactivate a Hosting Package due to Non-Payment
We have
quick guides
to make paying your invoices easy and stress-free.
Here are the quick steps to make a payment:
1.
Log in
to your 1-grid
Customer Zone
.
2. Go to
Billing > My Invoices
.
3. Locate your
unpaid invoice
for the suspended service.
4. Click
Pay Now
and choose your
preferred payment method
, and follow the prompts or steps for the method selected.
IMPORTANT:
Once payment clears, the system will automatically process the reactivation.
Reactivation for suspensions may take a few hours after payment confirmation.
For cancelled services, contact
Billing Support
via a
Customer Zone ticket
or directly via email at
[email protected]
to request manual reactivation.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if:
You’ve paid,
but your service is not yet reactivated
after 2 to 4 hours (2 to 3 business days for
Direct EFT
payments)
Your hosting product was cancelled, and you need to confirm eligibility for reactivation.
You believe your account was suspended by mistake or due to a technical issue.
If you need assistance restoring files or backups from a cancelled service, where available.
If you’d like to discuss
reactivation fees
or
product upgrades
.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
Impacted services.
FAQs
Will I lose my data during suspension?
Your data remains secure during temporary suspension. However, prolonged non-payment could lead to data deletion if the account transitions to cancellation outside of the period of 60 to 90 days.
Can I avoid suspension due to non-payment in the future?
Yes, we recommend that you enable
Debit Order payments
or make early payments through your
Customer Zone
.
What happens if my service was suspended due to malware or phishing?
Our team will guide you through cleaning and restoring your files before reactivating the service.
Additional Resources
Why Do I Have to Pay a Service Reactivation Fee?
Service Remains Suspended After Payment
How to Reactivate a Previously Suspended or Cancelled Hosting Product
How to Update My Payment Method in Customer Zone
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding suspensions and how to reactivate your services doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Authenticator App Troubleshooting Guide
content:
Most authentication problems when trying to
add your 1-grid account for 2FA for use with an Authentication App
like Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator or Authy, to name a few, are caused by small, and fixable issues.
In most cases, access can be restored in minutes without compromising your account’s security. If something feels wrong, it’s usually technical, not a breach, so there’s no need to panic!
This guide will help you quickly troubleshoot common authenticator app issues when
adding
or logging into your 1-grid Customer Zone to complete
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
successfully.
Table of Contents
How Authenticator Apps Work
Authenticator Apps for 2FA
What Issues Can Occur When Using an Authenticator App?
Quick Fix to Try First (Resolves Most Issues)
Common Fixes for Invalid or Rejected Codes
When Re-Syncing the App Is Needed
If You’ve Bought a New Phone or Lost Your Existing One
Important Things to Keep in Mind
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
How Authenticator Apps Work
With an Authenticator App, even if your password is exposed, your account stays protected by generating one-time codes as part of Two-Factor Authentication (2FA).
Authenticator apps generate
time-based, one-time codes
Codes refresh every
30 seconds
Codes work
offline
, but require the correct device time
Each account has a
unique, synced connection
But sometimes, issues or “authentication failed” errors may arise when using an authenticator app, but there is no need to worry!
Authenticator Apps for 2FA
Supported authenticator apps include, but may not be limited to:
Google Authenticator
Microsoft Authenticator
Apple Authenticator App
Authy
Duo
These platforms are widely trusted and used by many customers to keep their accounts secure with next-level security through two-factor verification.
What Issues Can Occur When Using an Authenticator App?
1. “Invalid code” or “Authentication failed” Error
This usually happens when your phone’s time drifts out of sync, causing the authenticator app to generate codes that look correct but aren’t accepted.
2. Can’t Generate a Valid 2FA Code
If codes keep expiring or never work, the app may be using the wrong account entry, or you may be entering the code too late.
3. Changed Phones or Reinstalled Your Authenticator App
Authenticator apps don’t automatically transfer accounts unless you restore them from a backup, so the original link may no longer exist.
4. Stuck During 2FA Setup or Login
This often happens when the setup is interrupted or the app doesn’t fully register the QR code.
5. No Longer Receiving Expected Prompts or Codes
Some authenticator apps rely on background permissions or notifications, and battery-saving settings or VPNs can restrict them.
Quick Fix to Try First (Resolves Most Issues)
Sync Your Device’s Date & Time (Most Effective Fix)
Authenticator apps generate
time-based, one-time codes
. If your device’s clock falls even slightly out of sync, the system may reject valid codes.
What to Check Immediately
Set
Date & Time
to
Automatic
Enable
Automatic Time Zone
App-Specific Guidance
Google Authenticator
Open the app
Go to
Settings → Time correction for codes → Sync now
Microsoft Authenticator
Ensure your phone’s system
date and time are set to Automatic
TIP
: This step alone resolves most authenticator-related login failures.
Common Fixes for Invalid or Rejected Codes
1. Confirm You’re Entering the Correct Code
Authenticator codes refresh every
30 seconds
, so it is important to ensure you are using the most valid one at that moment
Enter the
current 6-digit code
Ensure the code matches your
1-grid account
, and not another saved entry
TIP: If the countdown is almost finished, wait for the next code before entering it.
2. Restart and Update the Authenticator App
Temporary app or device issues can interrupt code generation, causing trouble with completing the initial setup or usage of the app.
Try this sequence:
Fully close the authenticator app and confirm that it’s not running in the background.
Restart your phone
Update the app to the latest version from the App Store or Play Store for the app specifically, or from the relevant platform that you are using
3. Review App Permissions and Check Background Activity
Authenticator apps may rely on background access to function reliably. This is why it is important to check that:
App notifications are enabled
Battery optimisation or power-saving mode is disabled (especially on Android)
Background app activity is allowed
4. Disable VPNs or Restricted Networks
In some cases,
VPNs
or restrictive networks can interfere with authentication so consider switching to a different connection method when using your authenticator app.
What to try:
Temporarily disable any
VPN
you may be using
Log in using your mobile data or a standard home internet connection
When Re-Syncing the App Is Needed
Remove to Re-Add the Authenticator Entry
If the setup was interrupted or the codes are invalid,
re-registering
the authenticator connection often fixes the issue quickly.
What to do next:
Remove the
1-grid account
from your authenticator app
Contact our Support Team to request a
secure authenticator reset
Log in again and scan the
new QR code
to re-link the app
IMPORTANT:
This creates a fresh, correctly synced authentication connection.
If You’ve Bought a New Phone or Lost Your Existing One
If you lost or switched devices and didn’t restore your authenticator app from a backup, you can:
Use your
backup codes
(if saved during setup or any login attempts using this), or
Contact our Support Team for
identity verification and a secure reset
on your new device so that you can restart the process.
IMPORTANT:
For security reasons, we can’t bypass authentication checks, but we’ll guide you through a safe and secure recovery process.
Important Things to Keep in Mind
Authenticator apps work
offline
, but rely on accurate device time
Login issues are usually technical, not security-related
We can assist with
resetting authentication
We cannot bypass verification steps, as this protects your account
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Resetting or understanding 2FA
Login issues related to 2FA
Guidance on using backup codes
We’ll guide you through the next steps securely and as smoothly as possible.
Outside Our Scope
What We’re Unable to Assist With
Bypassing any security authentication checks or providing backup codes
FAQs
Q. Can I use my mobile to complete 2FA if I access Customer Zone from my mobile?
Yes, you can absolutely complete Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) entirely from your mobile device without using a computer. You can complete this by
using Backup Codes
and selecting “Login using Backup Code” from the Two-Factor Authentication pop-up in your Customer Zone.
We recommend this method when you sign in directly from your authenticator device, as switching between the Authenticator app and your Customer Zone login page on mobile can be tricky.
Q. What happens if I delete my authenticator app?
If you remove the app without a backup, you lose the saved account links. You’ll need to use a
backup code
or contact Support for a
secure 2FA reset
.
Q. Can I install my authenticator app on multiple devices?
Some apps support multi-device syncing (like Authy), but not all do. For security reasons, we recommend
one primary device
unless you are confident in the app’s secure sync features.
Q. Can Support send me my authenticator codes?
Your authenticator app
generates codes directly on your device
to keep your account secure. This protects your account and prevents identity-based compromises.
Q. What if I’m not receiving codes or prompts?
Common causes:
Disabled notifications
Battery optimisation
VPN/network restrictions
App permission issues
Enabling notifications and disabling battery optimisation typically resolves this.
Additional Resources
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
All About Passwords at 1-grid
How to Add an Account for 2FA Using an Authenticator App
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Troubleshooting Authenticator App issues to complete Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) on your 1-grid Customer Zone doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Adding an Account for 2FA Using an Authenticator App
content:
Secure your 1-grid account by completing your
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
setup in minutes for your
1-grid Customer Zone
using an Authenticator App to scan a
QR code
, with no technical knowledge required.
This guide walks you through adding your account safely for 2FA using an Authenticator App like Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, Apple Authenticator (iOS Passwords) and Authy, and provides tips for avoiding common setup issues.
Table of Contents
What Is an Authenticator App?
Why Using an Authenticator App Matters
Authenticator Apps Supported by 1-grid
Before You Start (Critical Success Checklist)
Step-by-Step: Adding Your 1-grid Account with an Authenticator App
Google Authenticator App
Microsoft Authenticator App
Apple Authenticator (via iOS Passwords)
Authy Authenticator App
Accessing Your 1-grid Customer Zone on the Mobile for Your Authenticator App
Setting Up 2FA on Mobile Using the Manual Key (No QR Scan Needed)
How to Log in Using a Backup Code (Mobile-Friendly)
Common Setup Issues and Quick Fixes
Our Scope of Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is an Authenticator App?
An authenticator app is a
security tool installed on your phone
that generates
time-based, one-time verification codes
.
Instead of relying on SMS or email delivery, the app creates a
new 6-digit code every 30 seconds
, synced securely to your account.
No internet required
No messages to wait for
One of the safest
2FA
methods available
Why Using an Authenticator App Matters
Adding an authenticator app to your primary device helps to get you securely logged into your
1-grid Customer Zone
when completing
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
as next-level security, and it:
Protects your account from
unauthorised access
Prevents password-only breaches
Gives you control over your login security
Works offline, even without a signal
Authenticator Apps Supported by 1-grid
We officially support and recommend:
Google Authenticator
Microsoft Authenticator
Apple Authenticator (and via iOS Passwords)
Authy Authenticator
Other
TOTP
-based apps (such as
Duo
) may also work, but the instructions below focus on the most widely used and supported options above.
Before You Start (Critical Success Checklist)
It is important to be ready with the following when wanting to set up an account, like your 1-grid account for
2FA
during login into your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Checklist:
Your phone’s
Date & Time is set to Automatic
The camera is access-enabled on your phone
Your
1-grid Customer Zone
is open on a desktop or browser
A few uninterrupted minutes to complete the setup
This is important to help reduce setup issues, as well as to give you enough time to successfully retrieve a one-time code once you have successfully added your account to your Authenticator App, for completing
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
.
Step-by-Step: Adding Your 1-grid Account with an Authenticator App
IMPORTANT:
When scanning a QR code,
hold your phone up to your computer screen
so your camera can clearly read the code. It will attempt to scan it within the 4 green corners of the box on your camera/app.
Google Authenticator App
How to Add Your 1-grid Account for 2FA
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Use your registered email address and password.
Click
Get Started >>
.
A
QR code
and
manual setup key
will appear on your screen.
A manual code is also displayed in case you need to manually enter this.
Open
Google Authenticator
on your phone.
Tap
+ → Scan a QR code
.
Point your phone camera at the QR code on your screen.
It should automatically scan the code.
Enter the generated
6-digit code
to confirm.
TIP:
If scanning fails, choose
Enter a setup key
and manually enter the code shown. Manual entry prevents setup failure if camera permissions are blocked.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Google Authenticator, you can explore this
short video
.
Microsoft Authenticator App
How to Add Your 1-grid Account for 2FA
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Use your registered email address and password.
Click
Get Started >>
.
A
QR code
and
manual code
will appear on your screen.
Open
Microsoft Authenticator
.
Tap
+ → Personal account
.
Choose
Scan a QR code
.
Scan the QR code displayed on your screen by pointing your phone camera at the screen.
This should automatically scan the code.
Enter the
6-digit code
to complete setup.
TIP:
If prompted, allow camera permissions because without them, scanning won’t work. If scanning fails, enter the code provided manually.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Microsoft Authenticator, you can explore this
short video
.
Apple Authenticator (via iOS Passwords)
How to Add Your 1-grid Account for 2FA
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Use your registered email address and password.
Click
Get Started >>
.
A QR code and manual setup key will appear on screen.
On your iPhone, go to
Settings → Passwords
.
Select the relevant account (or add a new one).
Tap
Set Up Verification Code → Scan QR Code
.
Scan the QR code using your phone camera and enter the
6-digit code
shown to confirm.
TIP:
If scanning fails, choose
Enter Setup Key
instead and manually enter the code displayed.
Using the Apple Authenticator App instead?
To add your account via the Apple Authenticator app specifically and not through Passwords, you can check out their useful guide
here
.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Apple Passwords, you can explore this
guide
further.
Authy Authenticator App
How to Add Your 1-grid Account for 2FA
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
Use your registered email address and password.
Click
Get Started >>
.
A QR code and setup key for manual adding will appear.
Open
Authy
.
Tap
+ Add Account
.
Choose
Scan QR Code
.
Scan the QR code on your screen using your phone’s camera.
Enter the generated
6-digit code
to verify and confirm.
TIP:
Ensure your phone time is set to
Automatic
to avoid invalid codes.
For additional support with scanning a QR code using Authy, you can explore this
short video
.
Accessing Your 1-grid Customer Zone on the Mobile for Your Authenticator App
If you’re setting up or using Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
entirely on your mobile phone
, you’re not doing anything wrong, and you’re not alone. Some mobile devices and browsers make it difficult to switch smoothly between the
Customer Zone login page
and your
Authenticator app
, which can cause the page to refresh or restart the login process.
To support you through this, 1-grid provides a
secure, reliable alternative
that works even when app switching isn’t supported.
When This Applies
You may need this method if:
You’re unable to enter the 6-digit code in time before the page reloads
You’re logging into the 1-grid Customer Zone using a
mobile browser
Your
Authenticator app is on the same device
Switching between the browser and the Authenticator app causes the login page to refresh
This is a known mobile behaviour on some devices and browsers, so it’s not an error with your account or setup.
Setting Up 2FA on Mobile Using the Manual Key (No QR Scan Needed)
If you’re enabling 2FA on mobile for the first time, you may notice that scanning a QR code isn’t possible when everything is on the same screen. In this case, the
manual setup key
is the safest and most effective option.
How the Manual Key Setup Works
Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
using your mobile browser
Start the
2FA setup
process
When the QR code appears, select
“Can’t scan?”
or
“Enter key manually”
Copy the setup key shown on the screen, or write it down to manually enter it
Open your preferred Authenticator app
Add a new account and choose
Enter a setup key
Paste the key, name the account (e.g.
1-grid Customer Zone
), and save
Enter the generated 6-digit code back in the Customer Zone to complete setup
TIP:
This works well when you have no camera access, and it means no app switching and no risk of the page refreshing during setup.
IMPORTANT:
When app switching isn’t possible when you’re accessing your Customer Zone from the same device you are using for your Authenticator App, the
“
Login using Backup Code
”
option is the recommended method as it is the most stable and secure way to complete your login.
How to Log in Using a Backup Code (Mobile-Friendly)
Steps
Open your mobile browser and go to the
1-grid Customer Zone login page
Enter your email address and password as normal
When prompted for your 2FA code, select
“Login using Backup Code”
Enter one of the backup codes that were generated when you enabled 2FA
You’ll be logged in successfully without needing to switch apps
IMPORTANT:
Backup codes are
single-use
, secure, and designed specifically for situations like this.
Common Setup Issues and Quick Fixes
Here are some quick fixes, but you can explore our full
troubleshooting guide
for more.
“Invalid Code” or “Authentication Failed”
What’s happening:
Your phone’s time is slightly out of sync, or the code expired before it was entered successfully.
Quick Fix:
Set your phone’s date and time to
Automatic
, then enter a
fresh code
from the app.
IMPORTANT:
Remember, a code refreshes
every 30 seconds
, so it is important to be ready to enter the code.
Can’t Generate a Valid 2FA Code
What’s happening:
The account link was interrupted or not fully completed.
Quick Fix:
Restart the setup and complete it in one session without refreshing the page.
IMPORTANT:
Remember, you can contact our Support Team if you need help with resetting your 2FA, especially if you need to do a reinstall of the app (as below).
Changed Phones or Reinstalled the App
What’s happening:
Authenticator apps don’t always restore accounts automatically.
Quick Fix:
Use saved backup codes, or contact Support to
securely reset 2FA
and re-link your new device.
Stuck During Setup or Login
What’s happening:
The QR code expired, or the setup page refreshed.
Quick Fix:
Restart the login process and scan a
new QR code
.
IMPORTANT:
If you get stuck, you can contact our Support Team for assistance with a 2FA reset if this is needed.
No Longer Receiving Codes or Prompts
What’s happening:
Battery optimisation, blocked notifications, or VPN interference.
Quick Fix:
Allow background activity, enable notifications, and temporarily disable any VPN.
TIP:
Try connecting to a standard home network or your mobile data.
Our
Scope of Support
If you’re stuck and need any additional guidance or support, here’s an outline of what we can do and what is not within our
Scope of Support
.
Included Support
What We Can Help With
Secure 2FA resets
Basic guidance for re-adding authenticator apps
Verifying setup issues
Guiding you through recovery steps
We’ll guide you through the next steps securely and as smoothly as possible.
Outside Our Scope
What We’re Unable to Assist With
Security protection bypassing such as the codes during authentication checks
FAQs
Q. Can I use my mobile to set up 2FA if I don’t have access to a computer?
Yes, you can absolutely set up
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
entirely from your mobile device without using a computer. Modern authenticator apps and services allow for full, secure 2FA activation directly on smartphones by using the “
Manual Key
” method because you are unable to scan a QR code on the device you’re using.
Remember, if you are accessing your Customer Zone via your mobile browser on your authenticator device after you have set up 2FA, you may need to use the “Login with Backup Code” option.
Q. Do authenticator apps need internet access?
No, they work offline once they are set up.
Q. Why do codes change every 30 seconds?
This prevents reuse and keeps your account secure.
Q. Can I use more than one authenticator app?
Only one app can be actively linked at a time.
Q. What if I lose my phone?
Contact Support for a secure identity check and 2FA reset.
Additional Resources
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) and Why It Matters
Troubleshooting Authentication App Issues
Retrieve a Lost 1-grid Customer Zone Password
Passwords at 1-grid
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Adding your 1-grid Account to an Authenticator App to complete Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) doesn’t have to feel technical with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Check out our
Scope of Support
, and then contact our Support Team for further clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: How to Check Your Credit Balance on Your 1-grid Account
content:
Our option to pay using credit on your account makes it a simple, secure, and proactive way to stay on top of your payments, and we make it even easier to check your credit balance on your 1-grid account.
By
keeping funds
in your 1-grid account, you can
automatically pay
invoices
when they’re due, meaning no manual payments, no delays, and no risk of service suspension. It’s an ideal way for you to maintain full control over your billing while enjoying the convenience of automatic renewals and faster processing.
This guide will show you how to check your credit balance directly through your
Customer Zone
.
Table of Contents
What Is a Credit Balance on Your 1-Grid Account?
Benefits of Paying with Credit
How to Check Your Credit Balance on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Adding Funds as Credit on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Paying Invoices with Credit on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Need Additional Support?
What Is a Credit Balance on Your 1-Grid Account?
A credit balance on your 1-grid account means that funds are
preloaded/loaded
onto your 1-grid account either by yourself or perhaps as a credit for any overpayment. Use this to
pay any future invoices
for
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services, giving you peace of mind while other things need your attention.
Benefits of Paying with Credit
Instant Payments
means no waiting period as future invoices are settled immediately.
Automatic Renewal Protection
prevents downtime or missed renewals.
Budget-friendly
means you can manage expenses proactively by pre-funding your account.
Convenient
means avoiding repeated manual payments for every invoice.
Flexible
means using your credit for any 1-grid service whenever needed.
Secure
gives you peace of mind as payments are handled in your 1-grid Customer Zone.
How to Check Your Credit Balance on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Once you have added funds to your account, it’s easy to keep an eye on this to stay ahead of upcoming invoices for your domains, hosting, and other services. When time is of the essence, we’ve made it simple to stay ahead of the game.
It’s really quick and easy to view your credit balance on your account:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2. Scroll down to the
Available Credit
section.
Your credit amount is shown in
bold
. e.g.
R420.00, means this can be used
to pay outstanding
invoices
, for any
new services
you are
ordering
or any future
invoices
.
IMPORTANT: No credit
on the account means the amount displayed is
R0.00
, as it is above.
Adding Funds as Credit on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Adding funds to your account is made simple with our
quick guide
, so that you can secure payment on any future invoices, so you can focus on your business as a priority!
Paying Invoices with Credit on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Making payment on any of your invoices using your existing or new credit balance is just as easy! View our
quick guide
on how to do this, and never miss a payment again!
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
The credit doesn’t reflect a successful payment.
You’ve incorrectly applied or deducted an amount.
You’re struggling to view your credit or cannot see your credit balance.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
Details of the issue you are experiencing.
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Can I use credit to pay for any service?
Yes, account credit can be used for all active services such as domains, hosting, and email, and even for payment of any new orders.
How long does it take for credit to reflect?
Payments via Debit Order, PayFast, or Credit Card are instant. Manual EFT payments can take 2 to 3 working days to reflect.
Can I get a refund for unused credit?
Refunds may be processed under specific circumstances; please contact Billing Support for assistance.
Additional Resources
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
Everything You Need to Know About Invoicing at 1-grid
Introducing the 1-grid Android Application
Payment FAQs
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Checking the credit balance on your 1-grid account doesn’t have to feel like a challenge with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Paying Your Invoices with Credit on Your 1-grid Account
content:
At
1-grid
, we know how important it is to keep your
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services active without disruption. That’s why we offer the option of paying your invoices with credit on your 1-grid account, making it a simple, secure, and proactive way to stay on top of your payments.
By keeping funds in your 1-grid account, you can
automatically pay
invoices
when they’re due, meaning no manual payments, no delays, and no risk of service suspension. It’s ideal for customers who prefer full control over their billing while enjoying the convenience of automatic renewals and faster processing.
This guide will walk you through
everything you need to know about using account credit
at 1-grid, including what credit is,
how to add funds
,
how to check your balance
, and how to pay invoices using available credit directly through your
Customer Zone
.
Table of Contents
What Is Credit on Your 1-Grid Account?
Benefits of Paying with Credit
Instant Payments
Automatic Renewal Protection
Budget-friendly
Convenient
Flexible
Secure
Adding Credit to Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)

Additional Resources
What Is Credit on Your 1-Grid Account?
Credit on your 1-grid account means funds are preloaded onto your 1-grid account that can be used to pay future
invoices
for domains, hosting, and other services.
This means that you can
top up your credit
balance at any time using
Debit Order
,
Credit Card via PayFast
,
Instant EFT via PayFast
, or
Direct EFT
.
Once received, funds are automatically allocated to your account and can be used to pay any future invoices as they become due.
Benefits of Paying with Credit
Instant Payments
No waiting period, as future invoices are settled immediately.
Automatic Renewal Protection
Prevents downtime or missed renewals.
Budget-friendly
Manage expenses proactively by pre-funding your account.
Convenient
Avoid repeated manual payments for every invoice.
Flexible
Use it for any 1-grid service whenever needed.
Secure
Payments are handled through trusted gateways and your 1-grid Customer Zone.
Adding Credit to Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Adding funds to your account is made simple with our
quick guide
,
so that you can secure payment by paying future invoices with credit, so you can focus on your business as a priority!
TIP:
Funds added to your account are automatically available to pay future
invoices
or to pay for any
new orders
.
Checking Your Credit Balance on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
If you have added funds to your 1-grid account or have an existing credit balance, it’s easy to keep an eye on this to stay ahead of upcoming invoices for your domains, hosting, and other services. When time is of the essence, we’ve made it simple to stay ahead of the game with our
easy-to-follow guide
.
IMPORTANT:
A R0.00 balance means paying with credit will not appear as an option to pay an invoice.

To pay any existing invoices using your credit:

Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.

on

.

the
Unpaid
invoice, then

on it to

.
4. Check the
Amount of Credit
needed, type in the
amount

Can I use credit to pay for any service?
Yes, account credit can be used for all active services such as domains, hosting, and email, and even for payment of any new orders.
How long does it take for credit to reflect?
Payments via Debit Order, PayFast, or Credit Card are instant. Manual EFT payments can take 2 to 3 working days to reflect.
What happens if my credit balance isn’t enough?
You can top up your credit or pay the outstanding amount using another method.
Can I get a refund for unused credit?
Refunds may be processed under specific circumstances; please contact Billing Support for assistance.
Additional Resources

We’re Here to
Help
:
Paying your invoices with credit for your services doesn’t have to feel like a challenge with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
