title: Paying Your Invoices with SnapScan
content:
At 1-grid, we understand how important it is to keep your
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services running smoothly without any interruptions. That’s why we’ve made paying your invoices easier, faster, and more secure with
SnapScan
, one of South Africa’s most trusted mobile payment solutions.
With
SnapScan
, you can pay your invoices instantly by simply scanning a QR code from your smartphone, meaning no credit card or manual EFT is required. It’s safe, convenient, and gives you real-time confirmation, ensuring your services stay active without delay.
This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about
SnapScan payments with 1-grid,
including how it works, the key benefits, and step-by-step instructions to pay directly via your
Customer Zone
.
Table of Contents
What Is a SnapScan Payment?
Benefits of Paying via SnapScan
Instant Confirmation
Secure and Trusted
Convenient
Mobile-friendly
No Hidden Fees
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
How to Pay Your Invoice with SnapScan via Customer Zone
Security and Transparency
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a SnapScan Payment?
SnapScan is a mobile payment solution that allows you to pay instantly using your smartphone. You simply scan a QR code, confirm your payment, and you’re done. It’s that easy, and there’s no need for manual EFTs or card swipes.
SnapScan integrates directly with 1-grid
invoices
, providing a fast, secure, and cashless way to pay for your hosting, domains, and other services in seconds.
How It Works:
You log in to the app on your
Android mobile phone
or
iPhone/iPad
.
You scan the QR code to make a payment and confirm the payment.
The payment reflects in real-time, so no proof of payment is needed.
Benefits of Paying via SnapScan
Empowering you with the convenience, control, and peace of mind that SnapScan offers.
Key Benefits:
Instant Confirmation
Payments are reflected immediately, preventing service interruptions.
Secure and Trusted
SnapScan uses bank-level encryption and PIN or biometric verification.
Convenient
No need to log into your banking app or remember reference numbers.
Mobile-friendly
Make payments anywhere, anytime, directly from your smartphone.
No Hidden Fees
What you pay is what is billed, offering full transparency.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
While paying with SnapScan is quick and reliable,
debit order
remains the most recommended method for recurring services like hosting, domain renewals, and business email accounts.
Paying your 1-grid services by
debit order
is more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
Follow these simple steps to
update your payment method
:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3.
Select
Snapscan
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4.
Click
on
Save Changes
.
How to Pay Your Invoice with SnapScan via Customer Zone
To make a quick instant payment using SnapScan:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3.
Locate
the
Unpaid
invoice and
click
on it to
open it
.
4. Make sure that
Snapscan
is selected under
Payment Method
.
5. Open the SnapScan app on your
Android
or
iPhone/iPad
and
scan the QR Code
image on the invoice.
6. Complete payment on your phone, then click
Confirm Payment
on your invoice.
Your account will be updated immediately, and your services will remain active.
TIP:
Always double-check that your account has sufficient funds to avoid failed transactions or
service suspensions
.
Security and Transparency
SnapScan is powered by
Standard Bank
and uses
bank-level security
, ensuring every transaction is encrypted and verified. Your card details are never shared with 1-grid or stored on our systems.
You can track your full payment history within both
SnapScan
and your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
SECURITY TIP:
Always ensure you scan a QR code directly from your
1-grid invoice
, not from any third-party or email links.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
Your payment confirmation doesn’t reflect within a few minutes.
You experience any errors while using SnapScan and have tried to
troubleshoot with them
.
You need assistance updating your payment method or setting up a Debit Order.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
A screenshot of any error that you are receiving from Snapscan.
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Is SnapScan safe to use for my hosting payments?
Yes, SnapScan uses bank-level encryption and requires PIN or biometric verification to complete payments.
Can I use SnapScan for recurring payments?
Currently, paying invoices with SnapScan means that payments are once-off. For recurring payments, we recommend setting up a
Debit Order
.
What if I accidentally scan the wrong QR code?
Always confirm that your QR code is from your
1-grid Customer Zone invoice
before completing payment.
Can I still pay manually via EFT if I prefer?
Yes, direct EFT remains available, but SnapScan and Debit Orders provide faster, more reliable processing.
Additional Resources
Paying by Debit Orders at 1-grid
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
Suspended Services Remain After Payment
Invoicing at 1-grid
The 1-grid Android Application
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Paying invoices with SnapScan for your services doesn’t have to cause worry with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Paying Your Invoices via Direct EFT
content:
At 1-grid, we understand how important it is to keep your
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services running smoothly without any interruptions. That’s why we offer the option for paying your
invoices
via
Direct EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer)
as a trusted and familiar way to pay securely through your own bank account.
With
Direct EFT
, you stay in control of your payments by transferring funds directly from your bank to 1-grid’s account. While this method may take
2 to 3 working days
to reflect, depending on your bank, it’s ideal for customers who prefer manual payments and want to manage their transactions independently.
This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about
paying your invoices via Direct EFT
with 1-grid, including how it works, its pros and cons, and step-by-step instructions on how to make a payment and view this directly through your
Customer Zone
.
Table of Contents
What Is a Direct EFT Payment?
Pros and Cons of Paying via Direct EFT
Advantages
Disadvantages
Available Direct EFT Options
Internet Payment (EFT)
Cash Deposit at Standard Bank
International Payment
Our Standard Bank Account Details for Direct EFT Payments
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
How to Pay Your Invoice with Direct EFT (via Customer Zone)
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Direct EFT Payment?
A
Direct EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer)
is a manual bank payment where you transfer funds directly from your bank account to 1-grid’s. This method is familiar and secure but may take
2 to 3 working days
to reflect, depending on the bank’s processing time.
Once received, payments are automatically allocated to your
oldest outstanding
invoices
to prevent overdue accounts, including any unpaid
domain renewal
invoices
.
Pros and Cons of Paying via Direct EFT
Advantages
Familiar and widely accessible through online banking or mobile apps.
No need for third-party payment systems.
Secure if done via verified banking channels.
Ideal for customers who prefer manual control of their payments.
Disadvantages
Payments can take up to
72 hours
to reflect.
Services may be
suspended
if payment is delayed or made over weekends/public holidays.
Requires manual proof-of-payment uploads if not auto-verified.
EFTs are allocated automatically, so specific invoice selection isn’t guaranteed if there are multiple outstanding
invoices
, as the oldest invoices are always settled first.
IMPORTANT:
To avoid
service interruptions
, always pay
3 days before
your due date and use your
Customer Code
as your payment reference.
Available Direct EFT Options
Internet Payment (EFT)
This is the preferred manual method. Log in to your online banking portal and pay directly to 1-grid’s Bank Account using your Customer Code as your reference.
Using the internet banking payment facility allows you to load us as a beneficiary for settlement to your 1-grid account.
All internet payments must be correctly referenced to ensure that 1-grid is able to identify your payment and correctly allocate it to your account.
Payments from Standard Bank to Standard Bank will be reflected immediately in our bank account. The payment will be allocated to your account between 8 a.m. and 10 a.m. the next day.
Payments
from a different bank
can take approximately 2 to 3 working days to reflect
on your 1-grid account.
Cash Deposit at Standard Bank
You can make cash payments at your nearest bank branch directly into 1-grid’s Standard Bank account. However, please note that cash deposits often carry additional bank fees, which should be added to your total invoice amount to ensure full payment is received.
International Payment
For international customers, you can send payment via
SWIFT transfer
. Keep in mind that international banking fees apply, and funds may take longer (
up to 10 business days
) to clear.
Our Standard Bank Account Details for Direct EFT Payments
When making payment, you can use our Bank Details below, or reference them from our latest Bank Confirmation Letter.
Payment Reference:
(Customer Code)
Account Holder:
1 GRID (PTY) LTD
Bank:
Standard Bank
Account Number:
072987979
Universal Code:
051001
Account Type:
Cheque
1-grid Standard Bank Confirmation Letter_Latest_September 2025
Download
REMINDER:
Payments are
automatically allocated to your oldest outstanding
invoices
first
to prevent overdue accounts, including any unpaid
domain renewal
invoices
.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
While EFT works,
debit order
remains the most recommended method for recurring services like hosting, domain renewals, and business email accounts.
Paying your 1-grid services by
debit order
is more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
Follow these simple steps to
update your payment method
:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3.
Select
Direct EFT
or
Direct Deposit
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4.
Click
on
Save Changes
.
How to Pay Your Invoice with Direct EFT (via Customer Zone)
To make your payment using direct EFT or direct deposit:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3.
Locate
the
Unpaid
invoice and
click
on it to
open it
.
4. Make sure that
Direct EFT
or
Direct Deposit
is selected under
Payment Method
.
6. Take note of our
Banking Details
and make payment using your
Customer Code
listed under
Use this payment reference
.
7. Complete
Payment
via your Mobile Banking, Online Banking or at your nearest bank.
8. Once completed, send
Proof of Payment
to
[email protected]
.
Your account will be updated within
24 to 72 hours, depending on the bank you are paying from
and the EFT method being used.
TIP:
Always aim to pay at least 3 working days before your invoice due date to ensure no
service suspensions
.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
Your EFT payment hasn’t been reflected after 3 working days.
You entered the wrong reference number.
You need to confirm a payment or discuss an invoice allocation.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
How long does an EFT payment take to reflect?
EFT payments typically reflect within
2 to 3 working days
, depending on the bank.
Can I pay multiple invoices at once via EFT?
Yes, but please note that payments are automatically allocated to the
oldest unpaid invoices first
.
What if I accidentally use the wrong payment reference?
Contact our
Billing Support Team
with your proof of payment so we can manually match it to your account.
Additional Resources
Debit Orders at 1-grid
Payment Methods at 1-grid
Still Suspended After Payment
Invoicing at 1-grid
The 1-grid Android Application
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Paying your invoices with Direct EFT or Direct Deposit for your services doesn’t have to cause worry with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Paying Your Invoices with Secure Credit Card via PayFast
content:
At 1-grid, we know how important uninterrupted
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services are to your business. That’s why we’ve made it simple for paying
invoices
securely online with credit card
through
PayFast
, using your
Visa
or
Mastercard
credit card.
PayFast provides a
trusted, encrypted, and verified
payment platform that ensures your details remain safe while your payment reflects instantly, keeping your services active without delay.
This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about credit card payments with 1-grid, the benefits of paying using this method, and a step-by-step guide on how to make payment via your Customer Zone.
Table of Contents
What Is a Secure Credit Card Payment via PayFast?
Benefits of Paying via Secure Credit Card (PayFast)
Instant Processing
Enhanced Security
Convenience
Professional Reliability
Automatic Receipts
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
How to Pay Your Invoice with Secure Credit Card via Customer Zone
Security and Transparency
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is a Secure Credit Card Payment via PayFast?
A secure credit card payment via
PayFast
allows you to pay for your hosting, domains, or email renewals directly online without logging into your bank.
PayFast uses
3D Secure technology
, which is a multi-layered authentication process required by major banks to ensure that only the cardholder can authorise the payment. This protects your financial data and gives you complete control over every transaction.
Key Features of PayFast Secure Payments:
Bank-level security
using SSL encryption and fraud detection.
Instant payment confirmation
, ensuring no service downtime.
No extra fees
, so you pay only the invoice amount.
Compatibility with all major South African banks
and card providers.
Benefits of Paying via Secure Credit Card (PayFast)
Using PayFast for your payments offers several advantages for busy professionals and small business owners:
Instant Processing
Payments clear in real-time, ensuring uninterrupted service.
Enhanced Security
3D Secure authentication verifies transactions directly with your bank.
Convenience
No need for manual bank transfers or proof of payment emails.
Professional Reliability
Perfect for customers who prefer managing payments online at their convenience.
Automatic Receipts
You’ll receive an instant confirmation email once payment is processed.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
While paying your invoices with credit card through PayFast is quick and reliable,
debit order
remains the most recommended method for recurring services like hosting, domain renewals, and business email accounts.
It’s more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, and building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
Follow these simple steps to
update your payment method
:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3.
Select
PayFast Secure Credit Card
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4.
Click
on
Save Changes
.
How to Pay Your Invoice with Secure Credit Card via Customer Zone
To make a quick credit card payment using Secure Credit Card via PayFast:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3.
Locate
the
Unpaid
invoice and
click
on it to
open it
.
4. Make sure that
PayFast Secure Credit Card
is selected under
Payment Method
and
click
Subscribe Using PayFast
.
6. You’ll be redirected to the secure
PayFast portal
.
7.
Enter
your
credit card details
and
follow
the
3D Secure prompts
.
8.
*
If you have previously completed 5 to 7, you can
select
Recurring Payment
.
9. Once completed, you’ll receive instant payment confirmation via email.
Your account will be updated immediately once you have completed paying your invoice with credit card, and your services will remain active.
TIP:
Always double-check that your account has sufficient funds to avoid failed transactions or
service suspensions
.
Security and Transparency
To protect your account further:
Ensure your card is activated for
online payments
.
Never share your
One-Time PIN (OTP)
or
bank login details
.
Always verify you are on the secure PayFast domain (
https://payment.payfast.io/eng/process/payment/
) before entering any information.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
You’ve contacted your bank to troubleshoot issues on their end.
Your card payment failed, but funds were deducted.
You want help switching from PayFast to Debit Order after attempting to switch on your end.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
Screenshot of any error message you are receiving when trying to pay via PayFast’s portal.
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Is PayFast safe for online payments?
Yes, PayFast uses secure SSL encryption and 3D Secure verification to ensure your payment details remain protected.
Can I save my credit card for future use?
Yes, you can securely save your card on PayFast for faster future payments by selecting “Recurring Payment” once you have signed up for payment via Credit Card.
How soon will my payment reflect?
Payments made via PayFast are reflected immediately, ensuring no service interruption.
Can I still pay via Debit Order instead?
Yes, Debit Order remains our preferred payment option for convenience and reliability. You can update this anytime in your Customer Zone.
What should I do if my credit card payment fails?
Check your available funds, verify that your card supports online transactions, or contact your bank. If the issue persists, reach out to
[email protected]
.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Debit Orders at 1-grid
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
Services Still Suspended After Payment
Invoicing at 1-grid
The 1-grid Android Application
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Paying your invoices with a secure credit card via PayFast for your services doesn’t have to cause worry with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Paying Your Invoices with Instant EFT (via PayFast)
content:
At 1-grid, we know how important it is to keep your
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services running without interruption. That’s why we’ve partnered with
PayFast
to make paying your
invoices
simple, secure, and instant with
Instant EFT,
Masterpass
and
Zapper
, a trusted method for customers who prefer to pay directly from their bank account without using a
credit card
.
Instant EFT gives you the convenience of
real-time payment confirmation
, ensuring your invoices are processed immediately and your services remain active.
This guide will take you through everything you need to know about instant EFT payments with 1-grid, the benefits of paying using this method, and a step-by-step guide on how to make payment via your Customer Zone.
Table of Contents
What Is an Instant EFT Payment via PayFast?
Benefits of Paying via Instant EFT (PayFast)
Immediate Payment Confirmation
Bank-level Security
No Card Required
No Proof of Payment Needed
Seamless Experience
Instant EFT Payment Options Available
Credit & Cheque Card (via PayFast)
Samsung Pay
Instant EFT (via PayFast)
Capitec Pay
SnapScan
Zapper
Bank QR Code Apps
Scan to Pay (powered by Ukheshe)
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
How to Pay Your Invoice with Instant EFT via Customer Zone
Security and Transparency
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is an Instant EFT Payment via PayFast?
An
Instant EFT via PayFast
allows you to make an immediate electronic funds transfer directly from your bank account without waiting for manual clearance. It connects securely to your bank through
PayFast’s
bank-level encryption
, so payments reflect instantly and safely.
How It Works:
You log in to your bank through PayFast’s secure portal.
You approve the payment with your usual online banking details or app authentication.
The payment reflects in real-time, so no proof of payment is needed.
Supported Banks:
PayFast supports
major South African banks
, including Absa, Capitec, FNB, Nedbank, Standard Bank, TymeBank, and Investec.
Benefits of Paying via Instant EFT (PayFast)
Instant EFT combines speed, convenience, and security, making it an all-in-one trusted payment method.
Here’s why many customers love using Instant EFT:
Immediate Payment Confirmation
Services stay active without delay.
Bank-level Security
Transactions are encrypted and verified through your bank.
No Card Required
Pay directly from your account, making it ideal if you don’t use credit cards.
No Proof of Payment Needed
Payment confirmation is automatic and sent to your email.
Seamless Experience
No manual EFT details, no human error; just instant payment processing.
Instant EFT Payment Options Available
Credit & Cheque Card (via PayFast)
Pay instantly using your Visa or Mastercard. Transactions are secured with 3D Secure and encryption, giving you real-time confirmation and avoiding manual EFT delays.
Samsung Pay
A mobile wallet option that lets you tap-to-pay securely using your Samsung phone. Payments are authenticated via biometrics or PIN, making checkout fast and seamless.
Instant EFT (via PayFast)
Bank-to-bank transfer that clears immediately. You log into your bank through a secure gateway, authorise the payment, and the funds reflect instantly, meaning no manual proof of payment is needed.
Capitec Pay
Specifically for Capitec Bank users. Initiate the payment through Capitec’s banking app via PayFast’s Instant EFT interface, with immediate confirmation and convenience.
SnapScan
A QR-based mobile payment method. You scan a payment QR code from your invoice using the SnapScan app, confirm the amount, and complete the payment instantly.
Zapper
Another QR-based mobile wallet. You scan the Zapper QR code via the app, confirm payment details, and funds are transferred immediately, similar to SnapScan.
Bank QR Code Apps
Many banks offer built-in QR scanning features in their apps. When PayFast provides a QR code, you can use your bank’s app to scan it and pay directly without leaving the app.
Scan to Pay (powered by Ukheshe)
This method uses Ukheshe’s payment infrastructure to allow QR code-based payments. You scan a generated QR code from your invoice and complete the transaction securely inside the Ukheshe-enabled interface.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
It’s more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
While paying your invoices with instant EFT via PayFast is quick and reliable,
debit order
remains the most recommended method for recurring services like hosting, domain renewals, and business email accounts.
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
Follow these simple steps to
update your payment method
:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3.
Select
Instant EFT, Masterpass & Zapper (Via Payfast)
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4.
Click
on
Save Changes
.
How to Pay Your Invoice with Instant EFT via Customer Zone
To make a quick instant payment using Instant EFT:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → My Invoices
.
3.
Locate
the
Unpaid
invoice and
click
on it to
open it
.
4. Make sure that
Instant EFT, Masterpass & Zapper (Via PayFast)
is selected under
Payment Method
and
Pay Now
.
6. You’ll be redirected to the secure
PayFast portal
.
7.
Select
your
preferred instant payment option
and
follow
the
3D Secure prompts
and your
bank’s process
, where applicable.
8. Once completed, you’ll receive instant payment confirmation via email.
Your account will be updated immediately, and your services will remain active.
TIP:
Always double-check that your account has sufficient funds to avoid failed transactions or
service suspensions
.
Security and Transparency
At 1-grid, we prioritise
trust, transparency, and data protection
. Every transaction made through PayFast’s Instant EFT is processed using
SSL encryption
, ensuring your credentials and payment details remain confidential.
Security Best Practices:
Always check that you’re on the
official PayFast domain
(
https://payment.payfast.io/eng/process/payment/
).
Never share your banking
passwords
,
OTPs
, or
personal details
.
Make payments only via your
1-grid Customer Zone
or PayFast portal.
Keep your device and browser up to date for maximum protection.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
You’ve contacted your bank to troubleshoot issues on their end.
Your Instant EFT payment doesn’t reflect within a few minutes.
You made a payment to the wrong invoice or service.
You experience duplicate or failed transactions.
You’re unsure if a transaction was successful.
You want help switching from PayFast to Debit Order after attempting to switch on your end.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
Screenshot of any error message you are receiving when trying to pay via PayFast’s portal.
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
Is Instant EFT via PayFast safe?
Yes, PayFast uses advanced encryption, verified banking channels, and 3D Secure protocols to protect your details.
How long does it take for my payment to reflect
Instant EFT payments are reflected immediately, so there’s no waiting period or proof of payment required.
Can I use any bank with Instant EFT?
Instant EFT supports all major South African banks, including Absa, FNB, Capitec, Nedbank, Standard Bank, and TymeBank.
What should I do if my payment fails?
Retry using the same method, ensure sufficient funds, or contact your bank. If the issue persists, contact
[email protected]
for help.
Can I switch to Debit Order later?
Absolutely. You can switch to Debit Order at any time from your Customer Zone; it’s our most convenient and reliable option.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Debit Orders at 1-grid
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
Suspended Status Remains After Payment
Understanding Invoicing at 1-grid
The 1-grid Android Application
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Paying your invoices with Instant EFT via PayFast for your services doesn’t have to cause worry with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: A Guide to Invoices at 1-grid, and When to Expect Yours
content:
At 1-grid, we understand how important it is for you to have clarity and consistency when it comes to billing, so we’re here to give you some insight into invoices at 1-grid and when to expect yours. You may have noticed that invoices arrive at different times of the month, and that’s completely normal!
This guide will explain why this happens, how 1-grid’s billing cycle works, and how you can easily access, review, and manage your invoices online.
Table of Contents
What is an Invoice?
Why Are Invoices Important?
How Does Invoicing Work at 1-grid?
Benefits of Invoicing in Advance
Updating Your Payment Method or Invoice Due Date at 1-grid
Updating Your Payment Method Before an Invoice Due Date
Important Note for Debit Orders Switches
Changing Your Invoice Due Date
Our Invoicing Process and Reminder Timelines
Standard Invoicing Process
Example Timeline
Daily Reminder Schedule
Where Do Invoices Go?
How Can I View My Invoice?
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What is an Invoice?
An invoice is a formal payment request sent by 1-grid for
products or services
you’ve purchased, like your hosting plan, domain renewals, or add-ons.
Each invoice may include, but is not limited to,
key information
such as:
Tax Invoice Number:
A unique reference for tracking
Payment Status:
UNPAID or PAID
Due Date:
The date the payment is due
Invoiced To:
Registered owner of the 1-grid Account
Pay To:
1-grid Business Details
Invoice Date:
The date the invoice was created
Payment Method:
Your preferred method of payment
Invoice Items:
The product or service billed (e.g., hosting, domain, SSL).
Description: Lists the product or service
Amount: Lists the total amount due for that product or service
Sub Total: Lists the total of all products or services before VAT
15.00% VAT: Indicates the total amount of VAT due
Credit: Indicates any credit amount available
Total: Indicates the total amount due, all inclusive of the above
*Transaction Date: Indicates the date a payment was made
*Gateway: Indicates the payment gateway used (e.g. PayFast)
*Transaction ID: Indicates the ID allocated to the transaction of the payment
*Amount:
Indicates the amount for the transaction
Balance:
Indicates a total amount still due on the invoice (after any transactions, etc.)
Latest Invoice Example
IMPORTANT:
*Indicates any previous transactions applied to the invoice, perhaps as a part payment, etc. Which gets deducted from the Total due. When establishing the amount due, please always check the Balance on the invoice to make sure you are paying the correct amount.
Why Are Invoices Important?
Invoices serve as
proof of purchase
and are essential for:
Tracking your
accounting and tax records
.
Keeping your
services active and payments up to date
.
Maintaining
business transparency
between you and 1-grid.
They also help ensure that you have a
clear record of every renewal, upgrade, or service purchase
for your account.
How Does Invoicing Work at 1-grid?
At 1-grid, each service has its own
billing cycle
based on the date it was activated or renewed. This means invoices for different products may be generated at different times of the month/year.
For example:
Your
domain
may renew annually in June.
Your
hosting plan
may renew monthly on the 15th.
Your
email service
may be on a separate schedule.
Because every product is billed independently, your invoices are sent
when each service reaches its renewal period
, and not all at once. This allows us to make sure that you’re billed the correct rate for the exact days for your product or service.
For Example:
If you purchased a
WordPress Hosting
on the 13th of February, an invoice will be generated for the initial purchase, and your following invoice will be sent to you on the 13th of March and so forth.
TIP:
For products or services with
Debit Order
as the payment method, these invoices are automatically due on the 1st of the month,
keeping in mind that it will typically be tracked to allow deduction on the first working day after this date, should it fall on the weekend
. This means, if you purchase a product or service on the 15th, you will be billed on that date at a pro-rate, and then all future invoices will be due on the 1st of the following month, and thereafter.
Benefits of Invoicing in Advance
1-grid sends invoices
in advance
(approximately
5 to
7 days before
the due date for products and services, and often up to
60 days before
renewal for domains) to ensure you have enough time to:
Review your billing details
and make changes if needed.
Update payment methods
(e.g., card details or direct EFT).
Prevent service interruption
by paying before the due date.
This proactive approach gives customers full control over their payments, renewals, and avoids last-minute billing stress, giving you one less thing to worry about.
Updating Your Payment Method or Invoice Due Date at 1-grid
At 1-grid, we make it easy to manage your payments and billing preferences so you can stay in full control of your account without unnecessary admin or delays.
Updating
Your
Payment Method
Before an Invoice Due Date
If you update your
payment method
after an invoice has been generated, but before the due date, you
don’t need to request a new invoice
.
Your billing details remain the same, and the updated payment method will automatically apply to the current invoice once it’s processed.
This means you can switch between Debit Order, PayFast, or EFT payments confidently, knowing your changes take effect seamlessly.
Important Note for
Debit Orders Switches
If you use
Debit Order
as your payment method, please ensure you update it
at least 7 days before the scheduled debit date (due date)
.
This ensures your new banking details are verified and processed in time for your next billing cycle, helping you avoid any payment delays or service interruptions.
Changing Your Invoice Due Date
If you’d like to change your
invoice due date
, our team will
pro-rate your billing
to cover the transition period around the change.
This adjustment ensures a smooth and accurate handover between billing cycles, without overpayment or disruption to your services.
IMPORTANT:
At 1-grid, we’re committed to giving you
flexibility, transparency, and control
over your payments. Whether you’re updating your debit order, switching to another payment method, or requesting a billing adjustment, our process is designed to keep your services active and your experience effortless.
Our Invoicing Process and Reminder Timelines
At 1-grid, we’re committed to helping you manage your account with confidence and ease with our invoicing. To keep your services active and uninterrupted, we follow a
consistent, transparent invoicing process
supported by automated reminders, giving you plenty of time to review, plan, and pay before any action is required.
Our invoicing and reminder system ensures you’re always informed about upcoming payments and due dates, reducing the risk of missed invoices or unexpected service interruptions.
To stay informed about our
Domain Renewal
timelines, please visit our quick guides:
Understanding Invoicing for Domain Renewals at 1-grid
Understanding Domain Auto-Renew Statuses and How to View Yours
Standard Invoicing Process
Here’s how our monthly billing cycle works:
Invoice Issued:
Your main invoices are automatically generated and sent
on the 24th or 25th of each month
.
First Reminder:
Sent
7 days
after the invoice is issued, reminding you that payment is due soon.
Overdue Reminder:
Sent
4 days
after the first reminder if payment hasn’t yet been received.
Final Notice & Suspension Warning:
If payment remains outstanding, a
final notice
is sent before your services are
temporarily suspended
in line with the reminder timeline below.
This process helps maintain uninterrupted access to your hosting, email, and domain services, while also ensuring fairness and consistency for all customers.
Example Timeline
Date
Time
Notification Type
24/09/2025
01:54
Customer Invoice –
Payment Ref 25297
30/09/2025
05:47
Reminder – Your invoice is due soon
04/10/2025
05:18
First Invoice Reminder –
Action required (invoice overdue)
15/10/2025
05:16
Final Notice – Invoice is overdue
16/10/2025
09:18
Service Suspension Notification
Daily Reminder Schedule
Our automated
reminder system runs every day at 5 a.m.
, including weekends and public holidays.
This ensures consistent communication and provides every customer with
timely updates
so they can take action before services are affected.
Why This Matters
By following a clear, predictable invoicing and reminder process, we help you:
Plan:
You’ll always know when to expect your next invoice.
Stay Informed:
Multiple reminders ensure you never miss a due date.
Avoid Interruptions:
Early notice with 1-grid invoices gives you time to act before any
suspension
.
Manage Services Easily:
Log in anytime to view or pay invoices directly via the
Customer Zone
.
Where Do Invoices Go?
All invoices are automatically:
Emailed
to the
primary contact email address
on your 1-grid account.
Available
anytime in your
Customer Zone
under
Billing →
My Invoices
.
If you can’t find 1-grid invoices in your Inbox, check your
Spam or Trash folder
, or log in to the Customer Zone for instant access.
TIP:
You can also
add a Sub-Account to your 1-grid account
as an alternate email address from the primary contact to receive billing information and invoices, such as when you need your Accounting or Finance Team to receive and attend to these on your behalf.
How Can I View My Invoice?
It matters to us that you spend less time worrying about when your next payment is due or struggling to find your invoice, so we’ve got you covered with
this guide
to make it easy for you to view or download your invoice directly from your
Customer Zone
.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team if:
You received
duplicate invoices
or
incorrect billing dates
.
Your payment hasn’t reflected after the standard processing time.
You want to
consolidate
your billing dates for convenience by switching to Debit Order.
FAQs
What happens if I miss a payment?
If payment isn’t made by the due date, your service may be temporarily suspended until the invoice is settled.
Can I change my billing date?
In some cases, yes. Contact our Billing Support Team to discuss available options.
Where can I download old invoices?
You can access all past invoices directly through your
Customer Zone → Billing → My Invoices
.
Additional Resources
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
How Do I Update My Payment Method?
Checking Your Billing Cycle via Your Customer Zone
Introducing the 1-grid Android Application
Payment FAQs
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding how 1-grid invoices work with us doesn’t have to feel complicated with this easy-to-understand guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
content:
With our easy-to-use payment methods, you can find the right method that works for you, so that you have peace of mind that your services stay active, your payments are secure, and your business never misses a beat.
This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about our easy-to-use payment methods, from
debit order
to
direct EFT
payments, the benefits of each, and a step-by-step guide on how to select the right one for you via your Customer Zone.
Table of Contents
Making Payments Simple and Secure
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods
Debit Order
Secure Credit Card via PayFast
SnapScan
Instant EFT via PayFast
Direct EFT
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
Security and Transparency
FAQs
Additional Resources
Making Payments Simple and Secure
At 1-grid, it’s our goal to make keeping your services online as easy and smooth as possible, so that secure ways to pay because you’re always in control, ensuring your online presence stays uninterrupted so you can focus on what truly matters: growing your brand, while we take care of the admin behind the scenes.
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods
Debit Order
Overview:
Automatically deduct monthly payments from your bank account on your due date.
Key Benefits:
Prevents service interruptions.
Saves time as no manual payments are required.
Ensures consistent payment history for better account management.
Reduces stress and late payment fees.
Preferred for:
Hosting, email, and domain renewals that require uninterrupted uptime.
Secure Credit Card via PayFast
Overview:
Pay using Visa or Mastercard via
PayFast’s
secure online gateway.
Key Benefits:
Payments are processed instantly.
Data encrypted with bank-level security.
Ideal for managing multiple hosting or domain renewals quickly.
Perfect for:
Customers who prefer paying manually but still want instant confirmation.
SnapScan
Overview:
Pay quickly and securely using your smartphone.
How it works:
Scan the
SnapScan
QR code on your invoice and follow the in-app steps.
Key Benefits:
Fast, convenient mobile payments.
No need for bank details or manual entries.
Secure transactions with trusted encryption.
Perfect for:
Quick payments on the go.
Instant EFT via PayFast
Overview:
Pay directly from your bank account using
PayFast’s
secure gateway.
Key Benefits:
Instant confirmation, which means no waiting for manual proof of payment verification.
Safe and secure through
3D Secure authentication
.
Available through major South African banks.
Perfect for:
Customers who prefer online transfers but want faster clearance.
Direct EFT
Overview:
Traditional bank transfer method.
Key Benefits:
Simple and accessible for all customers.
Flexible for those who prefer managing payments manually.
Notes:
Always include your
Customer Code
as your reference.
Email proof of payment to
[email protected]
to assist with payment allocation.
Payments may take
2 to 3 working days
to reflect and are
allocated to the oldest invoices first
.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
Paying your 1-grid services by
debit order
is more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
By automating your payments, you save time, reduce administrative effort, and gain peace of mind knowing that your hosting, email, and domains stay up and running without interruptions.
With debit orders, your payments are processed automatically on the due date, helping you maintain financial consistency and a clear record of all transactions. Over time, this reliability can even strengthen your credit standing by showing consistent, on-time payments.
No Missed Payments = Avoid Service Interruptions
Secure and Authorised
Saves Time and Reduces Stress
Improve Financial Management
Maintain a Clear Payment History
Build a Stronger Credit Profile
Avoid Late Fees and Penalties
Ideal for Businesses
By setting up or switching to debit order payments, with 1-grid’s secure, automated system, you’re always in control, ensuring your online presence stays uninterrupted so you can focus on what truly matters: growing your brand, while we take care of the admin behind the scenes.
How to Update Your Payment Method via the Customer Zone
Follow these simple steps to choose or
update your payment method
:
1. Log in to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3. Select
your preferred option
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4. Click on
Save Changes
.
Security and Transparency
We know financial information is sensitive, and your trust matters. That’s why all relevant transactions with 1-grid are
processed securely
through
PCI DSS-compliant payment gateways
, ensuring your data remains safe and encrypted.
FAQs
Can I use more than one payment method?
Yes, you can have a primary payment method (such as Debit Order) and use another option (like PayFast) for specific invoices.
How do I know if my payment went through?
You’ll receive a payment confirmation email once the transaction is successful. You can also verify it in your
Customer Zone → Billing → My Invoices
.
Can I switch from EFT to Debit Order?
Absolutely! You can update your payment method to
Debit Order
anytime through your
Customer Zone
.
Is it safe to pay online via PayFast or SnapScan?
Yes, both are trusted South African payment gateways that use advanced encryption and bank-level security.
What happens if my Debit Order fails?
If there are insufficient funds, you’ll receive an email notification. Simply top up your account and retry via another payment method to avoid service suspension. You may also be charged a
Debit Order
Failed Fee, so please keep this in mind.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Debit Orders at 1-grid
3D Secure Payments Blocked
Checking Your Billing Cycle via Your Customer Zone
Everything You Need to Know About Invoicing at 1-grid
Viewing Your Invoices via Your Customer Zone
Everything Your Need to Know About Reactivaing Suspended or Cancelled Services
Introducing the 1-grid Android Application
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Choosing and selecting the right easy-to-use payment method for your services doesn’t have to cause worry with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: How to Add Funds as Credit to Your 1-grid Account
content:
At
1-grid
, we know how important it is to keep your
hosting
,
email
, and
domain
services active without disruption. That’s why we offer the option to
add funds as credit to your account
, making it a simple, secure, and proactive way to stay on top of your payments.
By adding funds to your 1-grid account, you can
automatically pay
invoices
when they’re due, giving you one less thing to worry about! This also means no manual payments, no delays, and no risk of service suspension. It’s perfect for maintaining full control over your billing while enjoying the convenience of automatic renewals and faster processing.
This guide will show you how to add funds as credit directly through your
Customer Zone
, using
secure payment options
.
Table of Contents
Why Add Funds as Credit to Your 1-grid Account?
Benefits of Adding Funds as Credit
Available Payment Options to Add Funds to Your 1-grid Account
Debit Order
Secure Credit Card via PayFast
SnapScan
Instant EFT (via PayFast)
Direct EFT
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
How to Add Credit to Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Checking Your Credit Balance on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Paying Invoices with Credit on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
Need Additional Support?
Why Add Funds as Credit to Your 1-grid Account?
Adding funds to your account as credit means that funds have been preloaded onto your 1-grid account so that this can be used to pay any future invoices for domains, hosting, and other services, giving you peace of mind while other things need your attention. You can even
use your credit to pay
for any
new
or
future orders
as your business expands, making planning for the future more exciting!
Benefits of Adding Funds as Credit
Instant Payments
means no waiting period as future invoices are settled immediately.
Automatic Renewal Protection
prevents downtime or missed renewals.
Budget-friendly
means you can manage expenses proactively by pre-funding your account.
Convenient
means avoiding repeated manual payments for every invoice.
Flexible
means using your credit for any 1-grid service whenever needed.
Secure
means peace of mind as payments are handled in your 1-grid Customer Zone.
Available Payment Options to Add Funds to Your 1-grid Account
Debit Order
The easiest and most reliable way to keep your services active is debit order as these payments are automated each month, ensuring your hosting, domains, and emails are never interrupted. Once set up, your invoices are settled automatically, saving you time and preventing missed payments.
Best if:
You want a hands-off, stress-free payment option.
Secure Credit Card via PayFast
PayFast lets you make instant, secure payments using your credit or cheque card. Your details are encrypted and never shared, giving you peace of mind with every transaction. You’ll also get real-time payment confirmation, ensuring your services stay active immediately after payment.
Best if:
You prefer flexibility and instant processing.
SnapScan
With SnapScan, you can pay invoices instantly by scanning a QR code from your smartphone, which means no card or bank login is needed. It’s fast, secure, and convenient for on-the-go payments, ensuring your services stay uninterrupted with just one scan.
Best if:
You’re a mobile user who values quick and easy payments.
Instant EFT (via PayFast)
Instant EFT allows you to make secure online payments directly from your bank account without waiting for traditional EFT clearance. Funds reflect immediately, and your 1-grid services are renewed or activated right away.
Best if:
You want instant confirmation without using a card.
Direct EFT
Direct EFT gives you full control of your payments by transferring funds directly from your bank to 1-grid’s account. While payments may take
2 to 3 working days
to reflect, this method suits those who prefer manual transactions and managing payments independently.
Best if:
You prefer traditional banking and full control over payment timing.
IMPORTANT:
To avoid
service interruptions
, always add funds at least
3 days before
any future due invoice date and use your
Customer Code
as your payment reference when making
Direct EFT
payments so that our Team can allocate this correctly.
Why Debit Order Is the Recommended and Preferred Option
Adding funds as credit to your account for your 1-grid services by
debit order
is more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active while simplifying your billing, building
trust
,
reliability
, and
financial confidence
.
How to Add Credit to Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Before attempting to add funds, it is important to make sure that you have an active order on your account. You will receive an alert before adding funds to let you know!
If you have at least one active order, you can proceed to add funds as credit to your account by following these steps:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Billing → Add Funds
.
3. Enter the
desired amount
in
Amount to Add
between R10.00 and R30,000.00.
4. Choose your preferred
Payment Method
(Debit Order, EFT, Credit Card, etc.).
5. Click on
Add Funds
and then follow any payment prompts that follow, according to the Payment Method you selected.
6. Confirm payment and wait for it to reflect (typically immediate for Card/PayFast or 2 to 3 working days for Direct EFT).
TIP:
Funds added to your account are automatically available to pay future invoices.
Checking Your Credit Balance on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Once you have added funds to your account, it’s easy to keep an eye on this to stay ahead of upcoming invoices for your domains, hosting, and other services. When time is of the essence, we’ve made it simple to stay ahead of the game with our
easy-to-follow guide
.
Paying Invoices with Credit on Your 1-grid Account (via Customer Zone)
Making payment on any of your invoices using your existing or new credit balance is just as easy! View our
easy-to-follow guide
on how to do this and never miss a payment again!
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
The credit doesn’t reflect after a successful payment.
An incorrect amount was applied or deducted.
You’re struggling to add funds to your account via your Customer Zone.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code), where applicable.
Details of the issue you are experiencing.
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
How long does it take for credit to reflect?
Payments via Debit Order, PayFast, or Credit Card are instant. Manual EFT payments can take 2 to 3 working days to reflect.
Additional Resources
Everything You Need to Know About Debit Orders at 1-grid
Our Easy-to-Use Payment Methods at 1-grid
How Do I Update My Payment Method?
Everything You Need to Know About Invoicing at 1-grid
Payment FAQs
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Adding funds as credit in your 1-grid account doesn’t have to feel like a challenge with this easy-to-reference guide. Stuck? Contact our Billing Team for clarity and guidance (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Understanding Why Your Support Ticket Has Been Closed
content:
At 1-grid, we want to resolve your queries as quickly and smoothly as possible. This means that sometimes tickets close automatically if we don’t hear back from you. That’s why it’s important to understand why your Support Ticket closed and how the process works.
Table of Contents
How the Ticket Lifecycle Works
What “Closed” Really Means
How to Reopen a Ticket
FAQs
Additional Resources
How the Ticket Lifecycle Works
1.
When you log a ticket
with our Support Team, it will reflect on our system as
Open
. (You can
view your logged Support Tickets in your 1-grid Customer Zone
.
)
2. You will then
receive an automated notification
letting you know we have received your request, and you will be provided with a ticket number for your reference.
3.
When we respond to your ticket
with a proposed solution that requires your confirmation or testing, or when we request more information to assist you further, we mark the ticket as
Waiting on Customer Feedback
.
4.
If we don’t hear back from you within 48 hours
, we’ll send you a
reminder email
.
5.
If you don’t respond within 24 hours, the system automatically marks the ticket as
Closed
.
What “Closed” Really Means
A closed ticket doesn’t mean we can’t help anymore. It simply means:
We didn’t hear back in time to keep the ticket open.
We assume your issue may be resolved.
You can still follow up anytime.
How to Reopen a Ticket
1.
Log in
to your
Customer Zone
.
2. Go to
Support
→
Tickets
.
3. Go to
This ticket has been Closed
, then
Submit a Reply
.
4. Or go to
Open Ticket
, then
Submit a New Ticket
.
IMPORTANT:
If you are struggling to reopen your previous ticket, you can email the Support Team directly, which will automatically generate a new ticket number, and you will receive this via email, as normal.
Your new ticket will then be
viewable through your Customer Zone
.
FAQs
What if I didn’t see the reminder email?
You can still create a new one anytime through your Customer Zone or by emailing the relevant Team directly.
For Billing Queries:
[email protected]
For Standard Hosting Support Queries:
[email protected]
Can I provide feedback after the ticket is closed?
Yes, your feedback response will create a new ticket for our Support Team and will automatically be linked to the previous ticket for ease of reference.
It is important to note that this will, however, create a new ticket number, so when you follow up with our Support Team by call or chat, make sure to provide them with both ticket numbers.
This makes it easier for them to find both and gain a clearer understanding of the issue and the progress.
Why do tickets close automatically?
It helps us keep the support queue efficient and focused on active queries, but you’re never locked out of getting help.
Additional Resources
Viewing Your Logged Support Tickets in Your 1-grid Customer Zone
How to Verify Your 1-grid Account for Support
How to Keep Your 1-grid Account Details Up to Date
Adding a Sub-Account or Technical Contact to Your 1-grid Account
Deleting a Sub-Account or Technical Contact from Your 1-grid Account
Preventing Unauthorised Customers from Accessing 1-grid Support
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Understanding why your ticket has been closed when you feel your query has not been addressed doesn’t have to be confusing, so this easy-to-understand guide makes it easy. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for help (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: How to Verify Your 1-grid Account for Support
content:
When contacting 1-grid for support, we may need to verify that you’re authorised to make changes to the account. This is for your protection and ours, so here’s a quick guide on how to verify your 1-grid account for Support.
Table of Contents
What Is Verification?
Why We Ask for Verification
How to Verify Your Account
What If You Don’t Have Some Items
What Happens After Verification
FAQs
Additional Resources
What Is Verification?
Verification is a process to confirm you are either the account owner or trusted to manage the account (e.g. sub-account, technical contact, business partner, etc.).
Why We Ask for Verification
Prevent unauthorised changes
(e.g. updating domain registration, changing passwords, etc.)
Protection against identity theft, fraud, or misuse
Ensures
only the correct people
can request support
How to Verify Your Account
Tickets
Submit your request from a registered email address listed on your 1-grid account.
This includes email addresses registered under any
Sub-Account or Technical Contact added
to your account.
Calls and Chats
Provide
three (3)
of the following items correctly:
Verification Item
What to Provide
Security Question
The answer you provided when signing up
Business VAT Number
If your account is for a business, the VAT number you provided when signing up
ID / Passport Number
The government ID number you used when creating the account
Physical Address
The address you used when signing up
Latest 3 Invoice Numbers
These are sent to the registered email on file
Listed Email Address
The email that receives notifications from 1-grid
Customer Code
Found on your invoices or in the Customer Zone dashboard
What If You Don’t Have Some Items
If you can’t provide some of the items, here are your options:
Use alternate items from the list
Verify via email by sending a request for support from a listed email address
What Happens After Verification
Once you’re verified, Support can proceed with handling your request (whether that’s a password reset, domain change, or account update).
FAQs
Q: Why do I need to provide verification details just to get support?
Verification protects your account and personal data. It ensures that only the account owner or
authorised contacts
can request changes or access sensitive information.
Q: I don’t remember my security question answer. What can I do?
You can use other verification items such as your ID number, invoice numbers, or registered email. If you can’t provide three valid items, our Support Team can guide you on the next steps.
Q: I no longer have access to the registered email address. How can I update it?
For security, we’ll need additional verification. Once your identity is confirmed, our team can help you update your email address.
How to Update My Account Details
Q: What if the account was registered by someone else, and I don’t have their details?
Only the registered account owner or an
authorised contact
can update details or request access. If you’re managing the account on their behalf, please ask the account owner to
add you as a Sub-Account or Technical Contact
, or they can contact us, and we will assist them to do so.
Q: I’ve provided details, but support still says I can’t be verified. Why?
If the details don’t match our records, we can’t complete verification. Please double-check the information against your invoices or
Customer Zone
. If you still need help, we’ll advise on what documentation can be submitted to update your account.
Q: Can I bypass verification if my issue is urgent?
For security reasons, we cannot bypass verification under any circumstances. This is to ensure your account remains safe. Once verified, we’ll assist you immediately.
Additional Resources
Protection of Personal Information Act (POPI Act) – POPIA
How To Keep Your 1-grid Account Details Up to Date
Preventing Unauthorised Customers from Accessing 1-grid Support
Adding a Sub-Account or Technical Contact to Your 1-grid Account
Deleting a Sub-Account or Technical Contact from Your 1-grid Account
Customer Zone: How to Update My Profile Security Question
How to Update My Security Question
Steps to Assist with Logging In to Your Customer Zone
Recovering a Lost 1-grid Customer Zone Password
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Verifying your account to get support isn’t meant to feel like a challenge, so this easy-to-use guide makes it easy. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for help (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Deleting a Sub-Account or Technical Contact from Your 1-grid Account
content:
With 1-grid, you can remove access/delete a user under a sub-account and delete a technical/support contact to remove them from different levels of access to your account and support on your behalf. Deleting a Sub-Account or Technical Contact from your 1-grid account keeps your account secure while giving the right people access to logins, support, account functions, technical functions and management.
Table of Contents
Different Ways to Remove Access to Your Account While Keeping It Secure
Why Remove/Delete Them?
Security and Authorisation
How to Delete a Sub-Account (User) or Technical/Support Contact
How to Remove Access for a User from a Sub-Account
Managing Existing Sub-Accounts or Technical/Support Contacts
FAQs
Additional Resources
Different Ways to Remove Access to Your Account While Keeping It Secure
Technical/Support Contacts
Deleting these contacts no longer authorises them to contact support on your behalf.
Sub-Accounts (Removing Access for a User)
This is different from technical/support contacts. Deleting a Sub-Account requires removing access for a user to no longer allow someone direct access to your Customer Zone via their own logins:
If they already have a 1-grid user account, they can no longer log in to your account using their existing credentials.
Important: They will no longer be able to do password resets actively, update account details, perform actions on the account, etc., as they will no longer form part of “User Management” on your Customer Zone.
Deleting a Sub-Account or Technical Contact from your 1-grid account means that neither will
no longer allow others to interact with 1-grid support (tickets, chat, calls)
and all previous permissions you assigned with no longer be valid.
Why Remove/Delete Them?
No longer requires delegation of tasks
Disallow admin, finance or technical staff to contact support directly
Prevent smooth access to support channels
Security and Authorisation
For your protection, only
authorised contacts
’ listed email addresses on your account will receive support via tickets, chat, or calls.
How to Delete a Sub-Account (User) or Technical/Support Contact
1.
Log in
to your
Customer Zone
2.
Go to
Contacts
3.
Select
the
applicable Contact
from the list
4.
Click
on
Delete Contact
IMPORTANT:
When deleting a Sub-Account, you need to navigate to “User Management” to remove their direct access from your Customer Zone. If this is not implemented, they will still be able to access your Customer Zone either using your own login details or direct access using theirs, depending on how they were set up at the time.
How to Remove Access for a User from a Sub-Account
1. Log in to your
Customer Zone
2. Go to
Contacts → +New Contact..
.
3.
Select User Management
4.
Click
to
Remove Access
next to the applicable user email address
Managing Existing Sub-Accounts or Technical/Support Contacts
Edit permissions/access
anytime in Customer Zone.
Add a new contact or sub-account
if someone new needs access or permissions.
Best practice: Review contacts regularly to ensure they’re
up to date
.
FAQs
Q:
Can I remove more than one sub-account or technical/support contact at a time?
No, you can only remove one sub-account or technical/support contact at a time; however, you can repeat all the steps until you have removed all the required contacts.
Q:
What happens if my staff changes?
Remove the old contact/sub-account and add the new person to keep support access and permissions current.
Additional Resources
How to Verify Your 1-grid Account for Support
Customer Zone: How to Update My Profile Security Question
How to Update My Security Question
Steps to Assist with Logging In to Your Customer Zone
Recovering a Lost 1-grid Customer Zone Password
Preventing Unauthorised Customers from Accessing 1-grid Support
Adding a Sub-Account or Technical Contact to Your 1-grid Account
How To Keep Your 1-grid Account Details Up to Date
Need Additional Support?
We’re Here to
Help
:
Deleting a Sub-Account or Technical Contact from your 1-grid account does not have to feel like a challenge because this easy-to-use guide makes it easy. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for help (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Viewing Your Logged Support Tickets in Your 1-grid Customer Zone
content:
Viewing your logged support tickets and their statuses, like open, closed, and in between, in your 1-grid Customer Zone makes it easy to stay up to date with your queries and their progress with our Support Team. Here’s how to find them, understand their status, and what to do if you need more assistance.
Table of Contents
Where to View Your Support Tickets in Your Customer Zone
Different Ticket Statuses, and What They Mean
FAQs
Additional Resources
Where to View Your Support Tickets in Your Customer Zone
1.
Log in
to your
Customer Zone
2.
Enter
your
Email Address
and
Password
3.
Navigate
to
Support
→
Tickets
Different Ticket Statuses, and What They Mean
You will see all current ticket statuses that could apply to any of your Support Tickets under the
View
section, once you have navigated via
Support
to the
Tickets
dashboard.
Being Processed
We indicate the ticket as received, and it is either in “Open” or “Pending” status.
This ticket has been Resolved
The ticket shows the issue is resolved and no further action is required, but you can reopen it within 8 hours without creating a new one.
Waiting – 3
rd
Party Escalation Feedback
This is when the issue has been escalated to a 3
rd
party vendor externally for further assistance or more information before we can offer a resolution.
Waiting – Internal Investigation Feedback
We escalate the issue to another internal department for further assistance or action toward a resolution.
Pending Callback
We mark the ticket for a call follow-up when we cannot resolve it immediately via the ticket and need a verbal discussion to move toward resolution.
This ticket has been Closed
We show this status when we have not received any response for further action or assistance, so we assume the issue is resolved. It does not reopen the ticket but instead creates a new one linked to the previous ticket and assigns you a new ticket number.
Waiting on Customer Feedback
This is when the ticket is waiting for further feedback from you as the customer before we can assist further or resolve the query in full.
FAQs
Q: Can I provide feedback after the ticket is closed?
Yes, your feedback response creates a new ticket for our Support Team and automatically links it to the previous ticket for easy reference.
It is important to note that this will, however, create a new ticket number, so when you follow up with our Support Team by call or chat, make sure to provide them with both ticket numbers.
This makes it easier for them to find both and gain a clearer understanding of the issue and the progress.
Q: Why do tickets close automatically?
It keeps the support queue efficient and focused on active queries, but we never lock you out of getting help.
Additional Resources
Why Is My Ticket Closed?
Customer Zone: How to log a new Support Ticket?
Introduction to the 1-grid Android Application
1-grid APP: How do I view and log Support Tickets?

We’re Here to
Help
:
Navigating to and viewing your logged Support Tickets in your 1-grid Customer Zone doesn’t have to feel like a maze, so this easy-to-understand guide makes it easy. Stuck? Contact our Support Team for help (
https://1grid.co.za/contact-us/
). We’re ready to see how we can help!

title: Everything You Need to Know About Debit Orders at 1-grid
content:
Managing your
hosting
and
domain
payments shouldn’t be stressful or time-consuming. With debit orders, you can automate your billing, so your services stay active, your payments are secure, and your business never misses a beat.
This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about debit orders with 1-grid, the benefits of paying via debit order, and a step-by-step guide on how to set this up via your
Customer Zone
.
Table of Contents
What is a Debit Order?
Benefits of Payment via Debit Order
No Missed Payments = Avoid Service Interruptions
Secure and Authorised
Saves Time and Reduces Stress
Improves Financial Management
Maintain a Clear Payment History
Build a Stronger Credit Profile
Avoid Late Fees and Penalties
Ideal for Businesses
How to Set Up or Update Your Debit Orders via Customer Zone
What Happens After You Set Up Your Debit Order
Security and Transparency
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
FAQs
Additional Resources
What is a Debit Order?
A
debit order
is an authorised agreement that allows 1-grid to automatically collect your payments directly from your bank account on your billing date.
It’s a safe and secure payment method, widely used across South Africa, that ensures your hosting, domain, or email services remain active without manual intervention.
TIP:
You stay focused on your business, while your payments take care of themselves.
Benefits of Payment via Debit Order
Paying for your 1-grid services by
debit order
is more than just convenient; it’s a smart, secure, and stress-free way to keep your services active. By automating your payments, you save time, reduce administrative effort, and gain peace of mind knowing that your hosting, email, and domains stay up and running without interruptions.
With debit orders, your payments are processed automatically on the due date, helping you maintain financial consistency and a clear record of all transactions. Over time, this reliability can even strengthen your credit standing by showing consistent, on-time payments.
No Missed Payments = Avoid Service Interruptions
Your hosting, email, and domain renew automatically without worry. Because payments happen automatically, your services stay active without the risk of suspension or disconnection due to missed payments.
Secure and Authorised
All debit orders are processed safely via trusted South African payment channels.
Saves Time and Reduces Stress
Instead of remembering billing dates or logging in to pay manually, your debit order runs in the background, freeing up your time to focus on your business or personal projects.
Improves Financial Management
A set, automated payment schedule helps you plan your budget more effectively. You’ll always know when payments are due and can easily track your hosting expenses.
Maintain a Clear Payment History
Every debit order transaction is recorded, giving you a simple, transparent way to view your complete payment history whenever needed. This makes tracking and auditing effortless.
Build a Stronger Credit Profile
Consistent, on-time debit order payments can positively impact your credit score over time, helping you establish reliability with financial institutions.
Avoid Late Fees and Penalties
When your account has sufficient funds, debit orders eliminate the risk of late payments and unnecessary bank charges, keeping your finances predictable and penalty-free.
Ideal for Businesses
Keeps company accounts consistent and reduces administrative tasks.
TIP:
Setting up or switching to debit order payments not only simplifies your billing; it builds
trust, reliability, and financial confidence
. With 1-grid’s secure, automated system, you’re always in control, ensuring your online presence stays uninterrupted so you can focus on what truly matters: growing your brand, while we take care of the admin behind the scenes.
How to Set Up or Update Your Debit Orders via Customer Zone
Follow these simple steps to add or update your debit order details:
1.
Log in
to your
1-grid Customer Zone
.
2.
Click
on
Update
in the
Your Info
section.
3.
Select
Debit Order
under
Payment Method
in your
My Details
section.
4.
Click
on
Save Changes
.
5.
Navigate to
the
Billing
section.
6.
Select
My Debit Details
.
7.
Enter
your
Bank name
,
Account holder
, and
Account number
, etc.
8.
Read through
the
Mandate
thoroughly, and tick “
Set debit order as default payment method
” to confirm your authorisation.
9.
Click Submit Bank Details
.
TIP:
Always double-check
that your account has sufficient funds before your debit date (usually the 1
st
of a month) to avoid failed transactions or
service suspensions
.
IMPORTANT:
If, for any reason, your services have been suspended and our Billing Team is required to reinstate your Debit Order upon reactivation of your services, they will select “
I, a 1-grid Sales Agent, accept these debit order mandate terms and conditions on behalf of the customer
” to reinstate the mandate on your behalf.
What Happens After You Set Up Your Debit Order
Once you’ve set up or updated your debit order:
Your future
invoices
will be automatically settled on your due date.
You can view and manage your
payment method
:
In
Customer Zone → Your Info → Update → My Details → Payment Method
In
Customer Zone → Billing → My Debit Details
In
Customer Zone → Billing → My Invoices
IMPORTANT:
If your debit order fails, you’ll be notified immediately via email so you can retry or update your details. You will be
charged a
R50.00 debit order failed fee
if this happens, so it is important to plan for fund availability.
Security and Transparency
We know financial information is sensitive, and your trust matters. That’s why all debit order transactions with 1-grid are
processed securely
through
PCI DSS-compliant payment gateways
, ensuring your data remains safe and encrypted.
When to Contact 1-grid Billing Support
Contact our Team:
If your services have been
suspended
.
You’ve made payment of the outstanding amounts (allow 2-3 business days for
direct EFT
payments to reflect).
Need your services
reactivated
via Debit Order.
Include the following:
Proof of payment, clearly reflecting the date, account number, amount, and reference number (Customer Code).
Written or verbal consent to reinstate the Debit Order mandate on your behalf.
TIP:
This helps 1-grid Billing Support get you back online faster.
FAQs
What if my bank details change?
You can log into your Customer Zone at least 7 days before your due date to make changes to your details.
What if my debit order fails?
If your debit fails due to insufficient funds, you’ll receive an email alert. You can retry payment manually or wait for the next debit run. It is important to remember that you will be charged a R50.00 debit order failed fee if this happens.
Can I cancel my debit order?
Yes, you can cancel anytime through your Customer Zone or by contacting
1-grid Billing Support
. Be sure to select an alternative payment method to keep your payments up to date.
Is my banking information safe?
Absolutely, all payment details are securely encrypted and handled via
trusted South African banking channels
.
Can I switch from EFT or credit card payment to debit order?
Yes, you can easily switch payment methods, at least 7 days before your due date, in your Customer Zone under
Your Info → Update → My Details → Payment Method
.

Viewing Your Invoices via Your Customer Zone
Where to View and Download Your Latest Statement
Everything You Need to Know About Invoicing at 1-grid
Everything You Need to Know About Reactivating Suspended or Cancelled Services
